Professor View 01 / 38
Travel & Social English -- 50 Aulas

Marlene Landucci

De "eu engulo as palavras" a viajante que conversa com confiança na Itália e na Turquia

A1+ São Paulo / Americana, SP Veterinária / Gestora de Imóveis 60 min / 3x semana / Online
Progresso Geral0%
Velocidade:
Marlene Landucci
54 anos
Veterinária / Gestora de Imóveis
São Paulo (Vila Nova Conceição) e Americana, SP
A1+ (Iniciante)
Travel English + Social English
50 aulas de 60 min, 3x/semana
Online (Zoom)

Jornada de Transformação

De: Veterinária que fala italiano, engole as palavras em inglês e depende de outros para se comunicar em viagens

Para: Viajante independente que conversa com confiança na Itália e na Turquia em 50 aulas

Promessa do Programa

"De 'eu engulo as palavras' a viajante que conversa com confiança na Itália e na Turquia"

Forças

  • Fala italiano fluente, facilitando cognação com inglês
  • Motivação forte: viagens planejadas para Itália e Turquia
  • Mulher independente e determinada, acostumada a gerenciar
  • Experiência de vida ampla (54 anos, duas cidades, múltiplas carreiras)
  • Gosta de tênis e atividades que exigem disciplina

Pontos de Melhoria

  • Nível A1+: vocabulário limitado para situações reais
  • "Engole as palavras": pronúncia precisa de trabalho intensivo
  • Insegurança para falar em público em inglês
  • Estruturas gramaticais básicas ainda instáveis
  • Pouca exposição ao inglês no dia a dia

Currículo -- 50 Aulas

#TemaFoco LinguísticoAtividade PrincipalHomework
Block 1: Foundations (1-5)
1Who Is Marlene?to be, subject pronounsDiagnostic + Survival CardRecord 60-90s self-intro
2My World in WordsPresent Simple affirmative, adverbs of frequencyDaily routine timelineWrite 5 routine sentences
3Numbers That MatterNumbers 1-100, time expressionsAirport numbers role-playRecord daily activity times
4Describing People and PlacesAdjectives, to be + adjectiveDescribe cities with photosDescribe your city in 5 sentences
5Block 1 ReviewReview: to be, present simple, adjectivesSpeed dating simulationCreate travel card
Block 2: Getting Ready to Travel (6-10)
6At the AirportCould I...?, I would like...Airport check-in simulationWatch airport movie scenes
7On the PlaneCan I have...?, Could you...?In-flight simulationRecord 3 plane requests
8Immigration and CustomsQuestions with do/doesImmigration interview role-playPractice 5 immigration questions
9Baggage and Ground TransportPossessives, Where is...?Lost luggage + taxi role-playWrite luggage complaint
10Block 2 ReviewReview: requests, airport vocabFull airport simulationNarrate full airport journey
Block 3: Arriving (11-15)
11Hotel Check-inI have a reservation, present continuousHotel check-in role-playWatch hotel scenes
12Getting AroundPrepositions, imperatives for directionsMap navigationWrite directions to 3 landmarks
13Money and ShoppingHow much is...?, payment vocabShopping simulationRecord buying 3 items
14At the RestaurantCould I have...?, food vocabRestaurant role-playRecord ordering dinner
15Block 3 ReviewReview: hotel, transport, foodDay-in-the-life simulationDay abroad diary
Block 4: Exploring (16-20)
16SightseeingI want to visit..., How do I get to...?Plan sightseeing dayResearch 3 Rome attractions
17Shopping for Stylethis/that/these/those, colors, sizesClothing store role-playDescribe 3 outfits
18When Things Go WrongI need help, body partsPharmacy role-playCreate emergency card
19Making PlansLet us..., Why don't we...?Plan evening outWrite invitation message
20Block 4 ReviewReview: sightseeing, shopping, emergenciesCity exploration simulation2-min tour guide recording
Block 5: Social Connections (21-25)
21The Art of Small TalkLike + -ing, present simple habitsHotel breakfast small talkRecord 5 conversation starters
22Telling Your StorySimple Past regular, was/wereTell last trip storyWrite best travel memory
23Dining with FriendsWould you like...?, recommendationsGroup dinner role-playRecommend Brazilian restaurant
24Staying ConnectedTech vocabulary, phone callsTech help + phone role-playSend 3 text messages in English
25Block 5 ReviewReview: small talk, past, socialCocktail party simulation2-min story recording
Block 6: Italy Prep (26-30)
26Italian AdventuresComparatives: bigger than, more beautifulCompare Italy and BrazilList 10 cognates
27Italian DiningI would like to try..., dietary needsItalian restaurant role-playWatch food documentary
28Navigating Italian CitiesDirection vocab, Which platform...?Navigate Rome with mapPlan route to Colosseum
29Italian MarketsNegotiation phrases, numbers reviewFlorence market role-playWrite souvenir shopping list
30Block 6 ReviewReview: Italy vocabularyFull Italy simulation3-min Italy plan recording
Block 7: Turkey Prep (31-35)
31Turkish CultureCultural phrases, polite expressionsGreetings role-playResearch 5 Turkish customs
32Turkish FoodFood adjectives, ordering at lokantaIstanbul restaurant role-playRecord ordering Turkish food
33The Grand BazaarDiscount phrases, negotiationBazaar negotiation role-playWrite carpet-buying dialogue
34Historical WondersSuperlatives, It was built in...Describe Hagia SophiaDescribe 3 Turkish landmarks
35Block 7 ReviewReview: Turkey vocabularyFull Istanbul simulation3-min Turkey plan recording
Block 8: Problem Solving (36-40)
36Lost and FoundPast simple irregular verbsPolice report role-playWrite lost item report
37Feeling UnwellSymptoms, body partsDoctor visit simulationMedical vocabulary flashcards
38Renting and BookingI would like to rent..., availabilityCar rental + tour bookingWrite tour booking
39Complaints and SolutionsI am not satisfied..., formal languageHotel complaint role-playWrite formal complaint email
40Block 8 ReviewReview: problems, health, bookingsMulti-scenario problem solvingRecord solving 3 problems
Block 9: Confident Communication (41-45)
41Sharing OpinionsI think..., In my opinion..., becauseOpinion exchangeRecord opinions on 3 countries
42Talking About HomeIn Brazil we..., Have you ever...?Cultural ambassador role-playWrite Brazil guide (10 sentences)
43Dreams and PlansGoing to, willPlan next tripCreate vision board with English
44Cultural ExchangeWhile in Brazil..., in Italy...Cultural comparison discussionCompare 3 aspects of countries
45Block 9 ReviewReview: opinions, future, culturePanel discussion simulation3-min speech recording
Block 10: Graduation (46-50)
46Travel Simulation Part 1All Blocks 1-5 vocabularyAirport to hotel simulationPrepare for assessment
47Travel Simulation Part 2All Blocks 6-8 vocabularyFull day abroad simulationSelf-evaluate 10 scenarios
48Social SimulationAll social vocabularyCocktail party + dinnerRecord 5 social situations
49Final AssessmentAll structuresSpeaking portfolio presentationPrepare graduation speech
50Graduation DayCelebrationVideo review of progressContinuation plan

Bem-vinda, Marlene!

Onboarding

Este é o seu material de apoio. Antes de cada aula, faça os exercícios abaixo para se preparar. Se precisar de ajuda durante a aula, use estas frases de emergência:

Frases de Emergência

1 "Could you repeat that, please?" "Poderia repetir, por favor?"
2 "I do not understand." "Eu não entendo."
3 "How do you say this in English?" "Como se diz isso em inglês?"
4 "Could you speak slowly, please?" "Poderia falar devagar, por favor?"
5 "Nice to meet you." "Prazer em conhecer você."
Aula 01 -- Pre-class

Who Is Marlene?

Diagnostic Session and Personal Introduction Baseline
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary

Vocabulary
Introduce·apresentar
"Let me introduce myself. I am Marlene."
Travel·viajar
"I love to travel to Italy and Turkey."
Property·imóvel
"I manage properties in São Paulo."
Manage·gerenciar
"She manages her own business."
Hobby·passatempo
"Tennis is my favorite hobby."
Nationality·nacionalidade
"My nationality is Brazilian."
Currently·atualmente
"I currently live in São Paulo."
Independent·independente
"I want to be an independent traveler."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice
Introduce
Travel
Property
Manage
Hobby
Nationality
Currently
Independent

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Leia o texto abaixo. As palavras em negrito são o vocabulário desta aula. Preste atenção nas formas do verbo to be (am, is, are).

My name is Marlene. I am from Brazil. I am 54 years old. My nationality is Brazilian. I currently live in São Paulo, in a neighborhood called Vila Nova Conceição. I also have a house in Americana.

I am a veterinarian by training, but now I manage properties in São Paulo. It is my own business. My favorite hobby is tennis. I love to travel! I want to be an independent traveler. Italy and Turkey are my dream destinations.

Let me introduce myself: I am Marlene Landucci, and I am ready to learn English!

1. What does Marlene manage? (O que a Marlene gerencia?)
A A veterinary clinic
B Properties in São Paulo
C A tennis club
2. What is Marlene's hobby? (Qual é o passatempo da Marlene?)
A Swimming
B Reading
C Tennis
3. Which verb form completes: "I ___ from Brazil"? (Qual forma completa?)
A am
B is
C are

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

Present Simple: Verb "to be" (Verbo "ser/estar")

Subject (Sujeito)To BeExample (Exemplo)
I (eu)amI am from Brazil.
You (você)areYou are a traveler.
He/She/It (ele/ela)isShe is a teacher.
We (nós)areWe are travelers.
They (eles/elas)areThey are at the airport.

Erro comum / Common mistake:

I is from Brazil. ✗

I am from Brazil. ✓

Dica (Tip):

I = am (sempre!) / He, She, It = is / You, We, They = are

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice
"I from Brazil. I Brazilian."
"She a property manager in São Paulo."
"We planning a trip to Italy."
"He from Turkey. He Turkish."
"They independent travelers."
"I currently learning English."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Organize a self-introduction in the correct order (Organize uma autoapresentação na ordem correta):

? "I manage properties in São Paulo."
? "Hi, my name is Marlene."
? "Nice to meet you!"
? "I am from Brazil."
? "I love to travel!"

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking
Hi, my name is Marlene. I am from Brazil.
I manage properties in São Paulo and Americana.
I love to travel. I am planning a trip to Italy.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
You meet someone at the airport gate. They say "Hi! Are you traveling to Italy?" What do you answer? (Alguém diz "Oi! Você está viajando para a Itália?" O que você responde?)
A "I is travel Italy."
B "Yes! I am Marlene. I am from Brazil."
C "Italy are my country."
Someone asks "What do you do?" How do you introduce your work? (Alguém pergunta "O que você faz?" Como você apresenta seu trabalho?)
A "I is property manager."
B "Me manage house."
C "I manage properties in São Paulo."

Stage 5: Think and Speak

Reflection
Imagine you are at the airport in Rome. You meet someone at the gate. Introduce yourself: say your name, where you are from, what you do, and what your hobby is. (Imagine que você está no aeroporto de Roma. Apresente-se: diga seu nome, de onde você é, o que você faz e qual é seu passatempo.)

Sugestão: "Hi, my name is Marlene. I am from Brazil. I manage properties in São Paulo. My favorite hobby is tennis."

Survival Card -- Aula 1

1 "My name is Marlene." "Meu nome é Marlene."
2 "I am from Brazil." "Eu sou do Brasil."
3 "Nice to meet you." "Prazer em conhecer você."
4 "I do not speak English very well." "Eu não falo inglês muito bem."
5 "Could you speak slowly, please?" "Poderia falar devagar, por favor?"
Aula 02 -- Pre-class

My World in Words

Daily Routines & Present Simple Affirmative
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary

Vocabulary
Routine·rotina
"I have a morning routine."
Wake up·acordar
"I wake up at six in the morning."
Breakfast·café da manhã
"I have breakfast before work."
Exercise·exercício / exercitar-se
"I exercise three times a week."
Usually·geralmente
"I usually go to the office at eight."
Always·sempre
"I always drink coffee in the morning."
Sometimes·às vezes
"Sometimes I play tennis after work."
Schedule·agenda / horário
"My schedule is very busy."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice
Routine
Wake up
Breakfast
Exercise
Usually
Always
Sometimes
Schedule

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Leia o texto abaixo. As palavras em negrito são o vocabulário desta aula. Preste atenção no Present Simple e nos adverbs of frequency (always, usually, sometimes, never).

Marlene wakes up at six every morning. She has a morning routine. She always drinks coffee and has breakfast before work. She usually checks her properties at eight.

In the afternoon, she sometimes plays tennis. She exercises three times a week. Her schedule is very busy, but she loves it!

Marlene never goes to bed late. She says: "My routine keeps me healthy and happy."

1. When does Marlene wake up? (Quando a Marlene acorda?)
A At six in the morning
B At eight in the morning
C At noon
2. How often does Marlene exercise? (Com que frequência ela se exercita?)
A Every day
B Three times a week
C Never
3. Consider the sentence "She drinks coffee." Where does the adverb "always" go? (Considere a frase "She drinks coffee." Onde vai o advérbio "always"?)
A She always drinks coffee (before the verb)
B She drinks always coffee (after the verb)
C She drinks coffee always (at the end of the sentence)

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

Present Simple Affirmative + Adverbs of Frequency (Presente Simples Afirmativo + Advérbios de Frequência)

Subject (Sujeito)Verb (Verbo)Example (Exemplo)
I / You / We / They (eu/você/nós/eles)verb (base form)I wake up at six.
He / She / It (ele/ela)verb + s/esShe wakes up at six.

Adverbs of Frequency (Advérbios de Frequência):

Adverb (Advérbio)TraduçãoFrequency (Frequência)Example (Exemplo)
alwayssempre100%I always drink coffee.
usuallygeralmente80%I usually go at eight.
sometimesàs vezes40%I sometimes play tennis.
nevernunca0%I never skip breakfast.

Posição do advérbio (Adverb position):

Subject + adverb + verb: "I always drink coffee."

Exceção: Sometimes pode ir no início: "Sometimes I play tennis."

Erro comum / Common mistake:

She wake up at six. ✗

She wakes up at six. ✓ (He/She/It + verb + s)

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice
"I at six in the morning."
"She has breakfast at seven."
"We exercise in the morning."
"He travels for work."
"They skip breakfast."
"My is very busy."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Organize the daily routine in the correct order (Organize a descrição da rotina diária na ordem correta):

? "I usually check my properties at eight."
? "I never go to bed late."
? "I wake up at six in the morning."
? "I sometimes play tennis in the afternoon."
? "I always have breakfast at seven."

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking
I always have coffee in the morning.
I usually check my properties before lunch.
I sometimes play tennis in the afternoon.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
Your friend asks: "What time do you wake up?" How do you answer? (Sua amiga pergunta: "Que horas você acorda?" Como você responde?)
A "I wakes up six morning."
B "I usually wake up at six in the morning."
C "I always waking up early."
Someone at a café in Rome asks: "Do you exercise?" How do you respond? (Alguém num café em Roma pergunta: "Você se exercita?" Como responder?)
A "I exercise does three week."
B "Exercise is sometimes."
C "Yes! I exercise three times a week. I usually play tennis."

Stage 5: Think and Speak

Reflection
Imagine you are at a café in Rome. A new friend asks about your daily routine. Describe your typical day: when you wake up, what you have for breakfast, what you do for work, and your hobbies. Use "always", "usually", and "sometimes". (Imagine que você está num café em Roma. Descreva sua rotina diária usando "always", "usually" e "sometimes".)

Sugestão: "I usually wake up at six. I always have coffee for breakfast. I sometimes play tennis in the afternoon."

Survival Card -- Aula 2

1 "I always have coffee in the morning." "Eu sempre tomo café de manhã."
2 "I usually wake up early." "Eu geralmente acordo cedo."
3 "My schedule is very busy." "Minha agenda é muito cheia."
4 "Sometimes I play tennis after work." "Eu às vezes jogo tênis após o trabalho."
5 "I never skip my morning routine." "Eu nunca pulo minha rotina matinal."
Aula 03 -- Pre-class

Numbers That Matter

Numbers 1-100 & Time Expressions
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary

Vocabulary
Number·número
"What is your phone number?"
Clock·relógio
"The clock says ten thirty."
Price·preço
"What is the price of this ticket?"
Gate·portão (de embarque)
"Your gate is number fourteen."
Hundred·cem / cento
"The ticket costs one hundred dollars."
Half·metade / meia (hora)
"It is half past ten."
Quarter·quarto (15 minutos)
"It is a quarter to three."
Flight·voo
"My flight is at two fifteen."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice
Number
Clock
Price
Gate
Hundred
Half
Quarter
Flight

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Leia o texto abaixo. As palavras em negrito são o vocabulário desta aula. Preste atenção em como dizemos horas e preços em inglês.

Marlene is at the airport. She checks the clock. It is half past one. Her flight is at a quarter to three. She needs to find gate fourteen.

She stops at a shop. "How much is this?" The price is twenty-five dollars. She also buys a coffee for three dollars and fifty cents. The total number is twenty-eight dollars and fifty cents.

At gate fourteen, she hears: "Flight 472, boarding in fifteen minutes." One hundred passengers are waiting. Marlene is ready!

1. What time is Marlene's flight? (A que horas é o voo da Marlene?)
A At half past one
B At a quarter to three
C At three o'clock
2. How much is the coffee? (Quanto custa o café?)
A Twenty-five dollars
B Three dollars and fifty cents
C Twenty-eight dollars
3. How do you say "2:45" in English? (Como se diz "2:45" em inglês?)
A A quarter to three
B A quarter past two
C Half past two

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

Telling Time + Asking Prices (Como dizer as horas + Perguntar preços)

Time (Hora)How to Say (Como dizer)Example (Exemplo)
3:00three o'clock"It is three o'clock."
3:15a quarter past three"It is a quarter past three."
3:30half past three"It is half past three."
2:45a quarter to three"It is a quarter to three."

Asking Prices (Perguntando preços):

Question (Pergunta)TraduçãoAnswer (Resposta)
How much is this?Quanto custa isso?"It is twenty-five dollars."
What time is it?Que horas são?"It is half past two."
What time is your flight?A que horas é seu voo?"My flight is at three o'clock."

Números importantes (Key numbers):

10 = ten, 15 = fifteen, 20 = twenty, 25 = twenty-five, 30 = thirty, 45 = forty-five, 50 = fifty, 100 = one hundred

Erro comum / Common mistake:

It is fifteen to three. ✗

It is a quarter to three. ✓ (15 min = a quarter)

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice
"My is at two fifteen."
"It is past ten."
"What is the of this ticket?"
"Your is number fourteen."
"The ticket costs one dollars."
"It is a to three."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Organize a sequência da Marlene no aeroporto na ordem correta:

?"How much is a coffee? Three dollars and fifty cents."
?"Flight 472, now boarding at gate fourteen."
?"Marlene checks the clock. It is half past one."
?"She walks to gate fourteen and waits."
?"Excuse me, what gate is flight 472? Gate fourteen."

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking
My flight is at three o'clock.
Meu voo é às três horas.
How much is this?
Quanto custa isso?
I need to be at gate fourteen at two thirty.
Eu preciso estar no portão quatorze às duas e meia.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
You are at a shop in the airport. You want to know how much a souvenir costs. What do you say? (Você quer saber o preço de uma lembrancinha. O que diz?)
A "What is the number of this?"
B "How much is this?"
C "What time is this?"
Someone asks: "What time is your flight?" Your flight is at 2:30. How do you answer? (Seu voo é às 2:30. O que responde?)
A "My flight is at two and thirty."
B "My flight is at half past two."
C "My flight is at quarter two."

Stage 5: Think and Speak

Reflection
Imagine you are at the airport in Rome. Tell someone about your flight: what time it leaves, what gate, and how much you paid for a coffee. Use numbers, "half past", "a quarter to", and "How much is...?" (Imagine que você está no aeroporto em Roma. Conte sobre seu voo.)

Sugestão: "My flight is at half past two. I need to go to gate fourteen. I bought a coffee for three dollars and fifty cents."

Survival Card -- Aula 3

1"What time is it?""Que horas são?"
2"How much is this?""Quanto custa isso?"
3"My flight is at half past two.""Meu voo é às duas e meia."
4"What gate is my flight?""Qual portão do meu voo?"
5"The price is twenty-five dollars.""O preço é vinte e cinco dólares."
Aula 04 -- Pre-class

Describing People and Places

Adjectives + "to be + adjective", "there is / there are"
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary

Vocabulary
Beautiful·bonito(a)
"Rome is a beautiful city."
Famous·famoso(a)
"The Colosseum is very famous."
Crowded·lotado(a)
"The streets are crowded in the morning."
Quiet·tranquilo(a)
"My hotel is quiet and comfortable."
Modern·moderno(a)
"Istanbul is a modern and ancient city."
Ancient·antigo(a)
"Istanbul is a modern and ancient city."
Friendly·simpático(a)
"The people are very friendly."
Delicious·delicioso(a)
"The food in Italy is delicious."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice
Beautiful
Famous
Crowded
Quiet
Modern
Ancient
Friendly
Delicious

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Leia o texto abaixo. As palavras em negrito são o vocabulário desta aula. Preste atenção em como usamos "to be + adjective" e "there is / there are".

Marlene is in Rome! The city is beautiful and very famous. There are many ancient buildings and modern shops. The streets are crowded in the morning, but her hotel is quiet and comfortable.

There is a small restaurant near the hotel. The food is delicious! The people are very friendly. There are also many parks. Marlene loves this city!

1. How is the city of Rome? (Como é a cidade de Roma?)
A Rome is beautiful and famous.
B Rome is quiet and modern.
C Rome is crowded and ancient.
2. What is near the hotel? (O que tem perto do hotel?)
A There is a big park.
B There is a small restaurant.
C There is a famous museum.
3. How are the people in Rome? (Como são as pessoas em Roma?)
A The people are quiet.
B The people are very friendly.
C The people are modern.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

To be + Adjective (Ser/Estar + Adjetivo)

Subject (Sujeito)to beAdjective (Adjetivo)Example (Exemplo)
Romeisbeautiful"Rome is beautiful."
The streetsarecrowded"The streets are crowded."
The foodisdelicious"The food is delicious."
The peoplearefriendly"The people are friendly."

There is / There are (Tem / Há)

Structure (Estrutura)Use (Uso)Example (Exemplo)
There isSingular (1 coisa)"There is a park near my hotel."
There arePlural (2+ coisas)"There are many restaurants."

Erro comum / Common mistake:

Have a park near here. ✗

There is a park near here. ✓

Em inglês, usamos "there is / there are" para dizer que algo existe em um lugar. Nunca "have" sozinho.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice
"Rome is a city."
" a park near my hotel."
"The people are very ."
" many restaurants in Rome."
"The food in Italy is ."
"The Colosseum is very ."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Organize a descrição do passeio da Marlene por Roma na ordem correta:

?"There is a beautiful park near the Colosseum."
?"Rome is a beautiful and ancient city."
?"The food is delicious and the people are friendly."
?"The streets are crowded but very famous."
?"My hotel is quiet and comfortable."

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking
There is a beautiful park near my hotel.
Tem um parque bonito perto do meu hotel.
The people are very friendly.
As pessoas são muito simpáticas.
São Paulo is a big and modern city.
São Paulo é uma cidade grande e moderna.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
You are visiting Istanbul and a friend asks: "How is the city?" What do you say? (Você está visitando Istambul e um amigo pergunta: "Como é a cidade?")
A "Istanbul has modern and ancient."
B "Istanbul is a modern and ancient city."
C "There is Istanbul modern and ancient."
You arrive at a new neighborhood and want to describe it. There are 5 restaurants. How do you say this? (Você chega em um bairro novo e quer descrever. Há 5 restaurantes.)
A "Have five restaurants here."
B "There is five restaurants here."
C "There are five restaurants here."

Stage 5: Think and Speak

Reflection
Descreva o seu bairro, Vila Nova Conceição, usando adjetivos e "there is / there are". Fale sobre os restaurantes, parques, ruas e pessoas. (Describe your neighborhood using adjectives and "there is / there are". Talk about the restaurants, parks, streets, and people.)

Sugestão: "My neighborhood is beautiful and quiet. There are many restaurants. The people are friendly. There is a big park near my house."

Survival Card -- Aula 4

1"Is there a pharmacy near here?""Tem uma farmácia perto daqui?"
2"The city is very beautiful.""A cidade é muito bonita."
3"The people are friendly.""As pessoas são simpáticas."
4"There are many restaurants.""Tem muitos restaurantes."
5"My hotel is quiet and comfortable.""Meu hotel é tranquilo e confortável."
Aula 05 -- Pre-class

Block 1 Review

Review: to be, present simple, adjectives, numbers
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Review

Vocabulary
Introduce·apresentar
"Let me introduce myself. I am Marlene."
Routine·rotina
"I have a morning routine."
Flight·voo
"My flight is at half past two."
Beautiful·bonito(a)
"Rome is a beautiful city."
Manage·gerenciar
"I manage properties in São Paulo."
Schedule·horário/agenda
"My schedule is very busy."
Gate·portão de embarque
"The flight leaves from gate twelve."
Friendly·simpático(a)
"The people are very friendly."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice
Introduce
Routine
Flight
Beautiful
Manage
Schedule
Gate
Friendly

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Leia o texto abaixo. Ele revisa TODAS as estruturas das Aulas 1-4: to be, present simple + advérbios de frequência, números e horas, adjetivos + there is / there are.

My name is Marlene. I am from Brazil. I manage properties in São Paulo. I usually wake up at six in the morning. I always have coffee before work.

Today I have a trip to Rome! My flight is at half past two. The gate is number twelve. The ticket costs three hundred dollars.

Rome is beautiful and ancient. The people are friendly. There is a nice cafe near my hotel. There are many famous monuments. I love this city!

1. What does Marlene do in São Paulo? (O que a Marlene faz em São Paulo?)
A She manages properties.
B She is a teacher.
C She works at a restaurant.
2. What time is her flight? (A que horas é o voo dela?)
A At six in the morning.
B At half past two.
C At twelve o'clock.
3. What is near her hotel in Rome? (O que tem perto do hotel dela em Roma?)
A There is a big park.
B There is a nice cafe.
C There are many shops.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Block 1 Summary

Grammar

1. To Be (Ser/Estar)

SubjectTo beExample
Iam"I am from Brazil."
He/She/Itis"Rome is beautiful."
We/You/Theyare"The people are friendly."

2. Present Simple + Advérbios de Frequência

Adverb (Advérbio)Frequency (Frequência)Example (Exemplo)
Always100%"I always have coffee."
Usually80%"I usually wake up at six."
Sometimes40%"I sometimes play tennis."

3. Telling Time (Horas)

Expression (Expressão)Meaning (Significado)Example (Exemplo)
half paste meia"My flight is at half past two."
quarter paste quinze"It is quarter past ten."
o'clockhora cheia"I wake up at six o'clock."

4. Adjectives + There is / There are (Adjetivos + Ter/Haver)

Structure (Estrutura)Use (Uso)Example (Exemplo)
Subject + is/are + adjectiveDescrever"Rome is beautiful."
There is + singularExiste (1 coisa)"There is a cafe."
There are + pluralExistem (2+ coisas)"There are many monuments."

Erros comuns / Common mistakes:

I have 54 years. ✗

I am 54 years old. ✓

I always am happy. ✗

I am always happy. ✓

Have a park near here. ✗

There is a park near here. ✓

Lembre-se: "to be" para idade e descrições, advérbio ANTES do verbo principal (mas DEPOIS do "to be"), e "there is/are" para dizer que algo existe.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice
"I from Brazil."
"I wake up at six."
"My flight is at two."
"The gate number is ."
"Rome is and ancient."
" a nice cafe near my hotel."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Organize a jornada completa da Marlene na ordem cronológica:

?"My flight is at half past two. The gate is number twelve."
?"The people are friendly. There is a nice cafe near my hotel."
?"My name is Marlene. I am from Brazil. I manage properties."
?"Rome is beautiful and ancient. There are many famous monuments."
?"I usually wake up at six. I always have coffee before work."

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking
My name is Marlene. I am from Brazil. I manage properties.
Meu nome é Marlene. Eu sou do Brasil. Eu gerencio imóveis.
I usually wake up at six. I always have coffee.
Eu geralmente acordo às seis. Eu sempre tomo café.
Rome is beautiful and ancient. The people are friendly.
Roma é bonita e antiga. As pessoas são simpáticas.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
You meet someone at the airport and they ask: "Tell me about yourself." What do you say? (Você conhece alguém no aeroporto e perguntam: "Fale sobre você.")
A "I have Marlene. I have from Brazil."
B "I am Marlene. I am from Brazil. I manage properties."
C "My name Marlene. Brazil. Properties."
You are in Rome and want to tell your friend about the city. How do you describe it? (Você está em Roma e quer contar para uma amiga sobre a cidade.)
A "Rome has beautiful. Have many restaurants."
B "Rome beautiful and ancient. People friendly."
C "Rome is beautiful and ancient. The people are friendly. There are many restaurants."

Stage 5: Think and Speak

Reflection
Imagine que você encontrou alguém em um café em Roma. Apresente-se, descreva sua rotina, diga a hora e descreva a cidade. Use tudo que aprendeu no Bloco 1. (You meet someone at a cafe in Rome. Introduce yourself, describe your routine, tell the time, and describe the city. Use everything you learned in Block 1.)

Sugestão: "Hi, my name is Marlene. I am from Brazil. I manage properties. I usually wake up at six. It is half past three now. Rome is beautiful and ancient. The people are friendly. There is a nice cafe near my hotel."

Survival Card -- Block 1 Review

1"Let me introduce myself. I am Marlene.""Deixe eu me apresentar. Eu sou a Marlene."
2"I usually wake up at six.""Eu geralmente acordo às seis."
3"My flight is at half past two.""Meu voo é às duas e meia."
4"Rome is beautiful and ancient.""Roma é bonita e antiga."
5"There is a nice cafe near my hotel.""Tem um café legal perto do meu hotel."
Aula 06 -- Pre-class

At the Airport

Could I...? / I would like...
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary

Vocabulary
Passport·passaporte
"Here is my passport and my ticket."
Luggage·bagagem
"I would like to check this luggage."
Check-in·check-in
"I would like to check in for my flight."
Boarding pass·cartão de embarque
"Is this my boarding pass?"
Aisle·corredor
"Could I have an aisle seat, please?"
Window·janela
"Could I have a window seat, please?"
Carry-on·bagagem de mão
"I have one carry-on bag."
Departure·partida
"Where is the departure gate?"

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice
Passport
Luggage
Check-in
Boarding pass
Aisle
Window
Carry-on
Departure

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Leia o texto abaixo. Marlene está no balcão de check-in do aeroporto. Preste atenção nas estruturas "Could I...?" e "I would like..." em negrito.

Marlene is at the airport. She walks to the check-in counter with her passport and her luggage.

She says: "Good morning. I would like to check in for my flight to Rome, please." The attendant asks for her passport. Marlene says: "Here is my passport."

The attendant asks: "Would you like a window or aisle seat?" Marlene answers: "Could I have a window seat, please?" The attendant gives her the boarding pass.

Marlene asks: "Where is the departure gate?" The attendant says: "Gate twelve, ma'am." Marlene says: "Thank you! I would like to take my carry-on bag with me."

1. O que Marlene pede no balcão de check-in? (What does Marlene ask for at the counter?)
A She asks for a taxi.
B She would like to check in for her flight.
C She asks for a hotel room.
2. Que tipo de assento Marlene escolhe? (What kind of seat does Marlene choose?)
A An aisle seat.
B A window seat.
C A middle seat.
3. Onde é o portão de embarque? (Where is the departure gate?)
A Gate five.
B Gate twenty.
C Gate twelve.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Polite Requests

Grammar

Em inglês, usamos "Could I...?" e "I would like..." para fazer pedidos educados. Veja a tabela:

Structure (Estrutura)Use (Uso)Example (Exemplo)
Could I + verb?Pedido/pergunta educada"Could I have a window seat, please?"
I would like + nounQuerer algo (educado)"I would like a coffee, please."
I would like + to verbQuerer fazer algo (educado)"I would like to check in for my flight."

Erro comum / Common mistake:

I want a seat. ✗

I would like a seat, please. ✓

Lembre-se: "I want" é direto demais em inglês. Use "I would like" para ser educado(a). Para perguntar, use "Could I...?" em vez de "Can I...?" (mais formal).

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice
" have a window seat, please?"
" to check in for my flight."
"Here is my and my ticket."
"Where is the gate?"
"I have one bag."
"Is this my ?"

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Organize a sequência do check-in no aeroporto na ordem correta:

?"Could I have a window seat, please?"
?"I would like to check in for my flight to Rome."
?"Where is the departure gate?"
?"Here is my passport and my ticket."
?"I have one carry-on bag."

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking
Could I have a window seat, please?
Eu poderia ter um assento na janela, por favor?
I would like to check in for my flight.
Eu gostaria de fazer o check-in do meu voo.
Here is my passport and my ticket.
Aqui está meu passaporte e minha passagem.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
Você está no balcão de check-in e quer um assento no corredor. O que você diz? (You are at the check-in counter and want an aisle seat. What do you say?)
A "I want aisle seat."
B "Could I have an aisle seat, please?"
C "Give me aisle."
Você quer despachar sua bagagem. Como faz o pedido educadamente? (You want to check your luggage. How do you ask politely?)
A "Check my luggage now."
B "I want check luggage."
C "I would like to check this luggage, please."

Stage 5: Think and Speak

Reflection
Imagine que você está no balcão de check-in do aeroporto. Faça o check-in, peça o tipo de assento que prefere, pergunte sobre o portão de embarque e mencione sua bagagem de mão. Use "Could I...?" e "I would like..." (Imagine you are at the airport check-in counter. Check in, ask for your preferred seat, ask about the departure gate, and mention your carry-on bag.)

Sugestão: "Good morning. I would like to check in for my flight to Rome. Here is my passport. Could I have a window seat, please? I have one carry-on bag. Where is the departure gate? Thank you!"

Survival Card -- Lesson 6

1"Could I have a window seat?""Eu poderia ter um assento na janela?"
2"I would like to check in.""Eu gostaria de fazer o check-in."
3"Here is my passport.""Aqui está meu passaporte."
4"Where is the departure gate?""Onde é o portão de embarque?"
5"I have one carry-on bag.""Eu tenho uma bagagem de mão."
Aula 07 -- Pre-class

On the Plane

Can I have...? / Could you...?
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary

Vocabulary
Seatbelt·cinto de segurança
"Please fasten your seatbelt."
Overhead·compartimento superior
"Could you put your bag in the overhead compartment?"
Tray table·mesinha
"Could you open the tray table?"
Blanket·cobertor
"Can I have a blanket, please?"
Turbulence·turbulência
"We are experiencing some turbulence."
Landing·pouso/aterrissagem
"We will be landing in thirty minutes."
Snack·lanche
"Can I have a snack, please?"
Headphones·fones de ouvido
"Could I have some headphones?"

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice
Seatbelt
Overhead
Tray table
Blanket
Turbulence
Landing
Snack
Headphones

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Leia o texto abaixo. Marlene está dentro do avião, pedindo coisas à comissária de bordo. Preste atenção nas estruturas "Can I have...?" e "Could you...?" em negrito.

Marlene is on the plane. She finds her seat and puts her bag in the overhead compartment. A flight attendant says: "Please fasten your seatbelt."

After takeoff, Marlene is cold. She calls the flight attendant: "Can I have a blanket, please?" The attendant brings her one. Then Marlene says: "Can I have a snack and some water, please?"

The flight attendant asks: "Would you like headphones for the movie?" Marlene says: "Yes, please! Could you help me open the tray table? It is stuck."

Later, the captain announces: "We are experiencing some turbulence. Please fasten your seatbelts." After a few minutes, the captain says: "We will be landing in thirty minutes."

1. O que Marlene pede quando está com frio? (What does Marlene ask for when she is cold?)
A She asks for headphones.
B She asks for a blanket.
C She asks for a pillow.
2. Por que Marlene pede ajuda com a mesinha? (Why does Marlene ask for help with the tray table?)
A It is broken.
B It is stuck.
C It is dirty.
3. Em quanto tempo o avião vai pousar? (How soon will the plane land?)
A In ten minutes.
B In one hour.
C In thirty minutes.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Polite Requests

Grammar

Em inglês, usamos "Can I have...?" para pedir coisas e "Could you...?" para pedir que alguém faça algo. Veja a tabela:

Structure (Estrutura)Use (Uso)Example (Exemplo)
Can I have + noun?Pedir coisas (asking for things)"Can I have a blanket, please?"
Could you + verb?Pedir que alguém faça algo (asking someone to do something)"Could you help me with my bag?"

Erro comum / Common mistake:

Give me water. ✗

Can I have some water, please? ✓

Lembre-se: "Give me" é direto demais e pode parecer rude. Use "Can I have...?" para pedir coisas e "Could you...?" para pedir ações.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice
" a blanket, please?"
" help me with my bag?"
"Please fasten your ."
"We will be in thirty minutes."
" a snack, please?"
" open the tray table?"

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Organize a sequência de um voo na ordem correta:

?"The plane takes off."
?"You board the plane and find your seat."
?"The plane is landing."
?"You fasten your seatbelt."
?"The flight attendant serves a meal."

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking
Can I have a blanket, please?
Posso ter um cobertor, por favor?
Could you help me with my bag? It is very heavy.
Você poderia me ajudar com minha mala? Ela é muito pesada.
We will be landing in thirty minutes.
Nós vamos pousar em trinta minutos.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
Você está com frio no avião e quer um cobertor. O que você diz? (You are cold on the plane and want a blanket. What do you say?)
A "Give me blanket."
B "Can I have a blanket, please?"
C "I want blanket now."
Sua mesinha está travada e você quer pedir ajuda à comissária. O que você diz? (Your tray table is stuck and you want to ask the flight attendant for help. What do you say?)
A "Open this table."
B "Table is broken."
C "Could you help me open the tray table?"

Stage 5: Think and Speak

Reflection
Imagine que você está no avião. Descreva o que você precisa: peça um cobertor, um lanche, fones de ouvido e ajuda com a mesinha. Use "Can I have...?" e "Could you...?" (Imagine you are on the plane. Describe what you need: ask for a blanket, a snack, headphones, and help with the tray table.)

Sugestão: "Excuse me, can I have a blanket, please? I am cold. Can I have a snack and some water? Could you help me open the tray table? It is stuck. Could I have some headphones for the movie? Thank you!"

Survival Card -- Lesson 7

1"Can I have a blanket, please?""Posso ter um cobertor, por favor?"
2"Could you help me with my bag?""Você poderia me ajudar com minha mala?"
3"Can I have a snack, please?""Posso ter um lanche, por favor?"
4"Please fasten your seatbelt.""Por favor, aperte o cinto de segurança."
5"We will be landing in thirty minutes.""Nós vamos pousar em trinta minutos."
Aula 08 -- Pre-class

Immigration and Customs

Questions with do/does
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary

Vocabulary
Customs·alfândega
"You must go through customs when you arrive."
Declaration·declaração
"Do you have a customs declaration form?"
Purpose·propósito
"What is the purpose of your visit?"
Vacation·férias
"I am here on vacation."
Declare·declarar
"Do you have anything to declare?"
Stamp·carimbo
"The officer stamps your passport."
Return·retorno/volta
"Do you have a return ticket?"
Citizen·cidadão
"Are you a Brazilian citizen?"

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice
Customs
Declaration
Purpose
Vacation
Declare
Stamp
Return
Citizen

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Leia o texto abaixo. Marlene está passando pela imigração no aeroporto de Roma. Preste atenção nas perguntas com "do" e "does" em negrito.

Marlene arrives at the airport in Rome. She goes to the immigration line. The officer looks at her passport and asks: "Do you have a return ticket?" Marlene answers: "Yes, I do."

The officer continues: "What is the purpose of your visit?" Marlene says: "I am here on vacation." Then the officer asks: "Do you have anything to declare?" She says: "No, I do not."

He checks the customs declaration form and says: "Everything is fine." He stamps her passport. Marlene asks: "Does the airport have a taxi area?" The officer says: "Yes, it does. Go straight and turn left."

Marlene is happy. She is officially in Italy! As a Brazilian citizen, she does not need a visa for short visits.

1. O que o oficial pergunta primeiro para Marlene? (What does the officer ask Marlene first?)
A He asks for her visa.
B He asks if she has a return ticket.
C He asks her name.
2. Qual é o propósito da visita de Marlene? (What is the purpose of Marlene's visit?)
A She is here for work.
B She is here to study.
C She is here on vacation.
3. Marlene precisa de visto para visitas curtas? (Does Marlene need a visa for short visits?)
A No, she does not.
B Yes, she does.
C The text does not say.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Questions with Do / Does

Grammar

Em inglês, usamos "do" e "does" para fazer perguntas no presente simples. Veja a tabela:

Auxiliary (Auxiliar)Subject (Sujeito)Example (Exemplo)
DoI / you / we / they"Do you have a return ticket?"
Doeshe / she / it"Does she need a visa?"

Respostas curtas (Short answers):

AffirmativeNegative
"Yes, I do.""No, I do not."
"Yes, she does.""No, she does not."

Erro comum / Common mistake:

Do she have a visa? ✗

Does she have a visa? ✓

Lembre-se: com he/she/it, use sempre "does", nunca "do". O verbo principal fica na forma base (have, need, come), sem "s".

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice
" a return ticket?"
"What is the of your visit?"
" a visa?"
"Do you have anything to ?"
"Where from?"
"The officer your passport."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Organize a sequência da imigração na ordem correta:

?"The officer asks about the purpose of your visit."
?"You collect your luggage and leave the airport."
?"You arrive at the airport and go to the immigration line."
?"The officer stamps your passport."
?"You show your passport to the officer."

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking
Do you have a return ticket?
Você tem uma passagem de volta?
What is the purpose of your visit?
Qual é o propósito da sua visita?
I am here on vacation.
Eu estou aqui de férias.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
O oficial da imigração pergunta: "Do you have anything to declare?" Você não tem nada. O que você responde? (The immigration officer asks: "Do you have anything to declare?" You have nothing. What do you say?)
A "I not have."
B "No, I do not."
C "No have nothing."
Você quer saber se o aeroporto tem uma área de táxi. Como você pergunta? (You want to know if the airport has a taxi area. How do you ask?)
A "Have taxi here?"
B "Where is taxi?"
C "Does the airport have a taxi area?"

Stage 5: Think and Speak

Reflection
Imagine que você está na imigração em Roma. O oficial faz perguntas. Responda: de onde você vem, qual o propósito da visita, se você tem passagem de volta, e se tem algo a declarar. Use "I do" / "I do not" nas respostas. (Imagine you are at immigration in Rome. The officer asks you questions. Answer: where you come from, the purpose of your visit, if you have a return ticket, and if you have anything to declare.)

Sugestão: "I come from Brazil. I am here on vacation. Yes, I do have a return ticket. No, I do not have anything to declare. I am a Brazilian citizen."

Survival Card -- Lesson 8

1"Do you have a return ticket?""Você tem uma passagem de volta?"
2"I am here on vacation.""Estou aqui de férias."
3"No, I do not have anything to declare.""Não, eu não tenho nada a declarar."
4"Where do you come from?""De onde você vem?"
5"I am a Brazilian citizen.""Eu sou cidadã brasileira."
Aula 09 -- Pre-class

Baggage and Ground Transport

Possessives, Where is...?
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary

Vocabulary
Suitcase·mala
"This is my suitcase. It is black and big."
Baggage claim·esteira de bagagem
"Where is the baggage claim?"
Taxi·táxi
"I need a taxi, please."
Driver·motorista
"Could you take me to this address?"
Address·endereço
"My hotel address is Via Roma 15, Rome."
Lost·perdido(a)
"My luggage is lost."
Receipt·recibo
"Can I have a receipt, please?"
Meter·taxímetro
"Is the meter running?"

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice
Suitcase
Baggage claim
Taxi
Driver
Address
Lost
Receipt
Meter

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Leia o texto abaixo. Marlene acabou de passar pela imigração e agora precisa pegar a bagagem e encontrar um táxi. Preste atenção nos possessivos (my, your, his, her) e nas perguntas com "Where is...?" em negrito.

Marlene walks to the baggage claim area. She looks around and thinks: "Where is my suitcase?" She waits fifteen minutes, but her luggage does not arrive. She goes to the help desk and says: "My luggage is lost."

The agent asks: "What does your suitcase look like?" Marlene says: "My suitcase is big and black." The agent says: "Your suitcase is on its way. Please wait."

After a few minutes, Marlene finds her suitcase. She goes outside and asks: "Where is the taxi area?" A man points left. She finds a taxi and says to the driver: "Could you take me to this address? Via Roma 15." She asks: "Is the meter running?" The driver says: "Yes, do not worry." When they arrive, Marlene asks: "Can I have a receipt, please?" His name is Marco, and he gives her the receipt.

1. O que Marlene percebe na esteira de bagagem? (What does Marlene notice at the baggage claim?)
A Her suitcase arrives quickly.
B Her luggage is lost.
C She forgets her suitcase.
2. O que Marlene pergunta ao sair do aeroporto? (What does Marlene ask when she leaves the airport?)
A "Where is the taxi area?"
B "Where is the bus stop?"
C "Where is my hotel?"
3. Qual é o nome do motorista do táxi? (What is the taxi driver's name?)
A Giovanni.
B Roberto.
C Marco.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Possessives + Where is / Where are

Grammar

Em inglês, usamos possessivos para dizer a quem algo pertence, e "Where is...?" / "Where are...?" para perguntar sobre localização. Veja a tabela:

Possessive (Possessivo)Subject (Sujeito)Example (Exemplo)
myI"My suitcase is big."
youryou"Your suitcase is on its way."
hishe"His name is Marco."
hershe"He gives her the receipt."
ourwe"Our hotel is near the center."
theirthey"Their luggage is in the taxi."

"Where is...?" / "Where are...?" (Onde está...? / Onde estão...?):

SingularPlural
"Where is my suitcase?""Where are my bags?"
"Where is the taxi area?""Where are the taxis?"

Erro comum / Common mistake:

Where is my luggages? ✗

Where is my luggage? ✓

Lembre-se: "luggage" é incontavel em inglês, então nunca recebe "s". Use sempre "Where is my luggage?" (singular).

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice
" the baggage claim?"
" luggage is lost."
"Could you take me to this ?"
"Is the running?"
"Can I have a , please?"
" name is Marco."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Organize a sequência da chegada de Marlene na ordem correta:

?"Marlene tells the agent: My luggage is lost."
?"Marlene walks to the baggage claim."
?"She arrives at the hotel and asks for a receipt."
?"She takes a taxi and gives the driver the address."
?"She waits for her suitcase but it does not arrive."

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking
Where is the baggage claim?
Onde é a esteira de bagagem?
My luggage is lost.
Minha bagagem está perdida.
Could you take me to this address?
Você poderia me levar a este endereço?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
Você está na esteira de bagagem, mas sua mala não aparece. O que você diz no balcão de ajuda? (You are at the baggage claim, but your suitcase does not appear. What do you say at the help desk?)
A "I want suitcase."
B "My luggage is lost."
C "Suitcase go where?"
Você entra no táxi e quer ter certeza de que o taxímetro está ligado. Como você pergunta? (You get in the taxi and want to make sure the meter is on. How do you ask?)
A "How much money?"
B "You use meter?"
C "Is the meter running?"

Stage 5: Think and Speak

Reflection
Imagine que você acabou de chegar em Roma. Descreva a sua experiência: você pegou a mala na esteira? A mala estava perdida? Como você chegou ao hotel? Usou táxi? Use possessivos (my, your, his, her) e "Where is...?" na sua resposta. (Imagine you just arrived in Rome. Describe your experience: did you get your suitcase? Was it lost? How did you get to the hotel? Did you take a taxi?)

Sugestão: "I arrived at the airport and went to the baggage claim. Where is my suitcase? My suitcase was there. Then I took a taxi. I gave the driver my hotel address. His name was Marco. He gave me a receipt."

Survival Card -- Lesson 9

1"Where is the baggage claim?""Onde é a esteira de bagagem?"
2"My luggage is lost.""Minha bagagem está perdida."
3"Could you take me to this address?""Você poderia me levar a este endereço?"
4"Is the meter running?""O taxímetro está ligado?"
5"Can I have a receipt, please?""Posso ter um recibo, por favor?"
Aula 10 -- Pre-class

Block 2 Review

Review of Lessons 6-9: Airport, Plane, Immigration, Baggage
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Review

Vocabulary
Boarding pass·cartão de embarque
"Please show me your boarding pass."
Carry-on·bagagem de mão
"Is this your carry-on bag?"
Purpose·motivo / propósito
"What is the purpose of your visit?"
Suitcase·mala
"My suitcase is big and black."
Departure·partida / embarque
"The departure gate is B12."
Headphones·fones de ouvido
"Can I have headphones, please?"
Declare·declarar
"Do you have anything to declare?"
Receipt·recibo
"Can I have a receipt, please?"

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice
Boarding pass
Carry-on
Purpose
Suitcase
Departure
Headphones
Declare
Receipt

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Leia o texto abaixo. Marlene relembra toda a sua jornada no aeroporto, desde o check-in até a chegada ao hotel. Preste atenção nas 4 estruturas em negrito: can/could, this/that, possessivos (my, your, his, her) e "Where is...?".

Marlene arrives at the airport very early. She goes to the check-in counter and says: "Can I check in for my flight to Rome?" The agent says: "Yes! This is your boarding pass. Your departure gate is B12."

She puts her suitcase on the belt and keeps her carry-on bag. At the gate, she asks: "Could I have headphones, please?" On the plane, she watches a movie and listens to music.

When they land, Marlene goes through immigration. The officer asks: "What is the purpose of your visit?" She answers: "Tourism." Then he asks: "Do you have anything to declare?" She says: "No, nothing."

At the baggage claim, she thinks: "Where is my suitcase?" After a few minutes, she finds it. She goes outside and takes a taxi. She gives the driver her hotel address: "Via Roma 15." She asks: "Can I have a receipt, please?" His name is Marco. That was a long but good day!

1. O que Marlene faz primeiro no aeroporto? (What does Marlene do first at the airport?)
A She checks in for her flight.
B She goes to the baggage claim.
C She takes a taxi to the hotel.
2. O que o oficial de imigração pergunta? (What does the immigration officer ask?)
A "Where is your suitcase?"
B "What is the purpose of your visit?"
C "Can I see your boarding pass?"
3. Como Marlene descreve o dia dela no final? (How does Marlene describe her day at the end?)
A A short and boring day.
B A stressful and terrible day.
C A long but good day.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Block 2 Summary

Grammar

Revisão das 4 estruturas gramaticais do Bloco 2 (Aulas 6-9):

1. Can / Could (pedidos e permissão)

EstruturaUsoExemplo
Can I...?Pedido informal"Can I have a receipt, please?"
Could I...?Pedido formal / educado"Could I have headphones, please?"

2. This / That (demonstrativos)

DemonstrativoUsoExemplo
thisPerto (near)"This is your boarding pass."
thatLonge / passado (far)"That was a long day."

3. Possessivos (my, your, his, her, our, their)

PossessivoSujeitoExemplo
myI"My suitcase is big."
youryou"Your departure gate is B12."
hishe"His name is Marco."
hershe"She puts her suitcase on the belt."

4. Where is...? / Where are...?

SingularPlural
"Where is my suitcase?""Where are the taxis?"
"Where is the departure gate?""Where are my bags?"

Erros comuns / Common mistakes:

Can I to have a receipt? ✗

Can I have a receipt? ✓

Após can/could, o verbo vem SEM "to".

Where is my luggages? ✗

Where is my luggage? ✓

"Luggage" é incontavel: nunca recebe "s".

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice
" I check in for my flight?"
"This is boarding pass."
" I have headphones, please?"
"What is the of your visit?"
" my suitcase?"
"Do you have anything to ?"

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Organize toda a jornada de Marlene no aeroporto na ordem correta:

?"On the plane, Marlene asks: Could I have headphones?"
?"She finds her suitcase at the baggage claim."
?"Marlene checks in and receives her boarding pass."
?"She takes a taxi and asks for a receipt."
?"The officer asks: What is the purpose of your visit?"
?"She goes to the departure gate B12."

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking
Can I check in for my flight to Rome?
Posso fazer o check-in do meu voo para Roma?
What is the purpose of your visit?
Qual é o motivo da sua visita?
Where is the baggage claim?
Onde é a esteira de bagagem?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
Você está no balcão de check-in e quer fazer o check-in do seu voo. O que você diz? (You are at the check-in counter and want to check in for your flight. What do you say?)
A "I want go plane."
B "Can I check in for my flight to Rome?"
C "Where is plane Rome?"
O oficial de imigração pergunta se você tem algo a declarar. Você não tem nada. O que você responde? (The immigration officer asks if you have anything to declare. You have nothing. What do you answer?)
A "I no have things."
B "Nothing declare."
C "No, nothing to declare."

Stage 5: Think and Speak

Reflection
Imagine que você vai contar para uma amiga toda a sua viagem de avião, do check-in até o hotel. Use o vocabulário das Aulas 6-9: boarding pass, carry-on, departure, headphones, purpose, declare, suitcase, receipt, taxi, address. Use can/could para pedidos e possessivos (my, your, his, her). (Imagine you are telling a friend about your entire airport journey, from check-in to the hotel.)

Sugestão: "I arrived at the airport. I checked in and got my boarding pass. My departure gate was B12. On the plane, I asked: Could I have headphones? At immigration, the officer asked the purpose of my visit. I said tourism. At the baggage claim, I found my suitcase. Then I took a taxi. I gave the driver my hotel address and asked for a receipt."

Survival Card -- Lesson 10

1"Can I check in for my flight?""Posso fazer o check-in do meu voo?"
2"This is my boarding pass.""Este é meu cartão de embarque."
3"What is the purpose of your visit?""Qual é o motivo da sua visita?"
4"Where is my suitcase?""Onde está minha mala?"
5"Can I have a receipt, please?""Posso ter um recibo, por favor?"
Aula 11 -- Pre-class

Hotel Check-in

Faça o check-in em um hotel com "I have a reservation" e aprenda o presente contínuo (I am staying, We are traveling). Vocabulário novo: reservation, lobby, key card, floor, elevator, view, sign.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para ouvir. / Listen to each word and read the example.

Reservation -- a room you booked before you arrive (reserva)
"I have a reservation for tonight." (Eu tenho uma reserva para hoje à noite.)
Lobby -- the big room at the entrance of a hotel (saguão)
"I am waiting in the lobby." (Estou esperando no saguão.)
Key card -- the small card that opens your room (cartão-chave)
"Here is your key card." (Aqui está o seu cartão-chave.)
Floor -- one level of the hotel (andar)
"My room is on the third floor." (Meu quarto é no terceiro andar.)
Elevator -- the machine that takes you up and down (elevador)
"The elevator is on the right." (O elevador fica à direita.)
View -- what you see from your window (vista)
"My room has a beautiful view." (Meu quarto tem uma vista linda.)
Sign -- to write your name on a form (assinar)
"Please sign here." (Por favor, assine aqui.)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Ligue cada palavra à sua tradução. / Match each word with its translation.

Reservation
Lobby
Key card
Floor
Elevator
View
Sign

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas. / Read the text and answer the questions.

Marlene arrives at the hotel. She walks into the lobby and goes to the front desk. "Hello, I have a reservation," she says. "I am staying for three nights." The receptionist smiles. "Welcome! Please sign here." Marlene is signing the form now. The receptionist gives her a key card. "Your room is on the third floor. The elevator is on the right. Your room has a beautiful view of the city." (Marlene chega ao hotel, diz que tem uma reserva e que está ficando por três noites.)

1. What does Marlene say at the front desk?
A "I have a reservation."
B "I want to buy this hotel."
C "Where is the airport?"
2. How many nights is she staying?
A One night.
B Three nights.
C Ten nights.
3. Where is her room?
A On the third floor.
B In the airport.
C In the lobby.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Present Continuous

GRAMMAR

Usamos o presente contínuo para ações que acontecem AGORA: am/is/are + verbo com -ing. Para a reserva, dizemos "I have a reservation" (não "I am have"). / We use the present continuous for actions happening NOW: am/is/are + verb-ing. For the booking, we say "I have a reservation" (not "I am have").

FormExample (EN)Tradução (PT)
Affirmative (+)I am staying here tonight.Estou ficando aqui hoje à noite.
Affirmative (+)She is checking in now.Ela está fazendo o check-in agora.
Negative (-)We are not leaving today.Não estamos saindo hoje.
Question (?)Are you staying two nights?Você vai ficar duas noites?

Key point / Ponto-chave: presente contínuo = am/is/are + verbo + -ing (I am staying, we are traveling). Para a reserva, use "I have a reservation" -- nunca "I am have a reservation".

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase completa. / Complete each sentence.

"I have a for tonight."
"I am for three nights."
"My room is on the third ."
"Here is your ."
"Please here."
"My room has a beautiful ."

Stage 2: Put the Check-in in Order

Order

Ouça primeiro, depois coloque os passos do check-in na ordem certa. / Listen first, then put the steps in order.

?The receptionist asks for your name and your passport.
?You arrive at the hotel and walk into the lobby.
?You take the elevator to your floor and find your room.
?You go to the front desk and say you have a reservation.
?You sign the form and take your key card.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e grave você mesma falando. / Listen to each phrase, then record yourself.

Hello, I have a reservation.
Olá, eu tenho uma reserva.
I am staying for three nights.
Vou ficar por três noites.
My room is on the third floor.
Meu quarto é no terceiro andar.
Where is the elevator, please?
Onde fica o elevador, por favor?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor frase para cada situação. / Choose the best sentence for each situation.

Você chega ao hotel. O que você diz no balcão? / You arrive at the hotel. What do you say?
A "Hello, I have a reservation."
B "Hello, I am have a reservation."
C "Hello, I reservation."
A receptionist pergunta quantas noites. / The receptionist asks how many nights.
A "I stay two nights yesterday."
B "I am staying for two nights."
C "Two nights is the elevator."
Você quer saber onde fica seu quarto. / You want to know where your room is.
A "Which floor is my room on?"
B "Which lobby is my reservation?"
C "My room is signing now."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta. Não há resposta certa ou errada. / Record yourself. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine you are checking into a hotel. Say it out loud: greet the receptionist, say "I have a reservation", say how many nights you are staying, and ask which floor your room is on. Use new words like reservation, floor, elevator and key card. (Imagine que você está fazendo o check-in em um hotel.)

Survival Card -- Lesson 11

1Hello, I have a reservation.Olá, eu tenho uma reserva.
2I am staying for three nights.Vou ficar por três noites.
3My room is on the third floor.Meu quarto é no terceiro andar.
4Where is the elevator, please?Onde fica o elevador, por favor?
5Here is my passport. I am ready to sign.Aqui está meu passaporte. Estou pronta para assinar.
Aula 12 -- Pre-class

Getting Around the City

Peça e dê dire ções na cidade com comandos (Go straight, Turn left, Cross) e preposições de lugar (next to, across from, on the corner). Vocabulário novo: corner, block, traffic light, crosswalk, straight, square, fountain.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para ouvir. / Listen to each word and read the example.

Corner -- the place where two streets meet (esquina)
"The cafe is on the corner." (O café fica na esquina.)
Block -- the part of a street between two corners (quarteirão)
"Walk two blocks and turn left." (Ande dois quarteirões e vire à esquerda.)
Traffic light -- the red, yellow, and green lights on the street (semáforo)
"Turn right at the traffic light." (Vire à direita no semáforo.)
Crosswalk -- the white lines where you cross the street (faixa de pedestres)
"Cross at the crosswalk." (Atravesse na faixa de pedestres.)
Straight -- forward, in the same direction (em frente, reto)
"Go straight for two blocks." (Siga em frente por dois quarteirões.)
Square -- a big open space in the center of a city (praça)
"The market is in the main square." (O mercado fica na praça principal.)
Fountain -- a structure in a square where water comes out (fonte, chafariz)
"Meet me at the fountain." (Encontre-me na fonte.)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Ligue cada palavra à sua tradução. / Match each word with its translation.

Corner
Block
Traffic light
Crosswalk
Straight
Square
Fountain

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas. / Read the text and answer the questions.

Marlene leaves the hotel and wants to find the main square. She stops a person on the street. "Excuse me, how do I get to the square?" The person smiles. "Go straight for two blocks. Then turn left at the traffic light. Cross the street at the crosswalk. The square is on your right." Marlene says thank you. The cafe is on the corner, across from the fountain. (Marlene sai do hotel, pergunta o caminho e segue em frente, vira à esquerda e atravessa na faixa.)

1. What does Marlene ask the person?
A "How do I get to the square?"
B "Where is my hotel room?"
C "What time is breakfast?"
2. How many blocks does she go straight?
A One block.
B Two blocks.
C Five blocks.
3. Where is the cafe?
A On the corner, across from the fountain.
B In the hotel lobby.
C Inside the airport.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Directions & Prepositions

GRAMMAR

Para dar uma direção, usamos um comando: começa com o verbo (Go, Turn, Cross), sem "you" e sem "to". Para dizer ONDE algo fica, usamos preposições de lugar: next to (ao lado de), across from (em frente a), between (entre), on the corner (na esquina). / To give a direction, use a command: start with the verb (Go, Turn, Cross), no "you" and no "to". To say WHERE something is, use prepositions: next to, across from, between, on the corner.

TypeExample (EN)Tradução (PT)
DirectionGo straight for two blocks.Siga em frente por dois quarteirões.
DirectionTurn left at the traffic light.Vire à esquerda no semáforo.
PlaceThe cafe is across from the fountain.O café fica em frente à fonte.
PlaceThe bank is next to the cafe.O banco fica ao lado do café.

Key point / Ponto-chave: uma direção é um comando -- comece com o verbo (Go straight, Turn left, Cross the street). Não use "you" nem "to" antes de left/right. Para dizer onde, use uma preposição: next to, across from, between, on the corner.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase completa. / Complete each sentence.

"Go for two blocks."
"Turn left at the ."
"Cross the street at the ."
"The cafe is on the ."
"The market is in the main ."
"Meet me at the ."

Stage 2: Put the Walk in Order

Order

Ouça primeiro, depois coloque os passos do caminho na ordem certa. / Listen first, then put the steps in order.

?You stop at the traffic light and cross at the crosswalk.
?You leave the hotel and turn right onto the main street.
?You find the cafe on the corner, across from the fountain.
?You walk straight for two blocks.
?You keep going straight until you reach the square.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e grave você mesma falando. / Listen to each phrase, then record yourself.

Excuse me, how do I get to the square?
Com licença, como eu chego à praça?
Go straight for two blocks.
Siga em frente por dois quarteirões.
Turn left at the traffic light.
Vire à esquerda no semáforo.
The cafe is across from the fountain.
O café fica em frente à fonte.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor frase para cada situação. / Choose the best sentence for each situation.

Você está perdida e quer chegar ao museu. / You are lost and want the museum.
A "Excuse me, how do I get to the museum?"
B "Excuse me, where you go museum?"
C "Museum me how please?"
Você dá uma direção para seguir em frente. / You tell someone to go forward.
A "You go to straight here."
B "Go straight for two blocks."
C "Straight is the corner now."
Você explica onde fica o banco. / You explain where the bank is.
A "The bank is next to the cafe."
B "The bank is turn left now."
C "The bank crosswalk corner."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta. Não há resposta certa ou errada. / Record yourself. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine you are lost in a new city. Say it out loud: stop a person, ask "How do I get to the square?", then give a short route back ("Go straight, turn left, cross at the crosswalk"). Use new words like corner, block, traffic light and fountain. (Imagine que você está perdida em uma cidade nova.)

Survival Card -- Lesson 12

1Excuse me, how do I get to the square?Com licença, como eu chego à praça?
2Go straight for two blocks.Siga em frente por dois quarteirões.
3Turn left at the traffic light.Vire à esquerda no semáforo.
4It is across from the fountain.Fica em frente à fonte.
5Thank you. I cross at the crosswalk.Obrigada. Eu atravesso na faixa de pedestres.
Aula 13 -- Pre-class

Money and Shopping

Faça compras e pergunte preços com "How much is this?" / "How much are these?" e aprenda this / that / these / those. Vocabulário novo: cash, change, wallet, expensive, cheap, discount, fitting room.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para ouvir. / Listen to each word and read the example.

Cash -- paper money and coins (dinheiro em espécie)
"I will pay with cash." (Vou pagar em dinheiro.)
Change -- the money you get back after you pay (troco)
"Here is your change." (Aqui está o seu troco.)
Wallet -- a small case where you keep your money (carteira)
"My wallet is in my bag." (Minha carteira está na minha bolsa.)
Expensive -- it costs a lot of money (caro)
"This bag is very expensive." (Esta bolsa é muito cara.)
Cheap -- it costs little money (barato)
"These postcards are cheap." (Estes cartões-postais são baratos.)
Discount -- money off the price (desconto)
"There is a discount today." (Hoje tem desconto.)
Fitting room -- a small room where you try on clothes (provádor)
"The fitting room is over there." (O provádor é ali.)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Ligue cada palavra à sua tradução. / Match each word with its translation.

Cash
Change
Wallet
Expensive
Cheap
Discount
Fitting room

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas. / Read the text and answer the questions.

Marlene is at a gift store. She sees a scarf and asks, "How much is this scarf?" The clerk says, "It is twenty dollars, but today there is a discount, so it is only fifteen." "That is cheap!" says Marlene. "And how much are those bags?" "Those bags are expensive -- forty dollars each." Marlene pays with cash. The clerk gives her the change and a receipt. (Marlene pergunta os preços, descobre que há um desconto e paga em dinheiro.)

1. What does Marlene ask the clerk first?
A "How much is this scarf?"
B "Where is the elevator?"
C "How do I get to the square?"
2. How much is the scarf with the discount?
A Twenty dollars.
B Fifteen dollars.
C Forty dollars.
3. How does Marlene pay?
A With cash.
B With her passport.
C With a key card.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- How Much & This/That/These/Those

GRAMMAR

Para perguntar o preço de UMA coisa: "How much is this/that?" Para MUITAS coisas: "How much are these/those?" this/these = perto de você; that/those = longe. / To ask the price of ONE thing: "How much is this/that?" For MANY things: "How much are these/those?" this/these = near you; that/those = far.

WordExample (EN)Tradução (PT)
this (one, near)How much is this scarf?Quanto custa este cachecol?
that (one, far)How much is that bag?Quanto custa aquela bolsa?
these (many, near)How much are these postcards?Quanto custam estes postais?
those (many, far)How much are those shoes?Quanto custam aqueles sapatos?

Key point / Ponto-chave: uma coisa = is (How much is this?). Muitas coisas = are (How much are these?). Perto = this/these; longe = that/those.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase completa. / Complete each sentence.

"How much is this ?"
"I will pay with ."
"Today there is a ."
"Here is your ."
"This bag is very ."
"The is over there."

Stage 2: Put the Purchase in Order

Order

Ouça primeiro, depois coloque os passos da compra na ordem certa. / Listen first, then put the steps in order.

?The clerk tells you the price and the discount.
?You walk into the store and look at the items.
?You pay with cash, take your change, and keep the receipt.
?You find a scarf you like and ask how much it is.
?You try it on in the fitting room to check the size.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e grave você mesma falando. / Listen to each phrase, then record yourself.

How much is this?
Quanto custa isto?
How much are these?
Quanto custam estes?
I will pay with cash, please.
Vou pagar em dinheiro, por favor.
Is there a discount today?
Tem desconto hoje?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor frase para cada situação. / Choose the best sentence for each situation.

Você aponta para um cachecol perto de você. / You point at one scarf near you.
A "How much is this scarf?"
B "How much are this scarf?"
C "How much this scarf?"
Você aponta para vários sapatos longe de você. / You point at many shoes far away.
A "How much is those shoes?"
B "How much are those shoes?"
C "How much this shoes?"
Você quer pagar em dinheiro. / You want to pay with cash.
A "I will pay with cash, please."
B "I pay with a cash now."
C "Cash is the discount please."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta. Não há resposta certa ou errada. / Record yourself. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine you are shopping for a gift. Say it out loud: ask "How much is this?", ask if there is a discount, say you will pay with cash, and ask for your change. Use new words like cash, discount, cheap and change. (Imagine que você está comprando um presente.)

Survival Card -- Lesson 13

1How much is this?Quanto custa isto?
2How much are these?Quanto custam estes?
3Is there a discount today?Tem desconto hoje?
4I will pay with cash, please.Vou pagar em dinheiro, por favor.
5Can I have my change and receipt?Pode me dar meu troco e o recibo?
Aula 14 -- Pre-class

At the Restaurant

Peça comida e bebida em um restaurante com "Could I have...?" e aprenda a / some / any (a coffee, some water, any dessert). Vocabulário novo: menu, waiter, dish, drink, dessert, bill, tip.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para ouvir. / Listen to each word and read the example.

Menu -- the list of food and prices (cardápio)
"Can I see the menu, please?" (Posso ver o cardápio, por favor?)
Waiter -- the person who serves you (garçom)
"The waiter is very friendly." (O garçom é muito simpático.)
Dish -- one kind of prepared food (prato)
"The fish dish is very good." (O prato de peixe é muito bom.)
Drink -- something you drink (bebida)
"Would you like a drink?" (Você gostaria de uma bebida?)
Dessert -- a sweet food after the main dish (sobremesa)
"Would you like some dessert?" (Você gostaria de uma sobremesa?)
Bill -- the paper that shows what you pay (conta)
"Could I have the bill, please?" (Pode trazer a conta, por favor?)
Tip -- extra money for the waiter (gorjeta)
"We leave a small tip." (Deixamos uma pequena gorjeta.)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Ligue cada palavra à sua tradução. / Match each word with its translation.

Menu
Waiter
Dish
Drink
Dessert
Bill
Tip

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas. / Read the text and answer the questions.

Marlene is at a restaurant. The waiter brings the menu. "Could I have the fish dish, please? And some water," says Marlene. "Would you like a drink or any dessert?" asks the waiter. "No dessert, thank you." At the end, Marlene says, "Could I have the bill, please?" She pays and leaves a small tip for the waiter. (Marlene pede um prato e água, recusa a sobremesa e pede a conta.)

1. What dish does Marlene order?
A The fish dish.
B A key card.
C A taxi.
2. Does she want any dessert?
A Yes, two desserts.
B No, she does not want any dessert.
C Yes, some cake and fruit.
3. What does she ask for at the end?
A The bill.
B The elevator.
C The boarding pass.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- a / some / any

GRAMMAR

Use a/an para uma coisa que dá para contar (a coffee). Use some em frases afirmativas com comida que não dá para contar (some water, some rice). Use any em perguntas e frases negativas (any dessert?, not any bread). / Use a/an for one countable thing; some in positive sentences for uncountable food; any in questions and negatives.

WordExample (EN)Tradução (PT)
a / an (count)I would like a coffee.Eu gostaria de um café.
some (+)Could I have some water?Pode me trazer um pouco de água?
any (?)Do you have any dessert?Vocês têm alguma sobremesa?
any (-)I do not want any dessert.Não quero nenhuma sobremesa.

Key point / Ponto-chave: a/an = uma coisa contável (a coffee). some = afirmativa, não contável (some water). any = pergunta/negativa (any dessert?). Nunca "a water" -- use "some water".

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase completa. / Complete each sentence.

"Could I see the , please?"
"Could I have the fish , please?"
"Could I have water?"
"Do you have dessert?"
"Could I have the , please?"
"We leave a small ."

Stage 2: Put the Dinner in Order

Order

Ouça primeiro, depois coloque os passos do jantar na ordem certa. / Listen first, then put the steps in order.

?You call the waiter and say, could I have the fish, please.
?You arrive at the restaurant and the waiter brings the menu.
?You ask for the bill, pay, and leave a small tip.
?You read the menu and choose a main dish.
?You order a drink and maybe some dessert.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e grave você mesma falando. / Listen to each phrase, then record yourself.

Could I see the menu, please?
Posso ver o cardápio, por favor?
Could I have the fish, please?
Pode me trazer o peixe, por favor?
Could I have some water?
Pode me trazer um pouco de água?
Could I have the bill, please?
Pode trazer a conta, por favor?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor frase para cada situação. / Choose the best sentence for each situation.

Você quer pedir o prato de frango. / You want to order the chicken dish.
A "Could I have the chicken, please?"
B "I want chicken give me now."
C "Chicken is the bill please."
Você quer água (não dá para contar). / You want water (uncountable).
A "Could I have a water?"
B "Could I have some water?"
C "Could I have any water now?"
Você pergunta se eles têm sobremesa. / You ask if they have dessert.
A "Do you have any dessert?"
B "Do you have a dessert tip?"
C "You have dessert is menu?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta. Não há resposta certa ou errada. / Record yourself. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine you are at a restaurant for dinner. Say it out loud: ask for the menu, order a dish and some water, say if you want any dessert, and ask for the bill. Use new words like menu, dish, drink and bill. (Imagine que você está jantando em um restaurante.)

Survival Card -- Lesson 14

1Could I see the menu, please?Posso ver o cardápio, por favor?
2Could I have the fish, please?Pode me trazer o peixe, por favor?
3Could I have some water?Pode me trazer um pouco de água?
4Do you have any dessert?Vocês têm alguma sobremesa?
5Could I have the bill, please?Pode trazer a conta, por favor?
Aula 15 -- Pre-class

Block 3 Review -- A Day Abroad

Revisão do bloco 3: hotel, direções, compras e restaurante em um dia completo. Revisão da gramática (presente contínuo, direções, how much, some/any) e das palavras-chave: reservation, elevator, corner, crosswalk, cash, discount, menu.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Revise cada palavra-chave do bloco. Toque em Listen para ouvir. / Review each key word from the block.

Reservation -- a room you booked before you arrive (reserva)
"I have a reservation." (Eu tenho uma reserva.)
Elevator -- the machine that takes you up and down (elevador)
"Where is the elevator?" (Onde fica o elevador?)
Corner -- the place where two streets meet (esquina)
"The cafe is on the corner." (O café fica na esquina.)
Crosswalk -- the white lines where you cross the street (faixa de pedestres)
"Cross at the crosswalk." (Atravesse na faixa de pedestres.)
Cash -- paper money and coins (dinheiro em espécie)
"I will pay with cash." (Vou pagar em dinheiro.)
Discount -- money off the price (desconto)
"There is a discount today." (Hoje tem desconto.)
Menu -- the list of food and prices (cardápio)
"Could I see the menu, please?" (Posso ver o cardápio, por favor?)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Ligue cada palavra à sua tradução. / Match each word with its translation.

Reservation
Elevator
Corner
Crosswalk
Cash
Discount
Menu

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas. / Read the text and answer the questions.

Marlene has a busy day. First, she says, "I have a reservation," and takes the elevator to her room. Then she goes out. "Go straight and turn left at the corner," a local says.
