Marlene crosses at the crosswalk. At the market, she asks, "How much is this?" There is a discount, so she pays with cash. For lunch, she reads the menu and orders some water. (Marlene faz o check-in, pede direções, faz compras e almoça.)

1. How does Marlene go to her room?
A She takes the elevator.
B She takes a taxi.
C She takes the menu.
2. How does she pay at the market?
A With her passport.
B With cash, because there is a discount.
C With the elevator.
3. What does she do for lunch?
A She reads the menu and orders some water.
B She goes back to the hotel.
C She asks for the boarding pass.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Block 3 Summary

GRAMMAR

Revisão dos quatro padrões do bloco: presente contínuo (I am staying) para agora; comandos (Go straight) para direções; How much is/are para preços; a/some/any para pedir comida. / Review of the four block patterns.

PatternExample (EN)Tradução (PT)
Present continuousI am staying here.Estou ficando aqui.
DirectionsGo straight, turn left.Siga em frente, vire à esquerda.
How muchHow much is this?Quanto custa isto?
a / some / anyCould I have some water?Pode me trazer um pouco de água?

Key point / Ponto-chave: um dia, quatro padrões. I am ...ing para agora; Go/Turn para direções; How much is/are para preços; a/some/any para pedir comida.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase completa. / Complete each sentence.

"I have a ."
"Cross at the ."
"The cafe is on the ."
"There is a today."
"I will pay with ."
"Could I see the , please?"

Stage 2: Put the Day in Order

Order

Ouça primeiro, depois coloque os passos do dia na ordem certa. / Listen first, then put the steps in order.

?You go shopping and ask how much things cost.
?You arrive and check in at the hotel with your reservation.
?You ask for the bill, pay, and leave a small tip.
?You go out and ask for directions to the square.
?You have lunch and order a dish and a drink.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e grave você mesma falando. / Listen to each phrase, then record yourself.

Hello, I have a reservation.
Olá, eu tenho uma reserva.
How do I get to the square?
Como eu chego à praça?
How much is this?
Quanto custa isto?
Could I have the bill, please?
Pode trazer a conta, por favor?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor frase para cada situação. / Choose the best sentence for each situation.

Você chega ao hotel. / You arrive at the hotel.
A "Hello, I have a reservation."
B "Hello, I am have a reservation."
C "Hello, how much is the menu?"
Você aponta para vários sapatos. / You point at many shoes.
A "How much is these shoes?"
B "How much are these shoes?"
C "How much this shoes?"
No restaurante, você quer água. / At the restaurant, you want water.
A "Could I have some water, please?"
B "Could I have a water, please?"
C "Water is the corner please."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta. Não há resposta certa ou errada. / Record yourself. There is no right or wrong answer.

Tell the story of one full day abroad. Say it out loud: check in at the hotel, ask for directions, go shopping and ask a price, and order dinner with a dish and a drink. Use words like reservation, corner, discount and menu. (Conte a história de um dia inteiro fora.)

Survival Card -- Lesson 15

1Hello, I have a reservation.Olá, eu tenho uma reserva.
2How do I get to the square?Como eu chego à praça?
3How much is this?Quanto custa isto?
4Could I see the menu, please?Posso ver o cardápio, por favor?
5Could I have the bill, please?Pode trazer a conta, por favor?
Aula 16 -- Pre-class

Sightseeing

Planeje um dia de passeio e diga o que quer visitar com "I want to visit..." e "I would like to see...". Vocabulário novo: museum, cathedral, ticket, tour, guide, monument, statue.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para ouvir. / Listen to each word and read the example.

Museum -- a building with art and history (museu)
"I want to visit the museum." (Eu quero visitar o museu.)
Cathedral -- a large and important old church (catedral)
"The cathedral is beautiful inside." (A catedral é linda por dentro.)
Ticket -- the paper you buy to enter a place (ingresso)
"I need a ticket for the museum." (Preciso de um ingresso para o museu.)
Tour -- an organized visit to see a place (passeio, tour)
"We will join a city tour." (Vamos fazer um passeio pela cidade.)
Guide -- the person who shows you around (guia)
"The guide tells the history." (O guia conta a história.)
Monument -- a big structure to remember a person or event (monumento)
"The monument is in the square." (O monumento fica na praça.)
Statue -- a figure of a person in stone or metal (estátua)
"The famous statue is in the museum." (A estátua famosa fica no museu.)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Ligue cada palavra à sua tradução. / Match each word with its translation.

Museum
Cathedral
Ticket
Tour
Guide
Monument
Statue

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas. / Read the text and answer the questions.

Marlene is planning a sightseeing day. "I want to visit the cathedral first," she says. "Then I would like to see the museum with the famous statue." She needs a ticket for the museum. In the afternoon, she wants to take a tour with a guide. The guide knows all the history. At the end, she will see the big monument in the square. (Marlene planeja o que quer visitar e ver, e precisa de um ingresso.)

1. What does Marlene want to visit first?
A The cathedral.
B The hotel.
C The airport.
2. What does she need for the museum?
A A passport.
B A ticket.
C A key card.
3. Who knows all the history?
A The guide.
B The waiter.
C The driver.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- I want to / I would like to

GRAMMAR

Para dizer o que você planeja, use I want to + verbo (mais informal) ou I would like to + verbo (mais educado). O verbo depois de to nunca muda (visit, see, take, buy). / To say your plan, use "I want to + verb" or, more politely, "I would like to + verb". The verb after "to" never changes.

FormExample (EN)Tradução (PT)
I want to (+)I want to visit the cathedral.Eu quero visitar a catedral.
I would like to (polite)I would like to see the statue.Eu gostaria de ver a estátua.
Question (?)Do you want to take a tour?Você quer fazer um passeio?
Negative (-)I do not want to wait.Não quero esperar.

Key point / Ponto-chave: sempre use to antes do verbo: "I want to visit", "I would like to see". Nunca "I want visit".

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase completa. / Complete each sentence.

"I want to visit the ."
"I would like to see the famous ."
"I need a for the museum."
"We want to take a ."
"The tells the history."
"The big is in the square."

Stage 2: Put the Tour in Order

Order

Ouça primeiro, depois coloque os passos do passeio na ordem certa. / Listen first, then put the steps in order.

?You visit the cathedral and stay quiet inside.
?You buy a ticket for the city tour.
?You take a photo at the big monument in the square.
?You meet the guide near the cathedral.
?You walk to the museum to see the famous statue.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e grave você mesma falando. / Listen to each phrase, then record yourself.

I want to visit the museum.
Eu quero visitar o museu.
I would like to see the statue.
Eu gostaria de ver a estátua.
I need a ticket, please.
Preciso de um ingresso, por favor.
Where is the monument?
Onde fica o monumento?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor frase para cada situação. / Choose the best sentence for each situation.

Você conta seu plano ao guia. / You tell the guide your plan.
A "I want to visit the cathedral."
B "I want visit the cathedral."
C "I visit want the cathedral."
Você pede de forma educada. / You ask politely.
A "I would like see the statue."
B "I would like to see the statue."
C "I like would the statue see."
Você precisa entrar no museu. / You need to enter the museum.
A "I need a ticket, please."
B "I need a guide museum tour."
C "Ticket I am the monument."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta. Não há resposta certa ou errada. / Record yourself. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine you are planning a sightseeing day in a famous city. Say it out loud: say what you want to visit, what you would like to see, and what you need (a ticket, a guide). Use new words like museum, cathedral, tour and monument. (Imagine que você está planejando um dia de passeio.)

Survival Card -- Lesson 16

1I want to visit the museum.Eu quero visitar o museu.
2I would like to see the statue.Eu gostaria de ver a estátua.
3I need a ticket, please.Preciso de um ingresso, por favor.
4We want to take a tour.Nós queremos fazer um passeio.
5Where is the monument?Onde fica o monumento?
Aula 17 -- Pre-class

Shopping for Style

Compre roupas e descreva cor e tamanho com adjetivos antes do substantivo (a small blue dress). Vocabulário novo: dress, shirt, shoes, jacket, size, color, try on.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para ouvir. / Listen to each word and read the example.

Dress -- a piece of clothing women wear, often to a party (vestido)
"I want a red dress." (Eu quero um vestido vermelho.)
Shirt -- a piece of clothing for the top of your body (camisa)
"The blue shirt is nice." (A camisa azul é bonita.)
Shoes -- what you wear on your feet (sapatos)
"These shoes are too big." (Estes sapatos estão grandes demais.)
Jacket -- a piece of clothing you wear when it is cold (jaqueta)
"I like the blue jacket." (Eu gosto da jaqueta azul.)
Size -- how big or small the clothing is (tamanho)
"What size do you wear?" (Que tamanho você usa?)
Color -- red, blue, green, black, and more (cor)
"What color is the dress?" (De que cor é o vestido?)
Try on -- to put on clothes to see if they fit (experimentar)
"Can I try on this dress?" (Posso experimentar este vestido?)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Ligue cada palavra à sua tradução. / Match each word with its translation.

Dress
Shirt
Shoes
Jacket
Size
Color
Try on

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas. / Read the text and answer the questions.

Marlene is at a clothing store. She wants a red dress for a party. The assistant asks, "What size are you?" Marlene is a medium. "Can I try on this red one?" she asks. The fitting room is at the back. The store also has a nice blue jacket and some small black shoes. Marlene likes the color of the dress. She will buy it. (Marlene quer um vestido vermelho, pergunta o tamanho e experimenta a roupa.)

1. What does Marlene want?
A A red dress.
B A blue ticket.
C A green menu.
2. What size is Marlene?
A Small.
B Medium.
C Large.
3. What else does the store have?
A A blue jacket and small black shoes.
B A hotel reservation.
C A city map.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Describing Clothes

GRAMMAR

Para descrever roupas, o adjetivo vem antes do substantivo: a red dress (nunca "a dress red"). Quando você usa os dois, o tamanho vem antes da cor: a small blue dress. / The describing word comes before the clothing; size comes before color.

OrderExample (EN)Tradução (PT)
color + clothinga red dressum vestido vermelho
color + clothingthe blue jacketa jaqueta azul
size + color + clothinga small blue dressum vestido azul pequeno
size + color + clothinglarge black shoessapatos pretos grandes

Key point / Ponto-chave: em inglês, o adjetivo vem antes do substantivo: a red dress. Ordem: tamanho + cor + roupa (a small blue dress). Nunca "a dress red".

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase completa. / Complete each sentence.

"I want a red ."
"What do you wear?"
"What is the dress?"
"Can I this jacket?"
"I like the blue ."
"These are too big."

Stage 2: Put the Shopping in Order

Order

Ouça primeiro, depois coloque os passos da compra na ordem certa. / Listen first, then put the steps in order.

?You ask the assistant for your size.
?You walk into the clothing store and look at the clothes.
?You decide you like the color and the size, and you buy it.
?You find a red dress that you like.
?You try it on in the fitting room.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e grave você mesma falando. / Listen to each phrase, then record yourself.

I want a red dress, please.
Eu quero um vestido vermelho, por favor.
What size do you have?
Que tamanhos vocês têm?
Can I try this on?
Posso experimentar isto?
Do you have this in a smaller size?
Vocês têm isto em um tamanho menor?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor frase para cada situação. / Choose the best sentence for each situation.

Você quer um vestido vermelho. / You want a red dress.
A "I want a red dress."
B "I want a dress red."
C "I red dress want."
Você descreve um vestido azul pequeno. / You describe a small blue dress.
A "a blue small dress"
B "a small blue dress"
C "a dress small blue"
Você quer experimentar a jaqueta. / You want to try the jacket on.
A "Can I try on this jacket?"
B "Can I try this on it jacket?"
C "Jacket I try size color?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta. Não há resposta certa ou errada. / Record yourself. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine you are shopping for clothes for a party. Say it out loud: say what you want with the color and size, ask your size, and ask to try it on. Use new words like dress, jacket, color and size. (Imagine que você está comprando roupas para uma festa.)

Survival Card -- Lesson 17

1I want a red dress, please.Eu quero um vestido vermelho, por favor.
2What size do you have?Que tamanhos vocês têm?
3Can I try this on?Posso experimentar isto?
4Do you have this in a smaller size?Vocês têm isto em um tamanho menor?
5I like the color. I will take it.Eu gosto da cor. Vou levar.
Aula 18 -- Pre-class

When Things Go Wrong

Peça ajuda quando se sentir mal: diga "I have a headache", "My stomach hurts" e "Can you help me?". Vocabulário novo: sick, headache, stomachache, fever, hurt, medicine, doctor.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para ouvir. / Listen to each word and read the example.

Sick -- not well; your body feels bad (doente, mal)
"I feel sick today." (Eu me sinto mal hoje.)
Headache -- a pain in your head (dor de cabeça)
"I have a headache." (Estou com dor de cabeça.)
Stomachache -- a pain in your stomach (dor de barriga)
"I have a stomachache." (Estou com dor de barriga.)
Fever -- when your body is too hot from illness (febre)
"I have a fever." (Estou com febre.)
Hurt -- to feel pain in a part of the body (doer)
"My head hurts." (Minha cabeça dói.)
Medicine -- what you take to get better (remédio)
"I need medicine for my headache." (Preciso de remédio para a dor de cabeça.)
Doctor -- a person who helps sick people (médico)
"I need to see a doctor." (Preciso ver um médico.)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Ligue cada palavra à sua tradução. / Match each word with its translation.

Sick
Headache
Stomachache
Fever
Hurt
Medicine
Doctor

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas. / Read the text and answer the questions.

Marlene does not feel well on her trip. "I feel sick," she says. "I have a headache, and my stomach hurts." She also has a fever. She goes to a pharmacy and asks: "Can you help me?" The pharmacist gives her some medicine and says: "Take one tablet two times a day. If you do not feel better, see a doctor." (Marlene se sente mal, pede ajuda e compra remédio.)

1. How does Marlene feel?
A Sick, with a headache.
B Happy and well.
C Tired but fine.
2. What does the pharmacist give her?
A A ticket.
B Some medicine.
C A map.
3. What should she do if she is not better?
A See a doctor.
B Take a tour.
C Buy a dress.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- I have a... / My ... hurts

GRAMMAR

Para uma doença, use I have a + palavra (headache, fever, stomachache). Para uma parte do corpo, use My + parte + hurts. Para pedir ajuda, diga Can you help me? / For an illness, use "I have a + word". For a body part, use "My + part + hurts". To ask for help, say "Can you help me?".

UseExample (EN)Tradução (PT)
Illness (+)I have a headache.Estou com dor de cabeça.
Body partMy stomach hurts.Minha barriga dói.
Ask for help (?)Can you help me?Você pode me ajudar?
Negative (-)I do not feel well.Não me sinto bem.

Key point / Ponto-chave: diga I have a headache (não "I am headache"), e My ... hurts para dor numa parte do corpo.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase completa. / Complete each sentence.

"I feel today."
"I have a ."
"My stomach ."
"I need for my fever."
"Can you me, please?"
"If I am very sick, I see a ."

Stage 2: Put the Pharmacy Visit in Order

Order

Ouça primeiro, depois coloque os passos na ordem certa. / Listen first, then put the steps in order.

?You tell the pharmacist your symptoms.
?You feel sick and you have a headache.
?You go back to your room to rest and drink water.
?You find a pharmacy near the hotel.
?You buy the medicine and ask how to take it.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e grave você mesma falando. / Listen to each phrase, then record yourself.

I feel sick. Can you help me?
Eu me sinto mal. Você pode me ajudar?
I have a headache.
Estou com dor de cabeça.
My stomach hurts.
Minha barriga dói.
I need to see a doctor.
Preciso ver um médico.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor frase para cada situação. / Choose the best sentence for each situation.

Você está com dor de cabeça. / You have a headache.
A "I have a headache."
B "I am headache."
C "I headache have a."
Sua barriga dói. / Your stomach hurts.
A "My stomach is hurt."
B "My stomach hurts."
C "I am stomach hurts."
Você precisa de ajuda. / You need help.
A "Can you help me, please?"
B "You can help me me?"
C "Help you can me please?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta. Não há resposta certa ou errada. / Record yourself. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine you feel sick on a trip. Say it out loud: say how you feel, say what hurts, and ask for help. Use new words like sick, headache, fever, medicine and doctor. (Imagine que você se sente mal numa viagem.)

Survival Card -- Lesson 18

1I feel sick. Can you help me?Eu me sinto mal. Você pode me ajudar?
2I have a headache.Estou com dor de cabeça.
3My stomach hurts.Minha barriga dói.
4I need medicine, please.Preciso de remédio, por favor.
5I need to see a doctor.Preciso ver um médico.
Aula 19 -- Pre-class

Calling for Help

Chame ajuda numa emergência: diga "This is an emergency", "Call the police!" e "There is an accident". Vocabulário novo: emergency, call, police, ambulance, accident, danger, hospital.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para ouvir. / Listen to each word and read the example.

Emergency -- a serious problem that needs help now (emergência)
"This is an emergency." (Isto é uma emergência.)
Call -- to telephone someone for help (ligar, chamar)
"Please call for help." (Por favor, chame ajuda.)
Police -- the people who keep the city safe (polícia)
"Call the police!" (Chame a polícia!)
Ambulance -- a vehicle that takes sick people to the hospital (ambulância)
"Call an ambulance!" (Chame uma ambulância!)
Accident -- a sudden bad event that hurts someone (acidente)
"There is an accident on the street." (Há um acidente na rua.)
Danger -- a situation that is not safe (perigo)
"I am in danger." (Estou em perigo.)
Hospital -- a place where doctors care for sick people (hospital)
"Take me to the hospital." (Leve-me ao hospital.)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Ligue cada palavra à sua tradução. / Match each word with its translation.

Emergency
Call
Police
Ambulance
Accident
Danger
Hospital

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas. / Read the text and answer the questions.

On her trip, Marlene sees a problem. "This is an emergency!" she says. There is an accident on the street, and a person is hurt. Marlene picks up the phone and calls for help. "Send an ambulance, please!" The operator says the police and the ambulance are on the way. They will take the person to the hospital. Marlene stays calm and stays on the phone. (Marlene vê um acidente, chama ajuda e dá o endereço.)

1. What does Marlene see?
A An accident.
B A museum.
C A restaurant.
2. What does she ask for?
A A ticket.
B An ambulance.
C A taxi.
3. Where will they take the hurt person?
A To the hospital.
B To the hotel.
C To the airport.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Call...! / There is...

GRAMMAR

Para uma ordem urgente, comece com o verbo: Call the police! Para dizer o que há, use There is a / an + palavra. Use an antes de som de vogal (an accident, an ambulance, an emergency). / For an urgent order, start with the verb. To say what there is, use "There is a / an + word".

UseExample (EN)Tradução (PT)
OrderCall the police!Chame a polícia!
There is (an)There is an accident.Há um acidente.
I needI need an ambulance.Preciso de uma ambulância.
Question (?)What is your address?Qual é o seu endereço?

Key point / Ponto-chave: comece a ordem com o verbo (Call the police!) e use an antes de som de vogal: an accident, an ambulance, an emergency.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase completa. / Complete each sentence.

"This is an ."
"Call the !"
"There is an on the street."
"Call an !"
"Take me to the ."
"I am in ."

Stage 2: Put the Emergency Call in Order

Order

Ouça primeiro, depois coloque os passos na ordem certa. / Listen first, then put the steps in order.

?You tell the operator what happened.
?There is an emergency and you feel in danger.
?You stay calm and wait for the police or the ambulance.
?You pick up the phone and call for help.
?You give your address and say if someone is hurt.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e grave você mesma falando. / Listen to each phrase, then record yourself.

This is an emergency. I need help!
Isto é uma emergência. Preciso de ajuda!
Call the police!
Chame a polícia!
There is an accident.
Há um acidente.
Call an ambulance!
Chame uma ambulância!

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor frase para cada situação. / Choose the best sentence for each situation.

Há um acidente na rua. / There is an accident on the street.
A "There is an accident."
B "There is a accident."
C "It is accident there."
Você manda chamar a polícia. / You tell someone to call the police.
A "You call the police you."
B "Call the police!"
C "Police you call the."
Uma pessoa está ferida. / A person is hurt.
A "Call an ambulance!"
B "Call a ambulance!"
C "Ambulance call you!"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta. Não há resposta certa ou errada. / Record yourself. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine there is an emergency on a trip. Say it out loud: call the operator, say what happened, and say what you need. Use new words like emergency, accident, ambulance and police. (Imagine que há uma emergência numa viagem.)

Survival Card -- Lesson 19

1This is an emergency. I need help!Isto é uma emergência. Preciso de ajuda!
2Call the police!Chame a polícia!
3Call an ambulance!Chame uma ambulância!
4There is an accident.Há um acidente.
5What is your address?Qual é o seu endereço?
Aula 20 -- Pre-class

Block 4 Review -- A Safe Trip

Revisão do Bloco 4: passeio, compras, saúde e emergências juntos. Pratique "I want to visit...", "I would like to try on...", "My ... hurts" e "Call for help!". Palavras revisadas: tour, ticket, dress, size, doctor, medicine, emergency.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Review

Vocabulary

Revisão das palavras do bloco. Ouça cada uma e leia o exemplo. / Review the block's words. Listen and read.

Tour -- an organized visit to see a place (passeio)
"We took a city tour." (Fizemos um passeio pela cidade.)
Ticket -- the paper you buy to enter a place (ingresso)
"I need a ticket for the museum." (Preciso de um ingresso para o museu.)
Dress -- a piece of clothing for a woman (vestido)
"I want to buy a blue dress." (Quero comprar um vestido azul.)
Size -- how big or small clothing is (tamanho)
"I need a bigger size." (Preciso de um tamanho maior.)
Doctor -- a person who helps sick people (médico)
"I need to see a doctor." (Preciso ver um médico.)
Medicine -- what you take to get better (remédio)
"I bought medicine at the pharmacy." (Comprei remédio na farmácia.)
Emergency -- a serious problem that needs help now (emergência)
"In an emergency, call for help." (Numa emergência, chame ajuda.)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Ligue cada palavra à sua tradução. / Match each word with its translation.

Tour
Ticket
Dress
Size
Doctor
Medicine
Emergency

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas. / Read the text and answer the questions.

Marlene has a busy day. In the morning, she says: "I want to take a tour." She buys a ticket for the museum. In the afternoon, she goes shopping: "I would like to try on this dress." She needs a bigger size. Later, she feels sick. "My head hurts," she says, so she buys medicine. At the hotel, she reads the emergency card, just in case. It is a full, safe day. (Marlene passeia, compra, cuida da saúde e fica segura.)

1. What does Marlene want to do in the morning?
A Take a tour.
B Sleep all day.
C Go to the airport.
2. What does she buy for her headache?
A A ticket.
B Medicine.
C A dress.
3. What does she read at the hotel?
A The emergency card.
B A menu.
C A boarding pass.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- The Block Together

GRAMMAR

Revisão das quatro estruturas do bloco: I want to + verbo (planos), I would like to + verbo (educado), My ... hurts (dor), e o verbo no início para ordens urgentes (Call...!). / Review of the four block patterns.

AulaExample (EN)Tradução (PT)
SightseeingI want to visit the museum.Quero visitar o museu.
ShoppingI would like to try on this dress.Eu gostaria de provar este vestido.
HealthMy head hurts.Minha cabeça dói.
EmergencyCall an ambulance!Chame uma ambulância!

Key point / Ponto-chave: com estas quatro estruturas, você planeja, compra, pede ajuda e chama socorro -- tudo numa viagem segura.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase completa. / Complete each sentence.

"We took a city ."
"I would like to try on this ."
"I need a bigger ."
"I bought at the pharmacy."
"In an , call for help."
"If I am very sick, I see a ."

Stage 2: Put the Day in Order

Order

Ouça primeiro, depois coloque o dia na ordem certa. / Listen first, then put the day in order.

?You go shopping and try on a dress in your size.
?In the morning, you take a tour and visit the cathedral.
?You rest in your hotel room, safe and happy.
?You buy a ticket and see the famous statue.
?You feel sick, so you buy medicine at the pharmacy.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e grave você mesma falando. / Listen to each phrase, then record yourself.

I want to visit the museum.
Eu quero visitar o museu.
I would like to try on this dress.
Eu gostaria de provar este vestido.
I have a headache.
Estou com dor de cabeça.
This is an emergency. Call for help!
Isto é uma emergência. Chame ajuda!

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor frase para cada situação. / Choose the best sentence for each situation.

Você conta seu plano de passeio. / You tell your sightseeing plan.
A "I want to visit the museum."
B "I want visit the museum."
C "I am visit the museum."
Você está com dor de cabeça. / You have a headache.
A "I am headache."
B "I have a headache."
C "My headache hurts a."
Há um acidente. / There is an accident.
A "Call an ambulance!"
B "Call a ambulance!"
C "You call ambulance you!"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta. Não há resposta certa ou errada. / Record yourself. There is no right or wrong answer.

Tell the story of one full, safe day on a trip. Say what you want to visit, what you would like to buy, what you do if you feel sick, and what you do in an emergency. Mix the whole block! (Conte a história de um dia completo e seguro numa viagem.)

Survival Card -- Lesson 20

1I want to visit the museum.Eu quero visitar o museu.
2I would like to try on this dress.Eu gostaria de provar este vestido.
3I have a headache.Estou com dor de cabeça.
4Can you help me, please?Você pode me ajudar, por favor?
5This is an emergency. Call for help!Isto é uma emergência. Chame ajuda!

Parabéns, Marlene!

Você completou 100% do Pre-class!
