Materiais Complementares -- Aula 1: Who Is Marlene?

Tema da aula: apresentação pessoal, viagens, imóveis, hobbies. Vocabulário: introduce, travel, property, manage, hobby, nationality, currently, independent.

Filme
Eat Pray Love (2010) -- Primeiros 20 minutos

Liz (Julia Roberts) se apresenta: "My name is Liz Gilbert", "I'm a writer", "I love to travel". Ela descreve a nacionalidade, os hobbies e o sonho de ser independente. Vocabulário idêntico ao da Aula 1: introduce, travel, hobby, nationality, independent.

Dica: Anote 3 frases onde Liz se apresenta. Compare com: "Let me introduce myself. I am Marlene. I love to travel to Italy."

Podcast
Slow English Podcast -- Episode: "Introducing Yourself in English"

Episódio de 10 minutos onde a apresentadora se introduz passo a passo: nome, nacionalidade, onde mora, hobbies, trabalho. Fala lentamente e repete cada estrutura. Conecta com "introduce", "nationality", "currently" e "manage" da Aula 1.

Dica: Ouça e pause após cada frase. Repita usando seus dados: "I am Marlene. I currently live in São Paulo. I manage properties."

Listen on Spotify ↗
YouTube
English with Lucy -- "How to Introduce Yourself in English (Complete Guide)" (7 min)

Lucy mostra como se apresentar passo a passo: nome, de onde é, o que faz, hobbies. Exatamente o padrão da Aula 1: "I am Marlene", "I manage properties", "My hobby is tennis", "I love to travel".

Dica: Pratique o modelo da Lucy com seus dados reais. Grave-se falando e compare com o áudio da aula.

Watch on YouTube ↗

Materiais Complementares -- Aula 2: My World in Words

Tema da aula: rotina diária, advérbios de frequência, Present Simple. Vocabulário: routine, wake up, breakfast, exercise, usually, always, sometimes, schedule.

Filme
The Intern (2015) -- Primeiros 15 minutos

Ben (Robert De Niro) descreve a rotina dele após a aposentadoria: "I wake up at 6", "I always have breakfast", "I usually exercise in the morning", "My schedule is very busy". Usa exatamente o vocabulário da Aula 2: routine, wake up, breakfast, exercise, usually, always.

Dica: Anote 3 frases da rotina do Ben. Compare com a sua: "I wake up at six", "I always drink coffee", "I sometimes play tennis".

Podcast
6 Minute English (BBC) -- Episode: "Morning routines"

Episódio específico sobre rotinas matinais: "I always wake up early", "I usually have breakfast at 7". Os apresentadores usam "usually", "always" e "sometimes" o tempo todo. Conecta diretamente com os advérbios de frequência da Aula 2.

Dica: Ouça e conte quantas vezes ouve "usually", "always" ou "sometimes". Depois descreva SUA rotina matinal usando esses advérbios.

Listen on Spotify ↗
YouTube
English Addict with Mr Duncan -- "My Daily Routine in English" (Adverbs of Frequency)

Mr Duncan ensina como descrever a rotina usando "I always...", "I usually...", "I sometimes...", "My schedule is...". Mostra cada advérbio com exemplos práticos. Estrutura idêntica à Aula 2.

Dica: Repita cada frase do vídeo com SUA rotina: "I usually go to the office at eight", "I always drink coffee in the morning", "Sometimes I play tennis after work".

Watch on YouTube ↗

Materiais Complementares -- Aula 3: Numbers That Matter

Tema da aula: números 1-100, horas, preços no aeroporto. Vocabulário: number, clock, price, gate, hundred, half, quarter, flight.

Filme
The Terminal (2004) -- Primeiros 20 minutos

Viktor (Tom Hanks) chega ao aeroporto JFK e precisa lidar com números o tempo todo: gate numbers ("Gate 67"), horários de voo ("Flight at 9:45"), preços de comida ("That's three dollars and fifty cents") e mudanças de portão. Vocabulário idêntico ao da Aula 3: number, gate, flight, price, clock, half, hundred.

Dica: Anote todos os números que você ouvir nos primeiros 20 minutos: gates, horários, preços. Compare com: "Gate 42", "It's half past nine", "The price is fifteen dollars".

Podcast
6 Minute English (BBC) -- Episode: "The fear of numbers"

Neil e Sam falam sobre números e por que muitas pessoas têm dificuldade com eles. Usam "number", "hundred", "half" e "price" em contextos reais. Conecta diretamente com o vocabulário da Aula 3: numbers 1-100, preços e horas.

Dica: Ouça e anote cada número mencionado. Depois pratique dizendo: "The price is twenty-five", "It's a quarter past three", "Gate number forty-two".

Listen on Spotify ↗
YouTube
Beginners English Lesson -- "How to Ask and Tell TIME in English" (12 min)

Lição completa sobre como dizer as horas: "It's half past nine", "It's a quarter to three", "It's ten o'clock". Ensina "half", "quarter" e "o'clock" com exemplos práticos. Estrutura idêntica à Aula 3: clock, half, quarter, number.

Dica: Pause o vídeo a cada exemplo e repita. Pratique com horários reais da sua rotina: "I wake up at half past six", "My flight is at a quarter to nine".

Watch on YouTube ↗

Materiais Complementares -- Aula 4: Describing People and Places

Tema da aula: adjetivos para descrever cidades, hotéis e pessoas. Vocabulário: beautiful, famous, crowded, quiet, modern, ancient, friendly, delicious. Gramática: there is / there are.

Filme
Under the Tuscan Sun (2003) -- Primeiros 30 minutos

Diane Lane descreve cidades e paisagens da Itália usando adjetivos: "It's beautiful", "The town is quiet and ancient", "The people are so friendly". Vocabulário idêntico ao da Aula 4: beautiful, ancient, quiet, friendly, crowded, famous. Preste atenção em como ela usa "there is" e "there are" para descrever o que existe na cidade.

Dica: Assista com áudio em inglês + legendas em português. Anote todos os adjetivos que ela usa para descrever lugares e pessoas. Compare com: "The city is beautiful", "There are many ancient buildings".

Podcast
6 Minute English (BBC) -- Episode: "Is tourism harmful?"

Sam e Rob discutem como o turismo afeta cidades europeias como Roma e Bruges. Usam adjetivos como "crowded", "beautiful", "famous" e "ancient" para descrever lugares turísticos. Conecta diretamente com o vocabulário da Aula 4 e com "there is / there are" em contexto real.

Dica: Ouça e anote cada adjetivo usado para descrever cidades. Depois pratique: "Rome is beautiful and ancient", "There are too many tourists", "The streets are crowded".

Listen on Spotify ↗
YouTube
Describing Places in English You Want to Travel -- Adjectives for Places (10 min)

Vídeo com adjetivos para descrever lugares de viagem: "beautiful", "crowded", "quiet", "modern", "ancient", "famous". Ensina como usar adjetivos com "there is" e "there are" em frases completas. Estrutura idêntica à Aula 4.

Dica: Pause o vídeo a cada exemplo e repita. Tente descrever uma cidade que você conhece: "São Paulo is modern and crowded", "There are many famous restaurants".

Watch on YouTube ↗

Materiais Complementares -- Aula 5: Block 1 Review

Revisão do Bloco 1: apresentação pessoal, rotina, números, descrição de lugares. Pratique tudo junto!

Filme
Lost in Translation (2003) -- First 30 minutes

Bill Murray chega em Tóquio, se apresenta no hotel, descreve a cidade e navega pela rotina matinal. Usa todas as habilidades do Bloco 1: apresentação pessoal ("I'm Bob"), rotina ("I usually wake up early"), adjetivos para lugares ("It's quiet", "The city is beautiful"), números e horários. Preste atenção em como ele usa "there is" e "there are" ao descrever o hotel.

Dica: Assista com áudio em inglês + legendas em português. Anote todas as frases em que Bob se apresenta, descreve lugares ou fala de sua rotina. Compare com o vocabulário das Aulas 1 a 4.

Podcast
6 Minute English (BBC) -- Show completo: Episódios sobre viagem e cultura

Cada episódio de 6 minutos traz uma conversa natural entre dois apresentadores sobre temas do dia a dia, incluindo viagens, cidades e cultura. Vocabulário simples e estruturas como "I usually...", "There is/are...", "It's beautiful/crowded". Perfeito para revisar todo o Bloco 1 em contexto real.

Dica: Escolha 2-3 episódios sobre viagem ou cidades. Ouça e anote as frases que usam vocabulário das Aulas 1 a 4: apresentações, rotina, números e descrição de lugares.

Listen on Spotify ↗
YouTube
10 Simple English Dialogues for Travel (A1-A2 level) -- English Conversation for Beginners

Vídeo com 10 diálogos simples de viagem: apresentação no aeroporto, check-in no hotel, pedir informações, descrever lugares e falar sobre rotina. Cobre todas as estruturas do Bloco 1: "My name is...", "I usually...", "There is/are...", "How much is...?". Nível A1-A2, perfeito para revisão.

Dica: Pause após cada diálogo e tente repetir as frases. Identifique qual aula (1 a 4) cada diálogo revisa.

Watch on YouTube ↗

Materiais Complementares -- Aula 6: At the Airport

Tema: check-in no aeroporto, pedidos educados. Vocabulário: passport, luggage, check-in, boarding pass, aisle, window, carry-on, departure. Gramática: Could I...? / I would like...

Filme
Up in the Air (2009) -- First 15 minutes

George Clooney faz check-in no aeroporto, passa pela segurança e embarca com naturalidade. Preste atenção nas frases educadas que ele usa: "I'd like an aisle seat", "Could I get an upgrade?", "Here's my boarding pass". Vocabulário da aula em ação: passport, luggage, carry-on, boarding pass, aisle.

Dica: Assista com áudio em inglês + legendas em português. Anote todas as frases com "Could I...?" e "I would like..." que aparecem nas cenas de aeroporto.

Podcast
The English Made Simple Podcast -- Episode 336: "10 Travel Phrases to Survive an Airport"

Episódio com 10 frases práticas para sobreviver no aeroporto em inglês, do check-in até a esteira de bagagens. Cobre exatamente o vocabulário da aula: boarding pass, luggage, carry-on, departure gate. Ouça como usar "Could I...?" e "I'd like..." em situações reais de aeroporto.

Dica: Ouça uma vez sem pausar, depois ouça de novo anotando as 10 frases. Tente repetir cada uma em voz alta.

Listen on Spotify ↗
YouTube
Everyday English for ESL -- "Airport Check-in" (English Conversation Practice)

Vídeo com diálogos reais de check-in no aeroporto: como pedir assento na janela ou corredor ("I'd like a window seat"), entregar o passaporte ("Here's my passport"), despachar bagagem ("I have one carry-on and one checked bag"). Estruturas "Could I...?" e "I would like..." aparecem em cada conversa.

Dica: Pause após cada diálogo e repita as frases do passageiro. Tente trocar as informações pelas suas (seu nome, seu destino).

Watch on YouTube ↗

Materiais Complementares -- Aula 7: On the Plane

Tema: pedidos a bordo, segurança e serviço de refeição. Vocabulário: seatbelt, overhead, tray table, blanket, turbulence, landing, snack, headphones. Gramática: Can I have...? / Could you...?

Filme
Catch Me If You Can (2002) -- Airport and plane scenes

Leonardo DiCaprio interage com comissários de bordo e passageiros em diversas cenas a bordo. Preste atenção nas frases educadas usadas dentro do avião: "Can I have a blanket?", "Could you bring me a drink?", "Please fasten your seatbelt." Vocabulário da aula em ação: seatbelt, overhead, tray table, snack, headphones.

Dica: Assista com áudio em inglês + legendas em português. Anote todas as frases com "Can I have...?" e "Could you...?" que aparecem nas cenas dentro do avião.

Podcast
Speak English Now Podcast -- #047: "English Airport Travel | Vocabulary Speaking Practice"

Episódio focado em vocabulário e frases práticas para viagens de avião em inglês. Cobre situações a bordo como pedir itens à tripulação, entender instruções de segurança e fazer pedidos durante o serviço de refeição. Estruturas "Can I have...?" e "Could you...?" aparecem em contextos reais de voo.

Dica: Ouça uma vez sem pausar, depois ouça de novo repetindo as frases em voz alta. Tente usar "Can I have a blanket?" e "Could you bring me some headphones?" como prática.

Listen on Spotify ↗
YouTube
Simple English for Travel -- "On the Plane" (Daily English Conversations)

Vídeo com diálogos reais dentro do avião: como pedir um cobertor ("Can I have a blanket?"), solicitar fones de ouvido ("Could you bring me headphones?"), entender avisos de turbulência e instruções sobre cinto de segurança. Estruturas "Can I have...?" e "Could you...?" aparecem em cada conversa com a tripulação.

Dica: Pause após cada diálogo e repita as frases do passageiro. Tente trocar os pedidos pelos seus próprios: "Can I have a snack?", "Could you open the overhead bin?"

Watch on YouTube ↗

Materiais Complementares -- Aula 8: Immigration and Customs

Tema: imigração, alfândega e perguntas com do/does. Vocabulário: customs, declaration, purpose, vacation, declare, stamp, return, citizen. Gramática: Do/Does questions.

Filme
The Terminal (2004) -- Immigration scenes at JFK Airport

Tom Hanks interpreta Viktor, um viajante preso na imigração do aeroporto JFK em Nova York. Preste atenção nas perguntas do oficial: "What is the purpose of your visit?", "Do you have a return ticket?", "Do you have anything to declare?" Vocabulário da aula em ação: customs, purpose, declaration, stamp, citizen.

Dica: Assista com áudio em inglês + legendas em português. Anote todas as perguntas que o oficial de imigração faz com "do" e "does".

Podcast
All Ears English -- AEE 2297: "Traveling Soon? English Phrases for the Airport"

Episódio com frases práticas para o aeroporto em inglês, incluindo como responder perguntas na imigração: "What is the purpose of your visit?", "How long are you staying?", "Do you have anything to declare?" Estruturas com do/does aparecem em contextos reais de viagem.

Dica: Ouça uma vez sem pausar, depois ouça de novo repetindo as frases. Pratique responder as perguntas de imigração em voz alta.

Listen on Spotify ↗
YouTube
Airport English: Customs, Immigration and Passport Control!

Vídeo com perguntas e respostas reais na imigração do aeroporto: "What is the purpose of your visit?", "How long do you plan to stay?", "Do you have anything to declare?" Mostra exatamente o que esperar ao chegar em um país de língua inglesa. Estruturas do/does aparecem em cada pergunta.

Dica: Pause após cada pergunta do oficial e tente responder antes de ver a resposta no vídeo. Pratique as respostas usando "Yes, I do" e "No, I do not."

Watch on YouTube ↗

Materiais Complementares -- Aula 9: Baggage and Ground Transport

Tema da aula: Baggage and Ground Transport. Preste atenção em como as pessoas descrevem bagagens, pedem táxis e usam possessivos (my, your, his, her).

Filme
Home Alone 2: Lost in New York (1992) -- Airport scenes

Kevin se perde no aeroporto, lida com bagagem e pega um táxi sozinho em Nova York. Preste atenção quando ele diz "Where is my family?" e fala com o taxista: "I need to go to the Plaza Hotel." Estruturas com possessivos e "Where is...?" aparecem o tempo todo.

Dica: Assista com áudio em inglês + legendas em inglês. Anote todas as vezes que Kevin usa "my" e "Where is."

Podcast
All Ears English -- AEE 1317: "6 Travel English Phrases Native Speakers Use"

Episódio com frases práticas de viagem que falantes nativos usam, incluindo como descrever bagagem, pedir táxi e dar endereços. Estruturas com possessivos como "my luggage," "your address" aparecem naturalmente.

Dica: Ouça uma vez sem pausar, depois ouça de novo repetindo as frases. Pratique dizer "Where is my suitcase?" e "My address is..." em voz alta.

Listen on Spotify ↗
YouTube
Lost Luggage?! English You Need at Baggage Claim

Vídeo com frases exatas para reportar bagagem perdida no aeroporto em inglês: "My suitcase is missing," "What does your bag look like?", "Where is the baggage claim?" Vocabulário e possessivos da Aula 9 aparecem em situações reais.

Dica: Pause após cada frase e repita em voz alta. Pratique descrever SUA mala usando possessivos: "My suitcase is black and large."

Watch on YouTube ↗

Aula 10: Block 2 Review

Tema da aula: Revisão completa do Block 2 (aeroporto, avião, imigração, bagagem, táxi). Preste atenção em como as estruturas "Could I," "I would like," "Can I have" e possessivos aparecem em contextos reais.

Filme
Love Actually (2003) -- Opening Airport Scene

O filme abre no aeroporto de Heathrow com cenas reais de pessoas se encontrando no setor de desembarque. Preste atenção nas frases de saudação, nos diálogos curtos e no vocabulário de aeroporto: "arrivals," "gate," "flight." Toda a energia do Block 2 está nessa cena!

Dica: Assista com áudio em inglês + legendas em português. Anote todas as palavras de aeroporto que você reconhece das aulas.

Podcast
Easy English Podcast -- "Travelling in Airports: Vocabulary, Useful Phrases, Real-life Travel Scenarios"

Episódio completo sobre inglês no aeroporto: check-in, segurança, alfândega, bagagem perdida. Cobre TODAS as estruturas do Block 2 em situações reais. Você vai ouvir "Could I have," "Where is," possessivos como "my passport," "your boarding pass."

Dica: Ouça uma vez inteiro, depois ouça de novo pausando e repetindo cada frase. Tente fazer o check-in completo em voz alta junto com o áudio.

Listen on Spotify ↗
YouTube
Airport English -- At the Airport -- Spoken English Lesson

Vídeo completo com frases para TODAS as etapas do aeroporto: check-in, security, boarding, immigration, baggage claim, taxi. Estruturas como "I would like to check in," "Could I see your passport," "Where is gate 14" aparecem exatamente como nas aulas.

Dica: Assista com legendas em inglês. Para cada situação, pause e faça o diálogo completo em voz alta como se você estivesse no aeroporto.

Watch on YouTube ↗

Materiais Complementares -- Aula 10: Block 2 Review

Tema da aula: revisão completa da jornada no aeroporto. Preste atenção em pedidos educados (Could I...?, I would like...), perguntas com do/does, possessivos e vocabulário de viagem.

Filme
The Terminal (2004) -- Airport scenes

Viktor (Tom Hanks) vive inteiramente no aeroporto: faz check-in, passa pela imigração, lida com bagagem e pede comida. Preste atenção em frases como "I would like...", "Could I...?", "Where is...?" e como ele se comunica com pedidos educados.

Dica: Assista com áudio em inglês + legendas em português. Anote cada vez que Viktor faz um pedido educado.

Podcast
All Ears English -- "How to Be Polite in English at an Airport"

Episódio sobre como ser educado em inglês no aeroporto. Cobre "Could I...?", "I would like...", "Can I have...?" em situações reais de check-in, bordo e imigração.

Dica: Ouça uma vez completo, depois repita as frases em voz alta.

Listen on Spotify ↗
YouTube
English with Lucy -- "Airport English: Everything You Need to Know"

Vídeo completo cobrindo TODA a jornada no aeroporto: check-in, boarding, avião, imigração, bagagem e táxi.

Dica: Pause após cada situação e tente falar as frases ANTES da Lucy.

Watch on YouTube ↗

Aula 11 -- Hotel Check-in

Tema da aula: fazer o check-in em um hotel ("I have a reservation") e usar o presente contínuo (I am staying, We are traveling). Reforço fora de aula -- marque como concluído ao terminar.

Film
"The Grand Budapest Hotel" -- the front desk scenes

A beautiful film set in a hotel. Watch the check-in and reception scenes and listen for hotel words: reservation, lobby, key, floor, and room.

Dica: assista com legenda em inglês e anote toda palavra de hotel que você ouvir (reservation, lobby, floor, room).

Podcast
"Travel English" -- At the Hotel (beginner episode)

A slow, clear podcast for beginners. Listen to a traveler check into a hotel and practice the phrase "I have a reservation" and present continuous (I am staying here for two nights).

Dica: ouça duas vezes. Na segunda, repita em voz alta "I have a reservation" e "I am staying for two nights".

YouTube
"Checking in at a Hotel -- English Conversation" (English with subtitles)

A short, simple video of a real hotel check-in. A guest says "I have a reservation," gives a name, signs the form, and takes the key card to the elevator and floor.

Dica: pause depois de cada fala do receptionist e repita. Foque nas frases "I am staying for..." e "Here is your key card."

Aula 12 -- Getting Around the City

Tema da aula: pedir e dar direções na cidade (Go straight, Turn left, Cross at the crosswalk) e usar preposições de lugar (next to, across from, on the corner). Reforço fora de aula -- marque como concluído ao terminar.

Film
"Emily in Paris" -- the street scenes

A light series set in a beautiful city. Watch the street and square scenes and listen for direction words: go straight, turn, corner, square, and fountain.

Dica: assista com legenda em inglês e anote toda palavra de direção que você ouvir (turn left, go straight, corner).

Podcast
"Travel English" -- Asking for Directions (beginner episode)

A slow, clear podcast for beginners. Listen to a traveler ask for directions on the street and practice "How do I get to...?" and commands like "Go straight" and "Turn left".

Dica: ouça duas vezes. Na segunda, repita em voz alta "How do I get to the square?" e "Turn left at the corner".

YouTube
"Asking for Directions in English -- Conversation" (English with subtitles)

A short, simple video of a real street conversation. A tourist says "Excuse me, how do I get to...?", and a local answers with go straight, turn left, the crosswalk, and the corner.

Dica: pause depois de cada direção e repita. Foque nas frases "Go straight for two blocks" e "It is across from the fountain".

Aula 13 -- Money and Shopping

Tema da aula: fazer compras e perguntar preços ("How much is this?" / "How much are these?") e usar this / that / these / those. Reforço fora de aula -- marque como concluído ao terminar.

Film
"Confessions of a Shopaholic" -- the store scenes

A fun film about shopping. Watch the store and checkout scenes and listen for shopping words: how much, price, discount, cash, and change.

Dica: assista com legenda em inglês e anote toda palavra de compra que você ouvir (how much, discount, cash, change).

Podcast
"Travel English" -- Shopping & Money (beginner episode)

A slow, clear podcast for beginners. Listen to a traveler buy a gift and practice "How much is this?" and "How much are these?" with this, that, these, and those.

Dica: ouça duas vezes. Na segunda, repita em voz alta "How much is this?" e "I will pay with cash".

YouTube
"Shopping in English -- Conversation at a Store" (English with subtitles)

A short, simple video of a real shopping conversation. A customer asks "How much is this?", learns about a discount, pays with cash, and takes the change and receipt.

Dica: pause depois de cada fala do clerk e repita. Foque nas frases "How much are these?" e "Is there a discount today?".

Aula 14 -- At the Restaurant

Tema da aula: pedir comida e bebida em um restaurante ("Could I have...?") e usar a / some / any. Reforço fora de aula -- marque como concluído ao terminar.

Series
"Chef's Table" -- the restaurant scenes

A beautiful series about food and restaurants. Watch the dining scenes and listen for restaurant words: menu, dish, waiter, drink, and dessert.

Dica: assista com legenda em inglês e anote toda palavra de restaurante que você ouvir (menu, dish, dessert, bill).

Podcast
"Travel English" -- At the Restaurant (beginner episode)

A slow, clear podcast for beginners. Listen to a traveler order dinner and practice "Could I have...?" with a, some, and any (a coffee, some water, any dessert).

Dica: ouça duas vezes. Na segunda, repita em voz alta "Could I have the fish, please?" e "Could I have some water?".

YouTube
"Ordering Food in a Restaurant -- English Conversation" (English with subtitles)

A short, simple video of a real restaurant order. A customer reads the menu, says "Could I have the fish, please?", asks for some water, and asks for the bill at the end.

Dica: pause depois de cada fala do waiter e repita. Foque nas frases "Could I have...?" e "Do you have any dessert?".

Aula 15 -- Block 3 Review -- A Day Abroad

Tema da aula: revisão do bloco 3 (hotel, direções, compras e restaurante) em um dia completo. Reforço fora de aula -- marque como concluído ao terminar.

Series
"Somebody Feed Phil" -- a full day in one city

A warm travel series. Watch one full episode in a city and listen for everything from this block: the hotel, the streets, shopping, and restaurants.

Dica: assista com legenda em inglês e anote uma palavra de cada tema (hotel, direções, compras, comida).

Podcast
"Travel English" -- A Day in the City (review episode)

A slow, clear podcast for beginners. Follow a traveler through a full day and review all the phrases from the block: check-in, directions, shopping, and ordering food.

Dica: ouça duas vezes. Na segunda, repita em voz alta uma frase de cada parte do dia.

YouTube
"A Day as a Tourist -- English for Travel" (English with subtitles)

A short video that follows a tourist through a whole day: hotel check-in, asking directions, shopping, and dinner. Great review of everything in this block.

Dica: pause em cada parte do dia e repita a frase-chave (I have a reservation / How do I get to / How much is this / Could I have the bill).

Aula 16 -- Sightseeing

Tema da aula: planejar um dia de passeio e dizer o que quer visitar ("I want to visit...", "I would like to see..."). Reforço fora de aula -- marque como concluído ao terminar.

Series
"Rick Steves' Europe" -- a guided city tour episode

A classic travel series. Watch a guided tour of a European city and listen for sightseeing words: museum, cathedral, monument, statue, and guide.

Dica: assista com legenda em inglês e anote toda palavra de passeio que você ouvir (museum, cathedral, tour, guide).

Podcast
"Travel English" -- Sightseeing & Attractions (beginner episode)

A slow, clear podcast for beginners. Listen to a traveler plan a day of sightseeing and practice "I want to visit..." and "I would like to see...".

Dica: ouça duas vezes. Na segunda, repita em voz alta "I want to visit the museum" e "I would like to see the statue".

YouTube
"Sightseeing in English -- Conversation at a Tourist Site" (English with subtitles)

A short, simple video of a real sightseeing conversation. A tourist says "I want to visit the cathedral", buys a ticket, and joins a tour with a guide.

Dica: pause depois de cada fala do guia e repita. Foque nas frases "I want to visit..." e "I need a ticket, please".

Aula 17 -- Shopping for Style

Tema da aula: comprar roupas e descrever cor e tamanho (a small blue dress). Reforço fora de aula -- marque como concluído ao terminar.

Series
"The Bold Type" -- the clothing and fashion scenes

A series about fashion and style. Watch the store and styling scenes and listen for clothes words: dress, shirt, jacket, color, and size.

Dica: assista com legenda em inglês e anote toda palavra de roupa e cor que você ouvir (dress, jacket, blue, red, size).

Podcast
"Travel English" -- Shopping for Clothes (beginner episode)

A slow, clear podcast for beginners. Listen to a traveler shop for clothes and practice describing color and size, like "a small blue dress".

Dica: ouça duas vezes. Na segunda, repita em voz alta "I want a red dress" e "Can I try this on?".

YouTube
"Shopping for Clothes in English -- Conversation" (English with subtitles)

A short, simple video of a real clothing store conversation. A customer asks for a color and size, tries clothes on, and decides what to buy.

Dica: pause depois de cada fala do assistant e repita. Foque nas frases "I want a red dress" e "What size do you have?".

Aula 18 -- When Things Go Wrong

Tema da aula: pedir ajuda quando se sente mal ("I have a headache", "My stomach hurts", "Can you help me?"). Reforço fora de aula -- marque como concluído ao terminar.

Series
"The Good Doctor" -- a simple hospital scene

A hospital drama. Watch a short scene where a patient describes how they feel. Listen for health words: sick, headache, fever, hurt, medicine, doctor.

Dica: assista com legenda em inglês e anote toda palavra de saúde que você ouvir (sick, headache, fever, doctor).

Podcast
"Travel English" -- At the Pharmacy & Feeling Sick (beginner episode)

A slow, clear podcast for beginners. Listen to a traveler talk to a pharmacist and practice "I have a headache" and "Can you help me?".

Dica: ouça duas vezes. Na segunda, repita em voz alta "I have a headache" e "My stomach hurts".

YouTube
"At the Pharmacy in English -- Conversation Practice" (English with subtitles)

A short, simple video of a real pharmacy conversation. A traveler says "I feel sick", asks for help, and buys medicine for a headache.

Dica: pause depois de cada fala do farmacêutico e repita. Foque nas frases "Can you help me?" e "I need medicine, please".

Aula 19 -- Calling for Help

Tema da aula: chamar ajuda numa emergência ("This is an emergency", "Call the police!", "There is an accident"). Reforço fora de aula -- marque como concluído ao terminar.

Series
"9-1-1" -- a simple emergency call scene

A drama about emergency calls. Watch a short scene where someone calls for help. Listen for words: emergency, accident, ambulance, police, hospital.

Dica: assista com legenda em inglês e anote toda palavra de emergência que você ouvir (emergency, police, ambulance).

Podcast
"Travel English" -- Emergencies & Calling for Help (beginner episode)

A slow, clear podcast for beginners. Listen to a traveler call an operator and practice "This is an emergency" and "Call an ambulance!".

Dica: ouça duas vezes. Na segunda, repita em voz alta "Call the police!" e "There is an accident".

YouTube
"Emergency English -- How to Call for Help" (English with subtitles)

A short, simple video of a real emergency call. A traveler says "This is an emergency", gives an address, and asks for an ambulance.

Dica: pause depois de cada fala do operador e repita. Foque nas frases "This is an emergency" e "What is your address?".

Aula 20 -- Block 4 Review -- A Safe Trip

Tema da aula: revisão do Bloco 4 (passeio, compras, saúde e emergências) numa viagem segura. Reforço fora de aula -- marque como concluído ao terminar.

Series
"An Idiot Abroad" -- a full travel day episode

A fun travel series. Watch one full day of a traveler in a new city and listen for words from the whole block: tour, ticket, dress, medicine, emergency.

Dica: assista com legenda em inglês e marque qual palavra do bloco você ouve em cada cena.

Podcast
"Travel English" -- A Full Day Abroad (review episode)

A slow, clear podcast for beginners. Listen to a traveler describe a full day -- sightseeing, shopping, and getting help -- and review all the block's phrases.

Dica: ouça duas vezes. Na segunda, repita as frases-chave "I want to visit...", "I would like to buy..." e "I have a headache".

YouTube
"A Day of Travel in English -- Full Conversation Review" (English with subtitles)

A short video that follows a traveler through a full day: a tour, shopping for a dress, and a visit to the pharmacy. A perfect review of the whole block.

Dica: pause depois de cada cena e diga em voz alta o que você faria. Use as quatro estruturas do bloco.

The Dream Packing Words The Code Getting There Practice Your Turn Wrap-Up
Lesson 1 -- Travel English

Marlene's
First Words

A1+ -- 60 minutes -- Alumni by Better

Chapter 1: The Dream

Tell Me About Your Travels

"What country do you want to visit?"
"Do you like to travel alone or with friends?"
"What is your favorite city in Brazil?"
Chapter 1: The Dream

Today's Goal

"By the end of this lesson, you will be able to introduce yourself confidently in English."

Chapter 2

Packing Words

Your vocabulary for today

Chapter 2: Packing Words

Key Vocabulary

To present yourself to someone new
Introduce
apresentar
"Let me introduce myself. I am Marlene."
To go to different places and countries
Travel
viajar
"I love to travel to Italy and Turkey."
A building or house that you own
Property
imóvel
"I manage properties in São Paulo."
To be in charge and organize things
Manage
gerenciar
"She manages her own business."
0 / 4 words revealed
Chapter 2: Packing Words

More Words

Something you love to do for fun
Hobby
passatempo
"Tennis is my favorite hobby."
The country where you were born
Nationality
nacionalidade
"My nationality is Brazilian."
Right now, at this moment in your life
Currently
atualmente
"I currently live in São Paulo."
You do things by yourself, no help needed
Independent
independente
"I want to be an independent traveler."
0 / 4 words revealed
Chapter 2: Packing Words

Repeat After Me

Chapter 2: Packing Words

Complete with the Right Word

I love to ___travel to Italy and Turkey.
Let me ___introduce myself. I am Marlene.
I ___manage properties in São Paulo.
I want to be an ___independent traveler.
Chapter 3

The Code

Discover the grammar pattern

Chapter 3: The Code

Look at the Pattern

"I am from Brazil."
"She is a teacher."
"We are travelers."
"He is from England."

"What do the highlighted words have in common?"

Chapter 3: The Code

The Rule: To Be

SubjectTo BeExample
IamI am Marlene.
YouareYou are a traveler.
He / She / ItisShe is a teacher.
WeareWe are travelers.
TheyareThey are at the airport.
Chapter 3: The Code

Common Mistake

"I is from Brazil."
"I am from Brazil."
"She are a manager."
"She is a manager."
Chapter 3: The Code

Fill in: am, is, are

I ___am currently learning English.
It ___is a beautiful day.
They ___are at the airport.
He ___is from England.
We ___are travelers.
Chapter 4

Getting There

Words in context

Chapter 4: Getting There

Meeting at the Airport

Marlene meets Tom at the gate. Listen to their conversation.

T

Tom

M

Marlene

Chapter 4: Getting There

Meeting at the Airport

T
"Hi there! Are you traveling to Italy too?"
M
"Yes! I am Marlene. I am from Brazil."
T
"Nice to meet you, Marlene! I am Tom. I am from England."
M
"Nice to meet you, Tom! This is my first trip alone."
T
"That is wonderful! What do you do in Brazil?"
M
"I manage properties in São Paulo. And you?"
T
"I am a teacher. I travel every year. It is my hobby!"
M
"I love traveling! I am currently learning English for my trips."
Chapter 4: Getting There

Did You Understand?

Where is Tom from?
Tom is from England.
What does Tom do?
Tom is a teacher.
What is Tom's hobby?
Traveling is Tom's hobby. He travels every year.
Chapter 4: Getting There

Airport Announcement

Listen carefully. What information do you hear?

0:000:00
Speed
What flight number was announced?
Flight AL0050.
What gate should passengers go to?
Gate B7.
Chapter 4: Getting There

What Did You Hear?

Where is the flight going?
The flight is going to Rome (FCO).
What time does boarding begin?
Boarding begins now at Gate B7.
Chapter 4: Getting There

Boarding Pass

BOARDING PASS

Alumni Airlines
GRUFCO
MARLENE LANDUCCI
AL0050
June 15, 2026
12A
B7
São Paulo
Rome
Where is Marlene flying to?
She is flying to Rome (FCO).
What is her seat number?
Her seat is 12A.
What gate should she go to?
She should go to Gate B7.
Chapter 5

Practice

Time to use what you learned

Chapter 5: Practice

Quick Fire

0 / 6
Question 1 of 6
Someone at the airport asks: "What is your name?" Introduce yourself.
"My name is Marlene. I am from Brazil."
Chapter 5: Practice

Keep Going!

"Use the Quick Fire card on the previous slide to continue through all 6 questions."

Navigate back with the left arrow if needed.

Chapter 5: Practice

Almost There!

"Complete all 6 Quick Fire questions for a perfect score!"

Chapter 5: Practice

Spot the Error

"I is from Brazil."

"I am from Brazil."

"She are a property manager."

"She is a property manager."

"They is traveling to Italy."

"They are traveling to Italy."

"He am a teacher from England."

"He is a teacher from England."

0 / 4 errors found
Chapter 5: Practice

Say It Out Loud

1. Say your full name and where you are from.
"My name is Marlene Landucci. I am from Brazil."
2. Tell someone about your work.
"I manage properties in São Paulo."
3. Say your nationality and your hobby.
"I am Brazilian. My favorite hobby is tennis."
4. Tell someone about your travel plans.
"I am traveling to Italy and Turkey."
5. Ask someone their name and where they are from.
"What is your name? Where are you from?"
6. Say why you are learning English.
"I am currently learning English for my trips."
Chapter 5: Practice

Build the Sentence

am / I / Brazil / from
"I am from Brazil."
is / hobby / tennis / my / favorite
"Tennis is my favorite hobby."
properties / manage / São Paulo / I / in
"I manage properties in São Paulo."
currently / am / English / I / learning
"I am currently learning English."
an / to / be / independent / want / I / traveler
"I want to be an independent traveler."
Chapter 5: Practice

Listen and Understand

Listen to someone introducing themselves.

0:000:00
Speed
What is the person's name?
Listen for the name in the audio.
Where are they from?
Listen for the country or city.
Chapter 6

Your Turn

From guided to free

Chapter 6: Your Turn

Role-Play: Guided

Airport Gate -- Meeting Tom

You are at the airport gate. Tom says "Hi! What is your name?" Introduce yourself using the keywords below.

Marlene Brazil properties São Paulo tennis travel
Chapter 6: Your Turn

Role-Play: Semi-Free

Hotel Lobby -- New Friend

You are at the hotel in Rome. Someone asks about you. Tell them about yourself.

Marlene Brazilian independent
Chapter 6: Your Turn

Role-Play: Free

Café in Rome -- Solo

You are at a café in Rome. A tourist sits next to you and says "Hi!" Start a conversation. Introduce yourself and ask about them.

Chapter 6: Your Turn

Record Your Introduction

Record a 60-second self-introduction. Include your name, nationality, work, hobby, and travel plans.
Chapter 7

Wrap-Up

What we accomplished today

Chapter 7: Wrap-Up

Let's Review

Teacher: Type the errors you noticed during class.

Self-Assessment

What I Learned

I can introduce myself in English.
I can say my name, nationality and profession.
I can use "am", "is" and "are" correctly.
I can ask someone "Where are you from?"
I can talk about my hobbies.
I can say where I currently live.
I can understand a simple introduction dialogue.
I can start a conversation with a stranger.

Badge Earned: First Words

You completed Lesson 1!

Lesson Complete

Day 1 -- Complete.

Next lesson: My World in Words -- Daily Routines

Lesson 2 -- Travel English

Marlene's
Morning

A1+ -- 60 minutes -- Alumni by Better

Chapter 1: The Dream

Your Daily Life

"What is the first thing you do in the morning?"
"Do you have breakfast at home or at a cafe?"
"When you travel, is your routine different?"
Chapter 1: The Dream

Today's Goal

"By the end of this lesson, you will be able to describe your daily routine using Present Simple and adverbs of frequency."

Chapter 2

Packing Words

Your vocabulary for today

Chapter 2: Packing Words

Key Vocabulary

The things you do every day, in the same order
Routine
rotina
"I have a morning routine."
To stop sleeping and open your eyes
Wake up
acordar
"I wake up at six in the morning."
The first meal of the day, in the morning
Breakfast
café da manhã
"I have breakfast before work."
Physical activity to stay healthy and strong
Exercise
exercício
"I exercise three times a week."
0 / 4 words revealed
Chapter 2: Packing Words

More Words

Most of the time, about 80%
Usually
geralmente
"I usually go to the office at eight."
Every time, 100% of the time
Always
sempre
"I always drink coffee in the morning."
Not always, not never. In the middle
Sometimes
às vezes
"Sometimes I play tennis after work."
A plan that shows your activities and times
Schedule
agenda
"My schedule is very busy."
0 / 4 words revealed
Chapter 2: Packing Words

Repeat After Me

Chapter 2: Packing Words

Complete with the Right Word

I have a morning ___routine. I do the same things every day.
I ___wake up at six and drink coffee.
I ___always drink coffee. Every single day!
My ___schedule is very busy. I have no free time!
Chapter 3

The Code

Discover the grammar pattern

Chapter 3: The Code

Look at the Pattern

"I always wake up at six."
"She usually wakes up at seven."
"We sometimes exercise in the morning."
"He never skips breakfast."

"What happens to the verb with She and He?"

"Where do the adverbs (always, usually, sometimes) go?"

Chapter 3: The Code

The Rule: Present Simple

SubjectVerbExample
I / You / We / Theyverb (base)I wake up at six.
He / She / Itverb + s/esShe wakes up at six.

Adverb position: Subject + adverb + verb

"I always drink coffee." | "She usually wakes up early."

Chapter 3: The Code

Common Mistakes

"She wake up at six."
"She wakes up at six."
"I drink always coffee."
"I always drink coffee."
Chapter 3: The Code

Grammar Practice

Word bank:haswalktravelsskip
She always ___has breakfast at seven.
I usually ___walk my dogs in the morning.
He sometimes ___travels for work.
They never ___skip breakfast.
Chapter 4

Getting There

A conversation about daily routines

Chapter 4: Getting There

At the Cafe in Rome

M
"Hi Lisa! So nice to see you here in Rome!"
L
"Marlene! What a surprise! How are you?"
M
"I am great! I always wake up early when I travel."
L
"Me too! What is your morning routine here?"
M
"I usually have breakfast at the hotel. Then I walk around the city."
L
"That sounds lovely! I sometimes skip breakfast and go straight to a cafe."
M
"Do you exercise when you travel?"
L
"Not always. But I always walk a lot! My schedule is very busy with sightseeing."
Chapter 4: Getting There

Did You Understand?

1. Does Lisa always have breakfast at the hotel?
No. She sometimes skips breakfast and goes to a cafe.
2. Does Lisa exercise when she travels?
Not always. But she always walks a lot.
3. What is Lisa's schedule busy with?
Her schedule is busy with sightseeing.
Chapter 4: Getting There

Listening 1

Listen to someone's daily routine

0:000:00
Speed
1. What time does she wake up?
She wakes up at six.
2. Does she always exercise?
She usually exercises in the morning.
Chapter 4: Getting There

Marlene's Daily Planner

DAILY SCHEDULE

Marlene L.
6:00Wake up + coffeealways
7:00Breakfastalways
8:00Check propertiesusually
12:00Lunchalways
15:00Tennissometimes
22:00Go to bednever late
Chapter 4: Getting There

Describe the Planner

Tell me about your morning — what do you ALWAYS do?
"I always wake up at six. I always have breakfast at seven."
What do you USUALLY do at 8 AM?
"I usually check my properties in the morning."
What do you SOMETIMES do in the afternoon?
"I sometimes play tennis in the afternoon."
Chapter 4: Getting There

Listening 2

A traveler's morning schedule

0:000:00
Speed
1. What time does the traveler have breakfast?
He usually has breakfast at eight.
2. Does he always exercise on vacation?
No, he sometimes exercises. He usually walks.
Chapter 4: Getting There

Build the Sentence

always / I / coffee / drink / morning / in the
"I always drink coffee in the morning."
wakes up / she / at six / usually
"She usually wakes up at six."
sometimes / tennis / I / play / afternoon / in the
"I sometimes play tennis in the afternoon."
Also correct: "Sometimes I play tennis in the afternoon."
Chapter 5

More Practice

Test your knowledge

Chapter 5: Practice

Quick Fire

0 / 6
Question 1 of 6
Someone asks: "What time do you wake up?" Answer with an adverb of frequency.
"I usually wake up at six in the morning."
Chapter 5: Practice

Spot the Error

"She wake up at six every day."
"She wakes up at six every day."
"I drink always coffee in the morning."
"I always drink coffee in the morning."
"He exercise three times a week."
"He exercises three times a week."
"They has breakfast at seven."
"They have breakfast at seven."
0 / 4 errors found
Chapter 5: Practice

Oral Practice

You are at a hotel in Rome. The receptionist asks: "What time do you need breakfast?" Answer using "usually".
"I usually have breakfast at seven, please."
A new friend asks: "Do you exercise every day?" Answer honestly using "sometimes" or "always".
"I sometimes play tennis. I always walk a lot!"
Tell your friend about your busy schedule. Use at least 3 adverbs of frequency.
"My schedule is busy. I always wake up early. I usually check my properties. I sometimes play tennis."
Chapter 5: Practice

Complete the Sentences

Word bank:isneverusuallywakes up
I ___usually walk my dogs in the morning.
She ___wakes up at six every day.
My schedule ___is very busy but I love it.
They ___never skip breakfast.
Chapter 5: Practice

True or False?

Based on Marlene's daily planner

DAILY SCHEDULE
6:00Wake upalways
7:00Breakfastalways
8:00Propertiesusually
12:00Lunchalways
15:00Tennissometimes
22:00Bednever late
1. "Marlene sometimes wakes up at six."
False. She ALWAYS wakes up at six.
2. "Marlene usually checks her properties at eight."
True!
3. "Marlene always plays tennis at three."
False. She SOMETIMES plays tennis.
4. "Marlene never goes to bed late."
True!
Chapter 5: Practice

Frequency Scale

0%50%100%
never sometimes usually always
Breakfast: "I _____ have breakfast." Where on the scale?
Example: "I always have breakfast." (100%)
Tennis: "I _____ play tennis." Where on the scale?
Example: "I sometimes play tennis." (40%)
Chapter 5: Practice

Say It Right

"I always have coffee in the morning."
"She usually wakes up at six."
"I sometimes play tennis in the afternoon."
Chapter 5: Practice

Your Routine

"Tell me 3 things about YOUR daily routine."

1. "I _______ ..."
2. "I _______ ..."
3. "I _______ ..."
Chapter 6

Your Turn

From guided to free

Chapter 6: Your Turn

Role-Play 1: Guided

Hotel Breakfast

You are at a hotel in Rome. The receptionist asks about your morning preferences. Tell her about your routine.

7 AMalwayscoffeebreakfast
Chapter 6: Your Turn

Role-Play 2: Semi-Free

Cafe Conversation

You are having coffee with your friend Lisa in Rome. She asks about your typical day in Brazil. Tell her your routine.

6 AMusuallypropertiessometimes tennisschedule
Chapter 6: Your Turn

Role-Play 3: Free

Hotel in Turkey

You are at a hotel in Istanbul, Turkey. Another guest at breakfast asks you: "What is your daily routine like?" Speak freely about your typical day.

Chapter 6: Your Turn

Record Your Routine

Describe your daily routine in at least 5 sentences. Use always, usually, sometimes, or never.
Chapter 7: Wrap-Up

Let's Review

Teacher: Type the errors you noticed during class.

Self-Assessment

What I Learned

I can describe my daily routine in English.
I can use Present Simple correctly (I wake / She wakes).
I can use always, usually, sometimes, and never.
I know where to put the adverb (before the verb).
I can understand a conversation about daily routines.
I can talk about my schedule with a friend.

Badge Earned: Morning Routine

You completed Lesson 2!

Next lesson: Numbers That Matter

Lesson 3 -- Travel English

Marlene's
Number Day

A1+ -- 60 minutes -- Alumni by Better

Chapter 1: The Dream

Numbers in Your Life

"How many countries have you visited?"
"What is your lucky number?"
"What time do you usually wake up?"
Chapter 1: The Dream

Today's Goal

"By the end of this lesson, you will be able to tell time, ask prices, and understand numbers at the airport."

Chapter 2

Packing Words

Your vocabulary for today

Chapter 2: Packing Words

Key Vocabulary

A word that tells you "how many"
Number
número
"What is your gate number?"
It shows you what time it is
Clock
relógio
"Look at the clock. It is two thirty."
How much money something costs
Price
preço
"The price is twenty-five dollars."
The door at the airport where you board the plane
Gate
portão de embarque
"My flight is at gate fourteen."
0 / 4 words revealed
Chapter 2: Packing Words

More Words

The number after ninety-nine
Hundred
cem / cento
"One hundred passengers are on this flight."
Fifty percent. One of two equal parts
Half
metade / meia
"It is half past two."
Twenty-five percent. One of four equal parts
Quarter
quarto / quinze minutos
"It is a quarter to three."
A trip on an airplane from one place to another
Flight
voo
"My flight is at three o'clock."
0 / 4 words revealed
Chapter 2: Packing Words

Repeat After Me

Chapter 2: Packing Words

Complete the Sentence

My _______ is at three o'clock.
flight
Look at the _______. What time is it?
clock
The _______ of a coffee is three dollars.
price
Please go to _______ fourteen for boarding.
gate
Chapter 3

The Code

Patterns for time and prices

Chapter 3: The Code

Discover the Pattern

"It is three o'clock."
"It is half past two."
"It is a quarter to three."
"The price is twenty-five dollars."

"What patterns do you see for telling time?"

Chapter 3: The Code

The Rule

PatternWhenExample
__ o'clockExact hour (:00)It is three o'clock. (3:00)
half past __Thirty minutes (:30)It is half past two. (2:30)
a quarter past __Fifteen minutes (:15)It is a quarter past four. (4:15)
a quarter to __Forty-five minutes (:45)It is a quarter to three. (2:45)
How much is __?Asking the priceHow much is a coffee?
Chapter 3: The Code

Common Mistakes

"It is fifteen to three."
"It is a quarter to three."
"The price is twenty-five."
"The price is twenty-five dollars."
Chapter 3: The Code

Practice the Pattern

It is _______ past four. (4:30)
half
My flight is at a _______ to six. (5:45)
quarter
How _______ is this souvenir?
much
The _______ is gate twelve.
number
Chapter 4

Getting There

Numbers at the airport

Chapter 4: Getting There

At the Info Desk

M
"Excuse me, what gate is flight 472?"
S
"Gate fourteen. Boarding at two thirty."
M
"What time is it now?"
S
"It is one forty-five. You have forty-five minutes."
M
"How much is a coffee?"
S
"Three dollars and fifty cents."
M
"Here you go. Thank you!"
S
"You are welcome. Have a nice flight!"
Chapter 4: Getting There

Did You Understand?

1. What gate did the staff say?
Gate fourteen.
2. How much is the coffee?
Three dollars and fifty cents.
3. How much time does Marlene have before boarding?
Forty-five minutes.
Chapter 4: Getting There

Listening 1

Listen to an airport announcement

0:000:00
Speed
1. What gate is the flight?
Listen for the gate number in the announcement.
2. What time is boarding?
Listen for the boarding time.
Chapter 4: Getting There

Marlene's Boarding Pass

BOARDING PASS
Alumni Airlines
Passenger
LANDUCCI, MARLENE
Seat
18A
From
GRU
São Paulo
To
FCO
Rome
Flight
AL 472
Gate
14
Boarding
2:30 PM
Chapter 4: Getting There

Listening 2

Shopping at the duty free

0:000:00
Speed
1. How much is the souvenir?
Listen for the price.
2. How many does the customer buy?
Listen for the quantity.
Chapter 4: Getting There

Spot the Error

"It is fifteen to three."

"It is a quarter to three."

"The price is twenty-five dollar."

"The price is twenty-five dollars."

"My flight is in three o'clock."

"My flight is at three o'clock."

"How many is this?"

"How much is this?"

0 / 4 errors found
Chapter 4: Getting There

Say the Time

3:00
three o'clock
4:15
a quarter past four
5:30
half past five
6:45
a quarter to seven
10:00
ten o'clock
12:30
half past twelve
Chapter 4: Getting There

Tell Me

"What time is your English class?"
"What time do you usually have lunch?"
"How much is a coffee in Brazil?"
Chapter 5

More Practice

Use numbers in real situations

Chapter 5: Practice

Quick Fire

0 / 6
Question 1 of 6
Someone asks: "What time is your flight?" Your flight is at 3:00.
"My flight is at three o'clock."
Chapter 5: Practice

Say the Price

$3.50
three dollars and fifty cents
$12.00
twelve dollars
$25.99
twenty-five dollars and ninety-nine cents
$50.00
fifty dollars
Chapter 5: Practice

What Time Is It?

Your alarm rings. It shows 7:00.
It is seven o'clock.
You check your watch. It shows 8:15.
It is a quarter past eight.
The airport screen shows 11:30.
It is half past eleven.
Your phone shows 1:45.
It is a quarter to two.
Chapter 5: Practice

Ask and Answer

1. Water bottle -- $2.00. Ask the price and say it.
"How much is the water?" "It is two dollars."
2. Sandwich -- $8.50. Ask the price and say it.
"How much is the sandwich?" "It is eight dollars and fifty cents."
3. Magazine -- $5.75. Ask the price and say it.
"How much is the magazine?" "It is five dollars and seventy-five cents."
4. Chocolate -- $4.25. Ask the price and say it.
"How much is the chocolate?" "It is four dollars and twenty-five cents."
Chapter 5: Practice

About You

"Say your phone number in English."
"What time do you wake up on weekends?"
"How much is dinner at your favorite restaurant?"
Chapter 6

Your Turn

From guided to free

Chapter 6: Your Turn

Role-Play: Guided

Airport Info Desk

You are at the airport info desk. Ask about your flight. Use the keywords below to build your questions.

gate 14 2:30 flight 472 What time...?
Chapter 6: Your Turn

Role-Play: Semi-Free

Duty Free Shop

You are at the duty free shop. Ask prices and buy souvenirs for your family.

$25 souvenir two $50
Chapter 6: Your Turn

Role-Play: Free

Café in Rome -- Solo

You are at a cafe in Rome. Order food and drinks, ask prices, and pay. The waiter says: "Welcome! What can I get you?"

Chapter 6: Your Turn

Describe Your Airport Day

Tell your teacher about your experience at the airport. Use numbers, times, and prices. Include your gate, flight time, and something you bought.
Chapter 7

Wrap-Up

What we accomplished today

Chapter 7: Wrap-Up

Let's Review

Teacher: Type the errors you noticed during class.

Self-Assessment

What I Learned

I can tell time in English.
I can ask "How much is this?"
I can say numbers 1 to 100.
I can understand airport announcements.
I can say prices in dollars and cents.
I can find my gate and flight time.

Badge Earned: Numbers Champion

You completed Lesson 3!

Lesson 4 -- Travel English

Marlene's
Postcard

A1+ -- 60 minutes -- Alumni by Better

Chapter 1: The Dream

Describing Your World

"Think of a beautiful place you visited. What was it like?"
"What is your favorite city? Why?"
"Describe Rome in one word."
Chapter 1: The Dream

Today's Goal

"By the end of this lesson, you will be able to describe places and people using adjectives, and use 'there is' and 'there are'."

Chapter 2

Packing Words

Your vocabulary for today

Chapter 2: Packing Words

Key Vocabulary

Very pretty, pleasant to look at
Beautiful
bonito, lindo
"Rome is a beautiful city."
Known by many people around the world
Famous
famoso
"The Colosseum is very famous."
Full of people, not much space
Crowded
lotado
"The streets are very crowded."
Not noisy, peaceful and calm
Quiet
tranquilo, silencioso
"The parks are quiet and peaceful."
0 / 4 words revealed
Chapter 2: Packing Words

More Words

New, recent, with new technology
Modern
moderno
"The hotel is very modern."
Very, very old, from thousands of years ago
Ancient
antigo, milenar
"The buildings in Rome are ancient."
Nice and kind to other people
Friendly
amigável, simpático
"The people in Rome are very friendly."
Tastes very, very good
Delicious
delicioso
"The pasta in Rome is delicious."
0 / 4 words revealed
Chapter 2: Packing Words

Repeat After Me

Chapter 2: Packing Words

Complete with the Right Word

Rome is a ___beautiful city.
The streets are very ___crowded in the summer.
The Colosseum is very ___famous around the world.
The pasta in Italy is ___delicious!
Chapter 3

The Code

Discover the grammar pattern

Chapter 3: The Code

Look at the Pattern

"Rome is beautiful."
"The streets are crowded."
"There is a park near the hotel."
"There are many restaurants."

"What patterns do you see?"

Chapter 3: The Code

The Rule: Adjectives & There is/are

Subject + is/are + Adjective
SubjectVerbExample
RomeisRome is beautiful.
The hotelisThe hotel is quiet.
The streetsareThe streets are crowded.
The peopleareThe people are friendly.
There is / There are
StructureUseExample
There isSingularThere is a park near the hotel.
There arePluralThere are many restaurants.
Is there...?Question (singular)Is there a pharmacy?
Are there...?Question (plural)Are there any cafes?
Chapter 3: The Code

Common Mistake

"Have a park near here."
"There is a park near here."
"The city is beauty."
"The city is beautiful."
Chapter 3: The Code

Fill in the Blank

Rome ___is beautiful.
There ___are many restaurants.
The hotel ___is quiet and ___modern.
___Is there a pharmacy near here?
Chapter 4

Getting There

Words in context

Chapter 4: Getting There

At the Cafe

M
"Excuse me, is there a supermarket near here?"
A
"Yes, there is one on the next street. It is very big."
M
"Are there any good cafes around here?"
A
"There are three cafes on this street. The one on the corner is very popular."
M
"What is the city like?"
A
"It is beautiful and very old. The buildings are ancient."
M
"Is it crowded?"
A
"Yes, the center is crowded, but the parks are quiet."
Chapter 4: Getting There

Did You Understand?

Is there a supermarket nearby?
Yes, there is one on the next street. It is very big.
How many cafes are on the street?
There are three cafes on the street.
Are the parks crowded or quiet?
The parks are quiet. The center is crowded.
Chapter 4: Getting There

City Description

Listen carefully. What adjectives do you hear?

0:000:00
Speed
What adjectives describe the city?
Beautiful, ancient, and crowded.
What is near the hotel?
There is a park and there are many restaurants.
Chapter 4: Getting There

Marlene's Postcard

POSTCARD

Dear Family,

Rome is beautiful! The people are very friendly.

The buildings are ancient and famous.

There is a beautiful park near my hotel. The food is delicious!

The center is crowded, but the parks are quiet.

My hotel is modern and comfortable.

With love, Marlene

To: Landucci Family, São Paulo, Brazil
Chapter 4: Getting There

Hotel Review

Listen to someone describing their hotel.

0:000:00
Speed
Is the hotel modern or ancient?
The hotel is modern.
Is the hotel quiet?
Yes, the hotel is very quiet and comfortable.
Chapter 4: Getting There

Spot the Error

"Have a park near the hotel."

"There is a park near the hotel."

"The city is beauty."

"The city is beautiful."

"There is many restaurants."

"There are many restaurants."

"The hotel is very quiet and comfort."

"The hotel is very quiet and comfortable."

0 / 4 errors found
Chapter 4: Getting There

Describe the Place

A big city
crowded, modern, famous
A beach
beautiful, quiet, crowded
A mountain
beautiful, quiet, ancient
A hotel
modern, quiet, beautiful
Chapter 4: Getting There

Your Description

"Describe Rome in 3 sentences. Use adjectives and 'there is / there are'."

1. Rome is...
2. There is / There are...
3. The people / The food...
Chapter 5

Practice

Time to use what you learned

Chapter 5: Practice

Quick Challenge

0 / 6
Question 1 of 6
You arrive at a hotel. Describe the hotel to a friend using adjectives.
"The hotel is modern and quiet. It is very beautiful."

Choose the Best Adjective

A museum with many visitors: ___crowded
A building from 2,000 years ago: ___ancient
People who smile and help you: ___friendly
The best pasta you ever had: ___delicious

There is or There are?

___There are many tourists in the center.
___Is there a park near here?

Describe This

A European City

About You

"Is there a park near your house?"
"Are there any good restaurants near you?"
"Describe your favorite restaurant. Is the food delicious?"

Your Turn

Hotel Reception -- Checking In

quiet comfortable modern

Cafe -- Meeting a Traveler

big modern friendly crowded

Dream Destination

Describe Your City

Chapter 7

Wrap-Up

What we accomplished today

Chapter 7: Wrap-Up

Let's Review

Teacher: Type the errors you noticed during class.

Self-Assessment

Lesson 4 Checklist

I can describe a city using adjectives.
I can use "there is" and "there are".
I can ask "Is there...?" and "Are there...?"
I can describe people as friendly, tall, or young.
I can describe hotels and restaurants.
I can describe my neighborhood.

Badge Earned: City Explorer

Graduation

Chapter 1: The Dream

Block 1 Review

"What 3 things can you now say in English that you could not say before?"
"Tell me about Rome -- use adjectives!"
"What time do you wake up?"
Chapter 1: The Dream

Today's Goal

"Today we review EVERYTHING from Block 1. By the end, you will prove you can introduce yourself, describe your routine, use numbers, and describe places."

Chapter 2

Packing Words

Vocabulary review from all lessons

Chapter 2: Packing Words

Do You Remember?

"Let me introduce myself. I am Marlene."
Introduce
apresentar
Lesson 1: Introductions
"I have a morning routine. I do the same things every day."
Routine
rotina
Lesson 2: Daily Routines
"My flight is at half past two."
Flight
voo

"Rome is beautiful and ancient."
Beautiful
bonito/a

0 / 4 words revealed
Chapter 2: Packing Words

More Words

"I manage properties in São Paulo."
Manage
gerenciar
Lesson 1: Introductions
"My schedule is very busy."
Schedule
agenda
Lesson 2: Daily Routines
"Please go to Gate B7 for boarding."
Gate
portão
Lesson 3: Numbers & Time
