Friendly
amigável
Lesson 4: Adjectives
0 / 4 words revealed
Chapter 2: Packing Words

Repeat After Me

Chapter 2: Packing Words

Complete with the Right Word

Let me ___introduce myself. I am Marlene.
I ___usually wake up at six in the morning.
My ___flight is at half past two.
Rome is ___beautiful and the people are very friendly.
Chapter 3

The Code

All grammar patterns reviewed

Chapter 3: The Code

Four Patterns

"I am Marlene." (to be)
"I usually wake up at six." (present simple + adverb)
"My flight is at half past two." (time)
"Rome is beautiful. There are many restaurants." (adjective + there is/are)

"Can you identify all 4 grammar patterns?"

Chapter 3: The Code

Block 1 Grammar

PatternStructureExample
To beI am / She is / They are"I am from Brazil."
Present SimpleI + verb / She + verb+s"I wake up at six."
Adverbs of FrequencyAdverb + verb"I usually have coffee."
Time Expressionsat + time / half past / quarter to"My flight is at half past two."
AdjectivesSubject + be + adjective"Rome is beautiful."
There is / There areThere is + singular / There are + plural"There are many restaurants."
Chapter 3: The Code

Common Mistakes

"I is from Brazil."

"I am from Brazil."

"She wake up at six every day."

"She wakes up at six every day."

"My flight is in three o'clock."

"My flight is at three o'clock."

"There is many restaurants."

"There are many restaurants."

0 / 4 errors found
Chapter 3: The Code

Complete with the Right Word

I ___am currently learning English.
She always ___has breakfast at seven.
The flight is at a ___quarter to three.
___There are many beautiful places in Rome.
Chapter 4

Getting There
Speed Dating

Meet new travelers, use all your skills

Chapter 4: Getting There

Meeting David

D
"Hello! I am David. I am from London. Are you traveling alone?"
M
"Hi David! I am Marlene. I am from Brazil. Yes, this is my first trip alone!"
D
"How wonderful! What do you do in Brazil?"
M
"I manage properties. I usually wake up at six and check my schedule."
D
"That sounds busy! How is Rome so far?"
M
"Rome is beautiful and ancient! There are many amazing restaurants."
D
"I agree! What time is your flight back?"
M
"My flight is at half past two on Sunday. Gate B7!"
Chapter 4: Getting There

Did You Understand?

Where is David from?
David is from London.
What did David ask about Rome?
He asked "How is Rome so far?"
What did David ask about Marlene's flight?
He asked "What time is your flight back?"
Chapter 4: Getting There

Listen Carefully

Someone describes their daily routine and city.

0:000:00
Speed
What time does the person wake up?
Listen for the time in the audio.
How do they describe their city?
Listen for the adjectives used.
Chapter 4: Getting There

Traveler Profile

TRAVELER PROFILE CARD

Alumni Travel Program
MARLENE LANDUCCI
São Paulo, Brazil
Property Manager
Wakes up at 6 AM, has coffee, checks properties
Rome, Italy
Independent, organized, adventurous
Chapter 4: Getting There

Hotel & City Review

Listen to a review of a hotel and city.

0:000:00
Speed
What adjectives are used to describe the hotel?
Listen for descriptive words.
Is there a restaurant at the hotel?
Listen for "there is" or "there are".
Chapter 4: Getting There

Spot the Error

"He are from London."

"He is from London."

"I drink always coffee in the morning."

"I always drink coffee in the morning."

"How many is a cappuccino?"

"How much is a cappuccino?"

"Rome is a city beauty."

"Rome is a beautiful city."

0 / 4 errors found
Chapter 4: Getting There

Numbers & Time

2:30 PM ___Half past two
$4.50 ___Four dollars and fifty cents
2:45 PM ___A quarter to three
$12.75 ___Twelve dollars and seventy-five cents
Chapter 4: Getting There

One-Minute Challenge

"Tell me everything about yourself in 1 minute: name, job, routine, your city, and your travel plans."

name job routine city travel
Chapter 5

Practice

Mix and master all skills

Chapter 5: Practice

Quick Challenge

0 / 6
Question 1 of 6
Introduce yourself to a stranger at the airport.
"Hi, I am Marlene. I am from Brazil. I manage properties."
Chapter 5: Practice

Mix Your Skills

1. Describe your hotel and say what time you have breakfast.
"My hotel is quiet and comfortable. I usually have breakfast at seven."
2. Say 3 adjectives about Rome and ask about restaurants.
"Rome is beautiful, ancient, and crowded. Are there any good restaurants nearby?"
3. Tell someone your flight time and gate number.
"My flight is at half past two. Please go to Gate B7."
4. Describe your morning routine using 2 adverbs.
"I always wake up at six. I usually check my properties before lunch."
Chapter 5: Practice

Complete the Conversation

David: "What is your name?"
Marlene: "___"My name is Marlene. I am from Brazil.
David: "What do you do every morning?"
Marlene: "___"I usually wake up at six. I always have coffee.
David: "How is Rome?"
Marlene: "___"Rome is beautiful! There are many restaurants.
David: "What time is your flight?"
Marlene: "___"My flight is at half past two.
Chapter 5: Practice

Describe & Compare

Describe São Paulo using 3 adjectives and "there is/there are".
"São Paulo is big, busy, and modern. There are many tall buildings. There is a lot of traffic."
Describe Rome using 3 adjectives and "there is/there are".
"Rome is beautiful, ancient, and romantic. There are many old churches. There is amazing food."
Chapter 5: Practice

Rapid Fire

1. Say your name and nationality.
"I am Marlene. I am Brazilian."
2. What do you do? (profession)
"I manage properties in São Paulo."
3. What time do you wake up?
"I usually wake up at six."
4. How much is a cappuccino? ($4.50)
"It is four dollars and fifty cents."
5. Describe Rome in one sentence.
"Rome is beautiful and there are many amazing restaurants."
6. Ask if there is a pharmacy nearby.
"Is there a pharmacy near here?"
Chapter 6

Your Turn

From guided to free

Chapter 6: Your Turn

Role-Play: Guided

Hotel Lobby -- Speed Dating

You meet a traveler at the hotel lobby. Introduce yourself and tell them about your life using the keywords below.

Marlene Brazil properties tennis
Chapter 6: Your Turn

Role-Play: Semi-Free

Café in Rome -- Your Day

You are at a café in Rome. A tourist asks about your day and the city. Describe your routine and Rome.

six AM always Rome beautiful there are
Chapter 6: Your Turn

Role-Play: Free

Hotel -- Full Conversation

You meet someone at the hotel. Have a full conversation: introduce yourself, ask about them, describe your routine, talk about Rome, and mention your flight. Use ALL Block 1 skills.

Chapter 6: Your Turn

Record Your Speech

Record a 1-minute speech about your trip to Rome. Include: introduction, routine, numbers, and description of the city.
Chapter 7

Wrap-Up

Block 1 complete

Chapter 7: Wrap-Up

Let's Review

Teacher: Type the errors you noticed during class.

Self-Assessment

What I Learned

I can introduce myself confidently.
I can describe my daily routine.
I can tell time and say prices.
I can describe cities using adjectives.
I can use "there is" and "there are".
I can have a conversation with a stranger.

Badge Earned: Block 1 Champion

You completed Block 1!

Next: Block 2 -- Getting Ready to Travel

Lesson Complete

Block 1 -- Complete!

Next: Block 2 -- Getting Ready to Travel

Lesson 6 -- Travel English

Marlene's First
Check-In

A1+ -- 60 minutes -- Alumni by Better

Chapter 1: The Dream

Before We Begin

"Tell me about yourself in 3 sentences."

"What time do you usually wake up?"

"Describe Rome in 2 adjectives."

Chapter 1: The Dream

Today's Goal

By the end of this lesson, you will be able to:

  • ✓ Check in at an airport counter
  • ✓ Use "Could I...?" and "I would like..."
  • ✓ Read a boarding pass
  • ✓ Ask for things politely
Chapter 2

Packing Words

Airport vocabulary

Chapter 2: Packing Words

New Words

"You need this document to travel to another country."
Passport
passaporte
"Here is my passport."
"Your bags and suitcases for the trip."
Luggage
bagagem
"I have one piece of luggage."
"The process before your flight where you show your passport."
Check-in
check-in
"The check-in counter is over there."
"The paper you need to board the airplane."
Boarding pass
cartão de embarque
"Here is your boarding pass."
0 / 4 words revealed
Chapter 2: Packing Words

More Words

"The seat next to the walking path inside the airplane."
Aisle
corredor
"I would like an aisle seat."
"The seat next to the window where you can see outside."
Window
janela
"Could I have a window seat?"
"A small bag you take inside the airplane with you."
Carry-on
bagagem de mão
"I have one carry-on bag."
"The time when your airplane leaves the airport."
Departure
partida
"The departure time is 10:30."
0 / 4 words revealed
Chapter 2: Packing Words

Repeat After Me

Chapter 2: Packing Words

Fill the Gap

"Here is my ______. I am from Brazil." passport

"I have one piece of ______ and one carry-on." luggage

"Could I have a ______ seat, please?" window

"The ______ time is 10:30 AM." departure

Chapter 3

The Code

Polite requests at the airport

Chapter 3: The Code

Four Patterns

"Could I have a window seat?" (polite request)
"I would like to check in, please." (polite want)
"Could I see your passport?" (polite request)
"I would like an aisle seat." (polite want)

"What do the colored words have in common?"

Chapter 3: The Code

The Rule

StructureUseExample
Could I + verb?Polite question / request"Could I have a window seat?"
Could I + see/have?Asking for something"Could I see your passport?"
I would like + nounPolite want (thing)"I would like an aisle seat."
I would like + to + verbPolite want (action)"I would like to check in."
Chapter 3: The Code

Common Mistakes

"I want a window seat."

"I would like a window seat." or "Could I have a window seat?"

"Could I to check in?"

"Could I check in?" (no "to" after Could I)

"I would like check in."

"I would like to check in." (need "to" after I would like)

Chapter 3: The Code

Your Turn

You want to check in. Say it politely: "I would like to check in, please."

You want a window seat. Ask politely: "Could I have a window seat, please?"

You want to see the departure board. Ask: "Could I see the departure board?"

You want an aisle seat. Say it: "I would like an aisle seat, please."

Chapter 4

Getting There

Marlene at Guarulhos Airport

Chapter 4: Getting There

At the Counter

A
"Good morning! Welcome to Alitalia. Could I see your passport, please?"
M
"Good morning! Here is my passport. I would like to check in for the flight to Rome."
A
"Of course. How many pieces of luggage do you have?"
M
"I have one suitcase and one carry-on bag."
A
"Would you like an aisle seat or a window seat?"
M
"I would like a window seat, please."
A
"Here is your boarding pass. Your departure is at Gate B5 at 10:30."
M
"Thank you so much! Gate B5 at 10:30. Got it!"
Chapter 4: Getting There

Did You Understand?

What did the agent ask to see first?
The agent asked to see her passport.
What question did the agent ask about seats?
He asked: "Would you like an aisle seat or a window seat?"
What gate and time did the agent say?
Gate B5 at 10:30.
Chapter 4: Getting There

Listen Carefully

An announcement at the airport.

0:000:00
Speed
What flight number was announced?
Listen for the flight number in the audio.
What gate was mentioned?
Listen for the gate number.
Chapter 4: Getting There

Your Boarding Pass

BOARDING PASS

Alitalia Airlines
LANDUCCI / MARLENE
GRU -- São Paulo Guarulhos
FCO -- Roma Fiumicino
AZ 671
B5
14A -- Window
10:30 AM
09:50 AM
Chapter 4: Getting There

Airport Help

A traveler needs help at the airport.

0:000:00
Speed
What did the traveler need?
Listen for what she asked for.
Did she use polite language?
Listen for "Could I" or "I would like".
Chapter 4: Getting There

Spot the Error

"Could I to have a window seat?"

"Could I have a window seat?" (no "to")

"I would like check in."

"I would like to check in."

"Here is my boarding card."

"Here is my boarding pass."

"I have two carry-ons luggages."

"I have two carry-on bags." (luggage is uncountable)

0 / 4 errors found
Chapter 4: Getting There

Airport Signs

DEPARTURES

Where planes leave

ARRIVALS

Where planes arrive

CHECK-IN

Where you get your boarding pass

GATE B5

Where you board the plane

BAGGAGE CLAIM

Where you pick up luggage

DUTY FREE

Shop without tax

Chapter 4: Getting There

Your Answers

"Do you prefer an aisle seat or a window seat? Why?"

"How many pieces of luggage do you usually take?"

"What is the first thing you do when you arrive at the airport?"

Chapter 5

Practice

Mix and master airport skills

Chapter 5: Practice

Quick Challenge

0 / 6
Question 1 of 6
You arrive at the check-in counter. Tell the agent you want to check in.
"I would like to check in for the flight to Rome, please."
Chapter 5: Practice

Make It Polite

You want to change your seat. Ask politely: "Could I change my seat, please?"

You want water on the plane. Say politely: "I would like some water, please."

You want to see the menu. Ask: "Could I see the menu, please?"

You want a blanket. Say politely: "I would like a blanket, please."

Chapter 5: Practice

Word Match

"The document you need to travel internationally." Passport

"The paper you need to board the airplane." Boarding pass

"A small bag you take inside the airplane." Carry-on

"The seat next to the walking path in the airplane." Aisle

Chapter 5: Practice

Transform: Rude to Polite

"I want a coffee." "I would like a coffee, please."

"Give me my boarding pass." "Could I have my boarding pass, please?"

"I want to sit by the window." "I would like a window seat, please."

Chapter 5: Practice

About You

"Think about your trip to Rome. What would you like to see there?"

"You are at the airport. What do you check first: the gate or the departure time?"

"What do you always carry in your carry-on bag?"

Chapter 6

Your Turn

From guided to free

Chapter 6: Your Turn

Role-Play: Check-In

Scenario: You are checking in at Guarulhos Airport for your flight to Rome. Your teacher is the agent.

Use these keywords:

window passport one suitcase I would like
Chapter 6: Your Turn

Role-Play: Duty Free

Scenario: You are at the Duty Free shop at the airport. You want to buy a gift. Your teacher is the shop assistant.

Use these keywords:

Could I I would like how much
Chapter 6: Your Turn

Role-Play: Lost Luggage

Scenario: You arrived in Rome, but your luggage is not at the baggage claim! Talk to the airport staff. Use everything you learned today.

No keywords this time. You are on your own!

Chapter 6: Your Turn

Record Your Check-In

Record yourself doing a complete airport check-in. Include: greeting, passport, luggage, seat preference, and thank you.
Chapter 7

Wrap-Up

Lesson 6 complete

Chapter 7: Wrap-Up

Let's Review

Teacher: Type the errors you noticed during class.

Self-Assessment

What I Learned

I can check in at an airport.
I can use "Could I...?" to ask politely.
I can use "I would like..." to say what I want.
I know airport vocabulary: passport, luggage, boarding pass.
I can read a boarding pass.
I can ask for things politely on the airplane.

Badge Earned: Airport Pro

You mastered airport check-in!

Next: More travel adventures await!

Lesson Complete

Lesson 6 -- Complete!

Next: Navigating the Airport

Lesson 7

Marlene's First Flight

On the Plane

Chapter 1: The Dream

Remember Last Time?

"What seat did you choose at check-in: window or aisle?"

"What polite phrase did you use? Could I... or I would like..."

"Now you are sitting on the plane. What do you see around you?"

Chapter 1: The Dream

Today You Will Learn

Ask for things on the plane using Can I have...?

Make polite requests with Could you...?

Vocabulary for airplane travel: seatbelt, tray table, turbulence, and more

Understand safety announcements and meal service

Chapter 2

Packing Words

Vocabulary for the airplane

Chapter 2: Packing Words

New Words

"You buckle this around your waist for safety."
Seatbelt
cinto de segurança
"Please fasten your seatbelt."
"The compartment above your head where you put your bag."
Overhead
compartimento superior
"Put your bag in the overhead compartment."
"The small table that folds down from the seat in front of you."
Tray table
mesa de refeição
"Please fold up your tray table."
"A warm cover you use when you are cold."
Blanket
cobertor
"Can I have a blanket, please?"
0 / 4 words revealed
Chapter 2: Packing Words

More Words

"When the plane shakes because of rough air."
Turbulence
turbulência
"We are experiencing some turbulence."
"When the airplane arrives and touches the ground."
Landing
aterrissagem
"We are preparing for landing."
"A small amount of food you eat between meals."
Snack
lanche
"Can I have a snack, please?"
"You put these on your ears to listen to music or movies."
Headphones
fones de ouvido
"Could you bring me headphones?"
0 / 4 words revealed
Chapter 2: Packing Words

Repeat After Me

Chapter 2: Packing Words

Fill the Gap

"Please fasten your ______ for takeoff." seatbelt

"Put your bag in the ______ compartment." overhead

"We are experiencing some ______. Please stay seated." turbulence

"Can I have a ______, please? I am cold." blanket

Chapter 3

The Code

Polite requests on the plane

Chapter 3: The Code

Look at the Pattern

"Can I have a blanket, please?"

"Can I have some water?"

"Could you bring me headphones?"

"Could you help me with my bag?"

What do the orange words have in common?

Chapter 3: The Code

The Rule

StructureUseExample
Can I have + noun?Ask for a thing"Can I have a blanket?"
Can I have + some + noun?Ask for food/drink"Can I have some water?"
Could you + verb?Ask someone to do something"Could you help me?"
Could you + bring/show?Ask for a service"Could you bring me headphones?"
Chapter 3: The Code

Common Mistakes

"Can I have a water?"

"Can I have some water?"

"Could you to help me?"

"Could you help me?" (no "to")

"Can I have headphones?"

"Could you bring me headphones?" (asking the person)

Chapter 3: The Code

Your Turn

You want a snack. Ask politely: "Can I have a snack, please?"

You want the attendant to bring water. Ask: "Could you bring me some water, please?"

You want headphones. Ask: "Can I have headphones, please?"

You want the attendant to help with your bag. Ask: "Could you help me with my bag, please?"

Chapter 4

Getting There

Marlene on the flight to Rome

Chapter 4: Getting There

On the Plane

FA
"Good afternoon! Welcome aboard. Please put your bag in the overhead compartment and fasten your seatbelt."
M
"Thank you! Can I have a blanket, please? It is cold."
FA
"Of course! Here you go. Would you like headphones for the movie?"
M
"Yes, please! Can I have headphones?"
FA
"Here you are. We will be serving a snack soon. Please fold down your tray table."
M
"Can I have a snack and some juice, please?"
FA
"Attention, passengers. We are experiencing some turbulence. Please fasten your seatbelt."
M
"OK! Could you tell me when we are landing?"
Chapter 4: Getting There

Did You Understand?

What did the flight attendant ask Marlene to do first?
Put her bag in the overhead compartment and fasten her seatbelt.
What did the flight attendant offer?
A blanket and headphones.
Why did the attendant say "fasten your seatbelt" again?
Because there was turbulence.
Chapter 4: Getting There

Listen Carefully

A safety announcement on the plane.

0:000:00
Speed
What should passengers fasten?
Their seatbelts.
Where should bags go?
In the overhead compartment or under the seat.
Chapter 4: Getting There

Safety Card

SAFETY INSTRUCTIONS

Alitalia Airlines -- Flight AZ 671
Fasten your seatbelt
Fold up your tray table
Store bags in the overhead compartment
In case of turbulence, stay seated
Before landing, return seat to upright position
Emergency exits are located at the front and rear
Chapter 4: Getting There

Meal Service

The flight attendant serves food and drinks.

0:000:00
Speed
What food options were offered?
Listen for the meal choices.
What did the passenger ask for?
Listen for "Can I have" or "Could you".
Chapter 4: Getting There

Spot the Error

"Can I have a water?"

"Can I have some water?"

"Could you to bring me a blanket?"

"Could you bring me a blanket?" (no "to")

"Please fasten your belt seat."

"Please fasten your seatbelt."

"Put your bag in the head over compartment."

"Put your bag in the overhead compartment."

0 / 4 errors found
Chapter 4: Getting There

Make a Request

You want orange juice. Ask politely: "Can I have some orange juice, please?"

You want the attendant to show you the exit. Ask: "Could you show me the exit, please?"

You want a pillow. Ask: "Can I have a pillow, please?"

You want the attendant to help with the tray table. Ask: "Could you help me with the tray table?"

Chapter 4: Getting There

Your Answers

"Do you usually sleep on the plane? Do you use a blanket?"

"What do you like to eat and drink on the plane?"

"Are you nervous during turbulence? What do you do?"

Chapter 5

Practice

Mix and master flight skills

Chapter 5: Practice

Quick Challenge

0 / 6
Question 1 of 6
You are on the plane and feeling cold. Ask for something warm.
"Can I have a blanket, please?"
Chapter 5: Practice

Can I Have or Could You?

You want a snack. "Can I have a snack?" (you want a THING)

You want the attendant to open the overhead bin. "Could you open the overhead bin?" (you want an ACTION)

You want some tea. "Can I have some tea?" (you want a THING)

You want the attendant to tell you the landing time. "Could you tell me the landing time?" (you want an ACTION)

Chapter 5: Practice

Word Match

"When the plane shakes because of rough air." Turbulence

"You buckle this around your waist for safety." Seatbelt

"The small table that folds down in front of you." Tray table

"You put these on your ears to listen to movies." Headphones

Chapter 5: Practice

Transform: Direct to Polite

"I want water." "Can I have some water, please?"

"Bring me headphones." "Could you bring me headphones, please?"

"I want a blanket." "Can I have a blanket, please?"

Chapter 5: Practice

About You

"What is the first thing you ask for when you sit on the plane?"

"Do you watch movies on the plane? What kind?"

"Imagine you are landing in Rome. How do you feel?"

Chapter 6

Your Turn

From guided to free

Chapter 6: Your Turn

Role-Play: Ask the Attendant

Scenario: You are on the plane. Your teacher is the flight attendant. Ask for three things.

Use these keywords:

blanket water headphones
Chapter 6: Your Turn

Role-Play: Meal Time

Scenario: The meal cart is here. Order your food and drink. Ask about your options. Your teacher is the attendant.

Use these keywords:

Can I have Could you please
Chapter 6: Your Turn

Role-Play: Turbulence!

Scenario: The plane is experiencing turbulence! You are worried. Talk to the flight attendant. Ask questions, ask for help. Use everything you learned today.

No keywords this time. You are on your own!

Chapter 6: Your Turn

Record Your Requests

Record yourself making 3 polite requests on the plane. Include: a blanket, water, and headphones. Use "Can I have" and "Could you".
Chapter 7

Wrap-Up

Lesson 7 complete

Chapter 7: Wrap-Up

Let's Review

Teacher: Type the errors you noticed during class.

Self-Assessment

What I Learned

I can ask for things using "Can I have...?"
I can make requests using "Could you...?"
I know airplane vocabulary: seatbelt, overhead, tray table.
I can understand a safety announcement.
I can order food and drink on the plane.
I know the difference between "Can I have" and "Could you".

Badge Earned: Sky Traveler

You mastered flying in English!

Next: More travel adventures await!

Lesson Complete

Lesson 7 -- Complete!

Next: Arriving in Rome

Lesson 8

Marlene Arrives in Rome

Immigration and Customs

Chapter 1: The Dream

The Plane Has Landed!

Last lesson: You learned to ask for things on the plane.

Tell your teacher: What two phrases did you learn?

Now the plane has landed in Rome. What happens next?

Chapter 1: The Dream

Today's Goal

Answer immigration questions

Go through customs with confidence

Ask and answer questions with do and does

Chapter 2

Packing Words

Vocabulary for immigration

Chapter 2: Packing Words

New Words

"The area at the airport where they check your bags."
Customs
alfândega
"You must go through customs when you arrive."
"A form where you say what you are bringing into the country."
Declaration
declaração
"Do you have anything to declare?"
"The reason why you are traveling."
Purpose
propósito / motivo
"What is the purpose of your visit?"
"A trip you take to relax and have fun."
Vacation
férias
"I am here on vacation."
0 / 4 words revealed
Chapter 2: Packing Words

More Words

"To officially say what you are bringing into a country."
Declare
declarar
"Do you have anything to declare?"
"The mark the officer puts in your passport."
Stamp
carimbo / carimbar
"The officer stamped my passport."
"To go back to your home country."
Return
retornar / voltar
"When do you return to Brazil?"
"A person who officially belongs to a country."
Citizen
cidadão
"I am a Brazilian citizen."
0 / 4 words revealed
Chapter 2: Packing Words

Say It Right

Customs -- /KUS-tumz/

Declaration -- /deh-kluh-RAY-shun/

Purpose -- /PER-pus/

Citizen -- /SIT-ih-zen/

Chapter 2: Packing Words

Fill the Gap

"What is the ______ of your visit?" purpose

"Do you have anything to ______?" declare

"The officer ______ my passport." stamped

"I am a Brazilian ______." citizen

Chapter 3

The Code

Questions with do and does

Chapter 3: The Code

Look at the Pattern

"Do you have a return ticket?"

"Do you have anything to declare?"

"Does she speak English?"

"Does your passport have a visa?"

What do the orange words have in common?

Chapter 3: The Code

The Rule

StructureUseExample
Do + I/you/we/they + verb?Questions with I/you/we/they"Do you have a visa?"
Does + he/she/it + verb?Questions with he/she/it"Does she live in Brazil?"
Yes, I/you/we/they + do.Short positive answer"Yes, I do."
No, he/she/it + does not.Short negative answer"No, she does not."
Chapter 3: The Code

Common Mistakes

"Does she has a visa?"

"Does she have a visa?" (after does, base verb)

"Do he speak English?"

"Does he speak English?" (he = does)

"Do you has a return ticket?"

"Do you have a return ticket?" (do + base verb)

Chapter 3: The Code

Practice: Do or Does?

"______ you have a return ticket?" Do

"______ she live in Rome?" Does

"______ they speak Italian?" Do

"______ your passport have a visa stamp?" Does

Chapter 4

Getting There

At immigration in Rome

Chapter 4: Getting There

At Immigration

IO
"Good afternoon. Passport, please. What is the purpose of your visit?"
M
"I am here on vacation."
IO
"How long do you plan to stay?"
M
"Ten days. I return on the twenty-fifth."
IO
"Do you have a hotel reservation?"
M
"Yes, I do. Here is my reservation."
IO
"Do you have anything to declare at customs?"
M
"No, I do not. Just personal items. Thank you!"
Chapter 4: Getting There

Did You Understand?

What was the first thing the officer asked for?
Her passport and the purpose of her visit.
What did the officer ask about customs?
If she had anything to declare.
How long is Marlene staying in Rome?
Ten days.
Chapter 4: Getting There

Listen Carefully

A traveler at the immigration counter.

0:000:00
Speed
What did the officer ask about?
The purpose of the visit and how long the traveler is staying.
Did the traveler have anything to declare?
Listen for "declare" and the answer.
Chapter 4: Getting There

Passport Stamp

IMMIGRATION STAMP

Fiumicino Airport -- Rome, Italy
LANDUCCI, Marlene
Brazilian citizen
Vacation
10 days
June 25, 2026
Nothing to declare
Chapter 4: Getting There

Customs Check

The customs officer checks your bags.

0:000:00
Speed
What did the customs officer ask?
Listen for questions about bags and declarations.
Did the traveler open their bag?
Listen for "open" and the response.
Chapter 4: Getting There

Spot the Error

"Does you have a visa?"

"Do you have a visa?"

"Do she speak English?"

"Does she speak English?"

"Does he has a return ticket?"

"Does he have a return ticket?"

"Do your passport have a stamp?"

"Does your passport have a stamp?"

0 / 4 errors found
Chapter 4: Getting There

Form the Question

Ask about someone's visa (she). "Does she have a visa?"

Ask about someone's plans (you). "Do you plan to stay long?"

Ask if someone has a return ticket (he). "Does he have a return ticket?"

Ask if travelers have anything to declare (they). "Do they have anything to declare?"

Chapter 4: Getting There

Your Turn: Ask Me!

Now you are the immigration officer!

Ask your teacher 3 questions using do or does.

Ideas: purpose, return, hotel, declare, visa

Chapter 5

Practice

Mix and master immigration skills

Chapter 5: Practice

Quick Challenge

0 / 6
Question 1 of 6
The officer asks: "What is the purpose of your visit?" You are on vacation.
"I am here on vacation."
Chapter 5: Practice

Do or Does?

Ask if I have a reservation. "Do you have a reservation?" (you = do)

Ask if the passport has a stamp (it). "Does it have a stamp?" (it = does)

Ask if we need a visa (we). "Do we need a visa?" (we = do)

Ask if the officer speaks Portuguese (he). "Does he speak Portuguese?" (he = does)

Chapter 5: Practice

Word Match

"The area where they check what you bring into the country." Customs

"The reason you are visiting a country." Purpose

"The mark an officer puts in your passport." Stamp

"To go back to your country after a trip." Return

Chapter 5: Practice

Transform: Statement to Question

"You have a return ticket." "Do you have a return ticket?"

"She speaks Italian." "Does she speak Italian?"

"They have something to declare." "Do they have anything to declare?"

Chapter 5: Practice

About You

"Do you feel nervous at immigration? Why?"

"What do you usually say when they ask the purpose of your visit?"

"Do you ever bring food from Brazil when you travel?"

Chapter 6

Your Turn

From guided to free

Chapter 6: Your Turn

Role-Play: At Immigration

Scenario: You just arrived in Rome. Your teacher is the immigration officer. Answer the questions.

Use these keywords:

vacation ten days return
Chapter 6: Your Turn

Role-Play: At Customs

Scenario: You are at customs now. The officer asks about your bags. Answer using do/does. Your teacher is the customs officer.

Use these keywords:

declare personal items No, I do not.
Chapter 6: Your Turn

Role-Play: Extra Questions!

Scenario: The immigration officer has extra questions! Answer everything using what you learned today.

No keywords this time. You are on your own!

Chapter 6: Your Turn

Record Your Answers

Record yourself answering 3 immigration questions: "What is the purpose of your visit?", "How long do you stay?", and "Do you have anything to declare?"
Chapter 7

Wrap-Up

Lesson 8 complete

Chapter 7: Wrap-Up

Let's Review

Teacher: Type the errors you noticed during class.

Self-Assessment

What I Learned

I can answer immigration questions in English.
I know the difference between "do" and "does" in questions.
I can say the purpose of my visit and my return date.
I know vocabulary for customs: declare, stamp, citizen.
I can go through customs and immigration with confidence.

Badge Earned: Immigration Expert

You can go through immigration in English!

Next: More adventures in Rome!

Lesson Complete

Lesson 8 -- Complete!

Next: Exploring Rome

Lesson 9

Marlene's Missing Suitcase

Baggage and Ground Transport

Chapter 1: The Dream

Through Immigration!

Last lesson: You went through immigration in Rome.

Tell your teacher: What two questions did the officer ask?

Now you are through immigration. Time to get your bags! But where is your suitcase?

Chapter 1: The Dream

Today's Goal

Report a lost suitcase at baggage claim

Use possessives: my, your, his, her

Ask Where is...? and take a taxi

Chapter 2

Packing Words

Vocabulary for baggage and transport

Chapter 2: Packing Words

New Words

"A big bag with a handle and wheels for traveling."
Suitcase
mala
"My suitcase is black and large."
"The area at the airport where you pick up your bags."
Baggage claim
esteira de bagagens
"Where is the baggage claim?"
"A car with a driver that takes you where you want to go."
Taxi
táxi
"I need a taxi to the hotel."
"The person who drives the car."
Driver
motorista
"The driver knows the way to the hotel."
0 / 4 words revealed
Chapter 2: Packing Words

More Words

"The street name and number where you are going."
Address
endereço
"What is the address of your hotel?"
"When something is not where it should be."
Lost
perdido(a)
"My suitcase is lost."
"A paper that shows you paid for something."
Receipt
recibo
"Can I have a receipt, please?"
"The machine in a taxi that counts the price."
Meter
taxímetro
"Please turn on the meter."
0 / 4 words revealed
Chapter 2: Packing Words

Say It Right

Suitcase SOOT-kays

Baggage claim BAG-ij KLAYM

Receipt rih-SEET (P is silent!)

Address uh-DRESS

Chapter 2: Packing Words

Complete the Sentence

"I cannot find my ___. It is black and large." suitcase

"Where is the ___? I need to pick up my bags." baggage claim

"The ___ says the hotel is 20 minutes away." driver

"Can I have a ___, please? I need it for my company." receipt

Chapter 3

The Code

Possessives and Where is...?

Chapter 3: The Code

Look at These

"My suitcase is lost."

"What is your address?"

"His taxi is here."

"Her driver knows the way."

What do the orange words have in common?

Chapter 3: The Code

The Rule

SubjectPossessiveExample
ImyMy suitcase is lost.
youyourWhat is your address?
hehisHis taxi is here.
sheherHer driver knows the way.
weourOur hotel is downtown.
theytheirTheir suitcases are on the belt.

Where is...? = Use to find things and places.

"Where is the baggage claim?" / "Where is my suitcase?"

Chapter 3: The Code

Common Mistakes

"Where is you suitcase?"

"Where is your suitcase?"

"She taxi is outside."

"Her taxi is outside."

"Where is the receipt?" (asking about YOUR receipt)

"Where is my receipt?"

Chapter 3: The Code

Fill the Gap

"I cannot find ___ suitcase." (I) my

"Where is ___ hotel?" (we) our

"___ driver is waiting outside." (he) His

"Is this ___ receipt?" (you) your

Chapter 4

Getting There

At baggage claim in Rome

Chapter 4: Getting There

At Baggage Claim

M
"Excuse me. My suitcase is not on the belt. I think it is lost."
AS
"I am sorry to hear that. Can I see your boarding pass?"
M
"Yes, here it is. My name is Marlene Landucci."
AS
"What does your suitcase look like?"
M
"It is black and large. It has my name on the tag."
AS
"What is the address of your hotel? We will deliver it."
M
"My hotel is Hotel Roma. The address is Via Veneto 42."
AS
"Here is your claim ticket. We will call when we find your suitcase."
Chapter 4: Getting There

Did You Understand?

What did the staff ask for first?
Marlene's boarding pass.
What did the staff ask about the suitcase?
What does it look like.
What will the airline do with the suitcase?
Deliver it to her hotel.
Chapter 4: Getting There

Listen Carefully

A traveler reports a lost bag at baggage claim.

0:000:00
Speed
What information did the staff ask for?
The boarding pass, bag description, and hotel address.
What possessives did you hear?
Listen for "my," "your," and "our."
Chapter 4: Getting There

Claim Ticket

LUGGAGE CLAIM TICKET

Fiumicino Airport -- Rome, Italy
LANDUCCI, Marlene
AZ 680 -- GRU to FCO
Black, large, name tag
Lost -- searching
Hotel Roma, Via Veneto 42
CLM-2026-0615
Chapter 4: Getting There

Taxi Ride

Marlene takes a taxi to the hotel.

0:000:00
Speed
What address did Marlene give the driver?
Hotel Roma, Via Veneto 42.
Did Marlene ask for a receipt?
Listen carefully for "receipt."
Chapter 4: Getting There

Spot the Error

"Where is me suitcase?"

"Where is my suitcase?"

"Him driver is outside."

"His driver is outside."

"What is you address?"

"What is your address?"

"She taxi is here."

"Her taxi is here."

0 / 4 errors found
Chapter 4: Getting There

Transform: Possessives

(I) "___ suitcase is lost." My

(she) "Where is ___ taxi?" her

(they) "___ bags are on the belt." Their

(he) "___ receipt is in the bag." His

Chapter 4: Getting There

Your Turn: Ask Me!

Now you ask the questions!

Ask your teacher 3 questions using possessives and Where is.

Ideas: suitcase, address, taxi, hotel, receipt

Chapter 5

More Practice

Use what you learned

Chapter 5: Practice

Quick Challenge

0 / 6
Question 1 of 6
You are at baggage claim and your suitcase is not there. Tell the staff.
"Excuse me. My suitcase is lost."
Chapter 5: Practice

Where Is ...?

You need to pick up your bags. "Where is the baggage claim?"

You need a taxi outside the airport. "Where is the taxi stand?"

You want to find your hotel. "Where is my hotel?"

You lost your receipt. "Where is my receipt?"

Chapter 5: Practice

Word Match

"The machine in the taxi that counts the price." Meter

"A paper that shows you paid." Receipt

"The area where you pick up your bags." Baggage claim

"The person who drives the car." Driver

Chapter 5: Practice

In the Taxi

Your teacher is the taxi driver. Practice:

1. Tell the driver the address of your hotel.

2. Ask the driver to turn on the meter.

3. Ask for a receipt at the end.

Chapter 5: Practice

About You

"What color is your suitcase?"

"Do you prefer taxi or bus from the airport?"

"Have you ever lost your luggage? What did you do?"

Chapter 6

Your Turn

From guided to free

Chapter 6: Your Turn

Role-Play: Lost Luggage

Scenario: Your suitcase is not on the belt. Your teacher is the airline staff. Report the problem and give the information they need.

Use these keywords:

my suitcase lost address
Chapter 6: Your Turn

Role-Play: Taxi Ride

Scenario: You are in a taxi in Rome. Your teacher is the driver. Give your address, ask about the meter, and request a receipt.

Use these keywords:

Hotel Roma meter receipt
Chapter 6: Your Turn

Role-Play: Hotel Arrival

Scenario: You arrive at Hotel Roma. Your suitcase is not here yet. Talk to the receptionist. Explain the situation, give your name, and ask for help. Use everything you learned today.

No keywords this time. You are on your own!

Chapter 6: Your Turn

Record Yourself

Describe your suitcase and give your hotel address to the airline.

Chapter 7

Wrap Up

Let us celebrate!

Chapter 7: Wrap Up

Error Correction

Errors from today's lesson:

What I Learned

Lesson 9 Checklist

I can report a lost suitcase in English.
I know possessives: my, your, his, her, our, their.
I can ask "Where is...?" to find things and places.
I know vocabulary for transport: taxi, driver, meter, receipt.
I can take a taxi and give my hotel address.

Badge Earned: Ground Transport Pro

You can handle luggage and taxis in English!

Next: More adventures in Rome!

Lesson Complete

Lesson 9 -- Complete!

Next: Exploring Rome

Lesson 10

Block 2 Review

Full Airport Simulation

Chapter 1: The Dream

Block 2 Recap

Block 2: You traveled from Brazil to Rome!

Tell your teacher: What three things can you do in English at the airport now?

Today: a FULL simulation from check-in to hotel!

Chapter 1: The Dream

Today's Goal

Review vocabulary from Lessons 6-9

Review grammar: Could I, I would like, Can I have, possessives

Complete a full airport simulation

Chapter 2

Packing Words

Block 2 Vocabulary Review

Chapter 2: Packing Words

Airport & Plane

"The place where you go to get on a plane."
Gate
portão de embarque
"My gate is number fourteen."
"The paper or digital pass that lets you on the plane."
Boarding pass
cartão de embarque
"Here is my boarding pass."
"The person who serves food and drinks on the plane."
Flight attendant
comissário(a) de bordo
"The flight attendant is very helpful."
"A big bag with a handle and wheels for traveling."
Suitcase
mala
"My suitcase is black and large."
0 / 4 words revealed
Chapter 2: Packing Words

Immigration & Transport

"The official document you need to travel to another country."
Passport
passaporte
"Here is my passport."
"The reason you are visiting a country."
Purpose
objetivo, propósito
"The purpose of my visit is tourism."
"When something is not where it should be."
Lost
perdido(a)
"My suitcase is lost."
"A car with a driver that takes you where you want to go."
Taxi
táxi
"I need a taxi to the hotel."
0 / 4 words revealed
Chapter 2: Packing Words

Say It Right

Boarding pass BOR-ding PAS

Receipt rih-SEET (P is silent!)

Purpose PER-pus

Flight attendant FLAIT uh-TEN-dunt

Chapter 2: Packing Words

Complete the Sentence

"Could I have a window ___, please?" seat

"The ___ of my visit is tourism." purpose

"Where is the ___? I need to pick up my bags." baggage claim

"Please turn on the ___." meter

Chapter 3

The Code

Grammar Review: Block 2

Chapter 3: The Code

Block 2 Grammar

StructureUseExample
Could I...?Polite requestCould I have a window seat?
I would like...Polite wantI would like to check in.
Can I have...?Asking for somethingCan I have some water?
My/Your/His/HerPossessivesMy suitcase is lost.
Where is...?Finding things/placesWhere is the baggage claim?
Do you have...?Yes/No questionsDo you have anything to declare?
Chapter 3: The Code

Which Structure?

You want to check in politely. "I would like to check in, please."

You want water on the plane. "Can I have some water, please?"

Your bag is missing at the airport. "My suitcase is lost."

You need to find the taxi area. "Where is the taxi stand?"

Chapter 3: The Code

Common Mistakes

"I can have a window seat?"

"Could I have a window seat?"

"Where is you passport?"

"Where is your passport?"

"I want a blanket." (too direct)

"Could I have a blanket, please?"

Chapter 3: The Code

Fill the Gap

"___ see your passport, please?" (polite request) Could I

"___ suitcase is black and large." (I) My

"___ is the baggage claim?" (finding a place) Where

"___ have some water, please?" (on the plane) Can I

Chapter 4

Getting There

Full Airport Simulation

Chapter 4: Getting There

At Check-in

M
"Hello. I would like to check in, please."
A
"Good morning! May I see your passport and booking confirmation?"
M
"Yes, here they are. Could I have a window seat, please?"
A
"Of course. You are in seat 12A. How many bags are you checking in?"
M
"One suitcase. It is black and large."
A
"Your gate is number 14. Boarding starts at 2:30."
M
"Where is gate fourteen?"
A
"Go straight and turn left after security. Have a good flight!"
Chapter 4: Getting There

Did You Understand?

What seat did Marlene get?
Seat 12A, a window seat.
What gate number was her flight?
Gate 14.
What time does boarding start?
At 2:30.
Chapter 4: Getting There

On the Plane

Marlene talks to the flight attendant.

0:000:00
Speed
What did Marlene ask the flight attendant for?
A blanket and some water.
What polite structures did you hear?
"Could I have" and "Can I have."
Chapter 4: Getting There

Boarding Pass

BOARDING PASS

Aeroporto de Guarulhos -- GRU
LANDUCCI, Marlene
AZ 680 -- GRU to FCO
14
12A -- Window
14:30
15:15
Chapter 4: Getting There

Immigration in Rome

Marlene goes through immigration.

0:000:00
Speed
What questions did the officer ask?
"What is the purpose of your visit?" and "How long are you staying?"
What possessives did you hear?
"Your passport," "your visit," "my visit."
Chapter 4: Getting There

Spot the Error

"I can have a window seat, please?"

"Could I have a window seat, please?"

"Where is me suitcase?"

"Where is my suitcase?"

"The propose of my visit is tourism."

"The purpose of my visit is tourism."

"I want some water." (too direct on the plane)

"Can I have some water, please?"

0 / 4 errors found
Chapter 4: Getting There

Build the Sentence

You want to check in at the airport. "I would like to check in, please."

The officer asks why you are visiting. "The purpose of my visit is tourism."

Your bag is not on the belt. "Excuse me. My suitcase is lost."

You arrive at the hotel by taxi and need a receipt. "Can I have a receipt, please?"

Chapter 4: Getting There

Your Turn: Ask Me!

Now you ask the questions!

Ask your teacher 4 questions using different structures from Block 2.

Use: Could I / I would like / Where is / Do you have

Chapter 5

More Practice

Use everything you learned

Chapter 5: Practice

Quick Challenge

0 / 6
Question 1 of 6
You arrive at the airport. Tell the agent you want to check in.
"I would like to check in, please."
Chapter 5: Practice

Word Match

"The place where you go to get on the plane." Gate

"The machine in the taxi that counts the price." Meter

"The reason you are visiting a country." Purpose

"A paper that shows you paid." Receipt

Chapter 5: Practice

Full Journey

1. Check-in: You want a window seat. "Could I have a window seat, please?"

2. On the plane: You want water. "Can I have some water, please?"

3. Immigration: The officer asks the purpose. "The purpose of my visit is tourism."

4. Baggage: Your bag is missing. "My suitcase is lost."

5. Taxi: Tell the driver the address. "My hotel is Hotel Roma. The address is Via Veneto 42."

6. Arrival: Ask for a receipt. "Can I have a receipt, please?"

Chapter 5: Practice

About You

"Do you always ask for a window seat or an aisle seat?"

"What do you usually ask the flight attendant for?"

"Have you ever lost your luggage? What happened?"

Chapter 5: Practice

Where Is ...?

You need to find 3 places at the airport.

Ask your teacher: "Where is the gate?" / "Where is the baggage claim?" / "Where is the taxi stand?"

Your teacher will answer. Listen and repeat the directions!

Chapter 6

Your Turn

From guided to free

Chapter 6: Your Turn

Role-Play: Check-in & Plane

Scenario: You are at the airport. Your teacher is the agent and then the flight attendant. Check in, ask for a window seat, then ask for water on the plane.

Use these keywords:

I would like window seat Can I have
Chapter 6: Your Turn

Role-Play: Immigration & Baggage

Scenario: You arrive in Rome. Your teacher is the immigration officer and then the baggage claim staff. Answer the officer's questions, then report your lost suitcase.

Use these keywords:

tourism my suitcase Hotel Roma
Chapter 6: Your Turn

Role-Play: Full Simulation

Scenario: FULL airport simulation! From check-in to hotel. Your teacher will play ALL the roles: check-in agent, flight attendant, immigration officer, baggage staff, taxi driver, hotel receptionist. Use everything you learned in Block 2!

No keywords this time. You are on your own!

Chapter 6: Your Turn

Record Yourself

Tell the full story: From check-in to hotel in Rome.

Chapter 7

Wrap Up

Block 2 Complete!

Chapter 7: Wrap Up

Error Correction

Errors from today's simulation:

What I Learned

Lesson 10 Checklist

I can check in at the airport in English.
I can ask for things politely on the plane.
I can answer immigration questions.
I can report lost luggage and take a taxi.
I can use all Block 2 grammar correctly.

Badge Earned: Block 2 Champion

You can travel from Brazil to Italy in English!

Block 2 Complete! Next: Exploring Rome!
